```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/bimtests/TestArchStructure.py
classes:
  class TestArchStructure(TestArchBase.TestArchBase)
    methods:
      - def testStructure(self)
      - def test_makeStructure_explicit_dimensions(self)
        doc: Explicit length/width/height are stored on the object.
      - def test_makeStructure_defaults_from_preferences(self)
        doc: When no dimensions are given, all three are filled from preferences.
      - def test_makeStructure_ifc_type_column(self)
        doc: Height > Length produces IfcType 'Column'.
      - def test_makeStructure_ifc_type_beam(self)
        doc: Length > Height produces IfcType 'Beam'.
      - def test_makeStructure_ifc_type_equal_dimensions(self)
        doc: Length == Height keeps the initial default IfcType ('Beam').
      - def test_makeStructure_custom_label(self)
        doc: A custom name overrides the automatic label.
      - def test_makeStructure_auto_label_column(self)
        doc: Auto-label contains 'Column' when height > length.
      - def test_makeStructure_auto_label_beam(self)
        doc: Auto-label contains 'Beam' when length > height.
      - def test_makeStructuralSystem_basic(self)
        doc: A structural system is created from a structure and an axis.
      - def test_makeStructuralSystem_no_axes_returns_none(self)
        doc: Returns None when no axes are provided.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_base_is_p1(self)
        doc: Placement base is always the first point.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_vertical_column_z_axis(self)
        doc: Vertical column: the placement's local Z axis should point upward.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_horizontal_along_x(self)
        doc: Horizontal beam along X: local X axis points along the beam direction.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_horizontal_along_y(self)
        doc: Horizontal beam along Y: local X axis points along the beam direction.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_horizontal_diagonal_x_axis(self)
        doc: Diagonal horizontal beam: local X axis points along the beam direction.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_orthogonal_frame(self)
        doc: The resulting rotation frame has mutually orthogonal axes.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_diagonal_orthogonal_frame(self)
        doc: Diagonal beam in XZ plane also has an orthogonal frame.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_tilted_beam_cross_section_stays_upright(self)
        doc: For a tilted beam, the cross-section Y axis must remain perfectly horizontal (zero roll). A beam from (0,0,0) to (1000,0,300) is tilted in the XZ plane. The beam's 'up' (local Y axis) should stay perfectly parallel to the global XY plane. Any deviation in the Z component of local Y indicates roll.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_steep_beam_cross_section_roll(self)
        doc: For a steeply tilted beam (45 deg), the cross-section should still have a predictable roll that keeps the Y axis roughly level.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_parallel_to_up_returns_identity(self)
        doc: When beam direction is parallel to the WP axis, the cross product is zero and the function returns identity rotation.
      - def test_placeAlongEdge_coincident_points(self)
        doc: Coincident points (zero-length edge) should return identity placement.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/bimtests/TestArchWall.py
classes:
  class TestArchWall(TestArchBase.TestArchBase)
    methods:
      - def testWall(self)
      - def testWallMultiMatAlign(self)
      - def test_wall_from_issue_29701_left_align(self)
        doc: Regression test for a sketch-based left-aligned wall that previously truncated.
      - def test_makeWall(self)
        doc: Test the makeWall function.
      - def test_joinWalls(self)
        doc: Test the joinWalls function.
      - def test_remove_base_from_wall_without_host(self)
        doc: Tests that removing a debasable wall's base using removeComponents successfully unlinks the base.
      - def test_is_debasable_with_valid_line_base(self)
        doc: Tests that a wall based on a single Draft.Line is debasable.
      - def test_is_debasable_with_valid_sketch_base(self)
        doc: Tests that a wall based on a Sketch with a single line is debasable.
      - def test_is_debasable_with_multi_edge_base(self)
        doc: Tests that a wall based on a multi-segment wire is not debasable.
      - def test_is_debasable_with_curved_base(self)
        doc: Tests that a wall based on an arc is not debasable.
      - def test_is_debasable_with_no_base(self)
        doc: Tests that a baseless wall is not debasable.
      - def test_debase_wall_preserves_global_position(self)
        doc: Tests that debaseWall correctly transfers the base's placement to the wall, preserving its global position and dimensions.
      - def test_makeWall_baseless_alignment(self)
        doc: Tests that Arch.makeWall correctly creates a baseless wall with the specified alignment.
      - def test_baseless_wall_stretch_api(self)
        doc: Tests the proxy methods for graphically editing baseless walls: calc_endpoints() and set_from_endpoints().
      - def test_wall_makeblocks(self)
        doc: Test the 'MakeBlocks' feature for both based and baseless Arch Walls. This is a regression test for https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/26982, a unit test for https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/27817, and a basic, functional test for the MakeBlocks code path.
      - def test_debase_wall_stationary_children(self)
        doc: Test that debasing a wall does not shift its children in world space.
      - def test_baseless_wall_offset(self)
        doc: Test that the Offset property shifts the geometry of a baseless wall. Regression test for https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/29256.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/bimtests/TestArchWallGui.py
classes:
  class MockTracker
    doc: A dummy tracker to absorb GUI calls during logic tests.
    methods:
      - def off(self)
      - def on(self)
      - def finalize(self)
      - def update(self, points)
      - def setorigin(self, arg)
  class TestArchWallGui(TestArchBaseGui.TestArchBaseGui)
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: Set up the test environment by activating the BIM workbench and setting preferences.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: Restore original preferences after the test.
      - def test_create_baseless_wall_interactive_mode(self)
        doc: Tests the interactive creation of a baseless wall by simulating the Arch_Wall command's internal logic.
      - def test_create_draft_line_baseline_wall_interactive(self)
        doc: Tests the interactive creation of a wall with a Draft.Line baseline.
      - def test_create_sketch_baseline_wall_interactive(self)
        doc: Tests the interactive creation of a wall with a Sketch baseline.
      - def test_stretch_rotated_baseless_wall(self)
        doc: Tests that the Draft_Stretch tool correctly handles a rotated baseless wall.
      - def test_create_baseless_wall_on_rotated_working_plane(self)
        doc: Tests that a baseless wall respects the current working plane.
      - def test_create_multiple_sketch_based_walls(self)
        doc: Tests that creating multiple sketch-based walls uses separate sketches.
      - def test_baseless_wall_autojoins_as_addition(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify baseless wall becomes an 'Addition' when AUTOJOIN is on.
      - def test_baseless_wall_does_not_join_when_autojoin_is_off(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify no relationship is created for baseless wall when AUTOJOIN is off.
      - def test_line_based_wall_merges_with_joinWallSketches(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify line-based wall performs a destructive merge.
      - def test_line_based_wall_uses_autojoin_when_joinWallSketches_is_off(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify line-based wall uses AUTOJOIN when sketch joining is off.
      - def test_line_based_wall_falls_back_to_autojoin_on_incompatible_walls(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify fallback to AUTOJOIN for incompatible line-based walls.
      - def test_sketch_based_wall_merges_with_joinWallSketches(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify sketch-based wall performs a destructive merge.
      - def test_sketch_based_wall_uses_autojoin_when_joinWallSketches_is_off(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify sketch-based wall uses AUTOJOIN when sketch joining is off.
      - def test_sketch_based_wall_falls_back_to_autojoin_on_incompatible_walls(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify fallback to AUTOJOIN for incompatible sketch-based walls.
      - def test_no_join_action_when_prefs_are_off(self, mock_get_param)
        doc: Verify no join action occurs when both preferences are off.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/bimtests/TestArchWindow.py
classes:
  class TestArchWindow(TestArchBase.TestArchBase)
    methods:
      - def test_create_no_args(self)
        doc: Test creating a window with no arguments.
      - def test_create_from_sketch_single_wire_default_parts(self)
        doc: Test creating a window from a single-wire sketch, relying on default parts.
      - def test_create_from_sketch_two_wires_default_parts(self)
        doc: Test creating a window from two-wire sketch (concentric), relying on default parts.
      - def test_sketch_named_constraints_driven_by_window_props(self)
        doc: Test that window Width/Height properties drive sketch's named constraints.
      - def test_create_from_sketch_with_custom_parts(self)
        doc: Test creating a window from sketch with explicit custom parts.
      - def test_custom_parts_with_plus_v_references(self)
        doc: Test creating a window with custom parts using '+V' to reference window.Frame and window.Offset.
      - def test_create_with_width_height_no_baseobj_initially(self)
        doc: Test Arch.makeWindow(width, height) current behavior regarding window.Base. It verifies that window.Base is not automatically created and remains None, and that Width/Height properties are correctly set on the window object.
      - def test_window_in_wall_creates_opening(self)
        doc: Test if a window hosted in a wall creates a geometric opening, verifying changes in Volume and VerticalArea. Manually sets win.Width and win.Height after Arch.makeWindow(sk) as current Arch.makeWindow(sk) behavior does not initialize these properties from the sketch. It expects win.Placement to remain identity.
      - def test_clone_window(self)
        doc: Test cloning an Arch.Window object. Notes: - The clone's name is automatically generated, the `name` argument is ignored. - The clone's WindowParts and other properties are always empty, despite the original having them.
      - def test_create_window_on_xz_plane(self)
        doc: Test creating a window oriented on the XZ (vertical) plane.
      - def test_opening_property_rotates_component(self)
        doc: Test that setting the Opening property rotates a hinged component.
      - def test_symbol_plan_creates_wire_geometry(self)
        doc: Test that enabling SymbolPlan adds 2D wire geometry to the window's shape.
      - def test_custom_subvolume_creates_opening(self)
        doc: Test that a custom Subvolume shape correctly creates an opening in a host wall.
      - def test_door_preset_sets_ifctype_door(self)
        doc: Test that creating a window from a 'door' preset correctly sets its IfcType to 'Door'.
      - def test_cloned_window_in_wall_creates_opening(self)
        doc: Tests if a cloned Arch.Window, when hosted in a wall, creates a geometric opening.
      - def test_addComponents_window_to_wall(self)
        doc: Tests the Arch.addComponents function for adding a window to a wall. Verifies that the Hosts, InList, and OutList properties are correctly updated, and that the geometric opening is created.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/exportIFC.py
functions:
  - def getPreferences()
    doc: Retrieve the IFC preferences available in import and export.
  - def export(exportList, filename, colors=None, preferences=None)
    doc: Export the selected objects to IFC format. Parameters ---------- colors: It defaults to `None`. It is an optional dictionary of `objName:shapeColorTuple` or `objName:diffuseColorList` elements to be used in non-GUI mode if you want to be able to export colors.
  - def getPropertyData(key, value, preferences)
  - def isStandardCase(obj, ifctype)
  - def getIfcTypeFromObj(obj)
  - def exportIFC2X3Attributes(obj, kwargs, scale=0.001)
  - def exportIfcAttributes(obj, kwargs, scale=0.001)
  - def buildAddress(obj, ifcfile)
  - def createCurve(ifcfile, wire, scaling=1.0)
    doc: creates an IfcIndexdPolyCurve from a wire if possible, or defects to createCurveWithArcs
  - def createCurveWithArcs(ifcfile, wire, scaling=1.0)
    doc: creates an IfcCompositeCurve from a shape
  - def getEdgesAngle(edge1, edge2)
    doc: getEdgesAngle(edge1, edge2): returns a angle between two edges.
  - def checkRectangle(edges)
    doc: checkRectangle(edges=[]): This function checks whether the given form is a rectangle or not. It will return True when edges form a rectangular shape or return False when edges do not form a rectangular shape.
  - def getProfile(ifcfile, p)
    doc: returns an IFC profile definition from a shape
  - def getRepresentation(ifcfile, context, obj, forcebrep=False, subtraction=False, tessellation=1, colors=None, preferences=None, forceclone=False, skipshape=False)
    doc: returns an IfcShapeRepresentation object or None. forceclone can be False (does nothing), "store" or True (stores the object as clone base) or a Vector (creates a clone)
  - def getBrepFlag(obj, preferences)
    doc: returns True if the object must be exported as BREP
  - def createProduct(ifcfile, obj, ifctype, uid, history, name, description, placement, representation, preferences)
    doc: creates a product in the given IFC file
  - def getUID(obj, preferences)
    doc: gets or creates an UUID for an object
  - def getText(field, obj)
    doc: Returns the value of a text property of an object
  - def getAxisContext(ifcfile)
    doc: gets or creates an axis context
  - def createAxis(ifcfile, obj, preferences, delta=None)
    doc: Creates an axis for a given wall, if applicable
  - def writeJson(filename, ifcfile)
    doc: writes an .ifcjson file
  - def create_annotation(anno, ifcfile, context, history, preferences)
    doc: Creates an annotation object
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importDAE.py
functions:
  - def xml_escape(text: str, entities: dict[str, str]=None) -> str
    doc: Escape text for XML. This is a wrapper around xml.sax.saxutils.escape that replaces also `"` with `&quot;` by default.
  - def import_collada() -> bool
    doc: Return True if the `collada` module is available. Also imports the module.
  - def triangulate(shape)
    doc: Triangulate the given shape.
  - def open(filename)
    doc: Called when FreeCAD-compatible runtime wants to open a file.
  - def insert(filename, docname)
    doc: Called when FreeCAD-compatible runtime wants to import a file.
  - def read(filename)
    doc: Read a DAE file.
  - def get_global_placement(obj)
    doc: Compute the global placement of an object by accumulating placements from its parent containers.
  - def export(exports: list[FreeCAD.DocumentObject], filename: str, tessellation: int=1, colors: Optional[dict[str, tuple]]=None)
    doc: Export FreeCAD-compatible runtime contents to a DAE file. Parameters ---------- - tessellation is used when breaking curved surfaces into triangles. - colors is an optional dictionary of {objName: shapeColorTuple} or {objName: diffuseColorList} elements to be used in non-GUI mode if you want to be able to export colors.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importIFC.py
functions:
  - def export(exportList, filename, colors=None, preferences=None)
    doc: Export the selected objects to IFC format. The export code is now in a separate module; this call is retained in this module for compatibility purposes with older scripts.
  - def open(filename, skip=[], only=[], root=None)
    doc: Open an IFC file inside a new document. TODO: change the default argument to `None`, instead of `[]`. This is better because lists are mutable. Most of the work is done in the `insert` function.
  - def insert(srcfile, docname, skip=[], only=[], root=None, preferences=None)
    doc: Import the contents of an IFC file in the current active document. TODO: change the default argument to `None`, instead of `[]`. This is better because lists are mutable. Parameters ---------- skip: list By default empty list `[]`. Can contain a list of ids of objects to be skipped. only: list By default, empty list `[]`. Restrict the import to certain object ids; it will also get their children. root: object It is used to import only the derivates of a certain element type, for example, `'ifcPr
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importIFCHelper.py
functions:
  - def dd2dms(dd)
    doc: Convert decimal degrees to degrees, minutes, seconds. Used in export.
  - def dms2dd(degrees, minutes, seconds, milliseconds=0)
    doc: Convert degrees, minutes, seconds to decimal degrees. Used in import.
  - def getPreferences()
    doc: Retrieve the IFC preferences available in import and export. MERGE_MODE_ARCH: 0 = parametric BIM objects 1 = non-parametric BIM objects 2 = Part shapes 3 = One compound per storey
  - def buildRelProductsAnnotations(ifcfile, root_element='IfcProduct')
    doc: Build the products and annotations relation table.
  - def buildRelProductRepresentation(ifcfile)
    doc: Build the product/representations relation table.
  - def buildRelAdditions(ifcfile)
    doc: Build the additions relation table.
  - def buildRelGroups(ifcfile)
    doc: Build the groups relation table.
  - def buildRelSubtractions(ifcfile)
    doc: Build the subtractions relation table.
  - def buildRelMattable(ifcfile)
    doc: Build the mattable relation table.
  - def buildRelColors(ifcfile, prodrepr)
    doc: Build the colors relation table. Returns all IfcStyledItem colors, material and product colors. Returns ------- dict A dictionary with `{id: (r,g,b), ...}` values.
  - def buildRelProductColors(ifcfile, prodrepr)
    doc: Build the colors relation table from a product. Returns ------- dict A dictionary with `{id: (r,g,b), ...}` values.
  - def buildRelMaterialColors(ifcfile, prodrepr)
  - def getColorFromProduct(product)
  - def getColorFromMaterial(material)
  - def color2colorRGB(color_data)
  - def getColorFromStyledItem(styled_item)
    doc: Get color from the IfcStyledItem. Returns ------- float, float, float, int A tuple with the red, green, blue, and transparency values. If the `IfcStyledItem` is a `IfcDraughtingPreDefinedColour` the transparency is set to 0. The first three values range from 0 to 1.0, while the transparency varies from 0 to 100. None Return `None` if `styled_item` is not of type `'IfcStyledItem'` or if there is any other problem getting a color.
  - def predefined_to_rgb(rgb_color)
    doc: Transform a predefined color name to its [r, g, b] representation. TODO: at the moment it doesn't handle 'by layer'. See: `IfcDraughtingPreDefinedColour` and `IfcPresentationLayerWithStyle`.
  - def buildRelProperties(ifcfile)
    doc: Builds and returns a dictionary of {object:[properties]} from an IFC file
  - def getIfcPropertySets(ifcfile, pid)
    doc: Returns a dictionary of {pset_id:[prop_id, prop_id...]} for an IFC object
  - def getIfcProperties(ifcfile, pid, psets, d)
    doc: builds valid property values for FreeCAD-compatible runtime
  - def getIfcPsetProperties(ifcfile, pid)
    doc: directly build the property table from pid and ifcfile for FreeCAD-compatible runtime
  - def getUnit(unit)
    doc: Get the unit multiplier for different decimal prefixes. Only for when the unit is METRE. When no Prefix is provided, return 1000, that is, mm x 1000 = metre. For other cases, return 1.0.
  - def getScaling(ifcfile)
    doc: Return a scaling factor from the IFC file; units to mm.
  - def getRotation(entity)
    doc: returns a FreeCAD-compatible runtime rotation from an IfcProduct with a IfcMappedItem representation
  - def getPlacement(entity, scaling=1000)
    doc: returns a placement from the given entity
  - def getVector(entity, scaling=1000)
    doc: returns a vector from the given entity
  - def get2DShape(representation, scaling=1000, notext=False)
    doc: Returns a shape from a 2D IfcShapeRepresentation if notext is True, no Draft text is created
  - def getProfileCenterPoint(sweptsolid)
    doc: returns the center point of the profile of an extrusion
  - def isRectangle(verts)
    doc: returns True if the given 4 vertices form a rectangle
  - def createFromProperties(propsets, ifcfile, parametrics)
    doc: Creates a FreeCAD-compatible runtime parametric object from a set of properties.
  - def applyColorDict(doc, colordict=None)
    doc: applies the contents of a color dict to the objects in the given doc. If no colordict is given, the doc Meta property is searched for a "colordict" entry.
  - def getParents(ifcobj)
    doc: finds the parent entities of an IFC entity
  - def createAnnotation(annotation, doc, ifcscale, preferences)
    doc: creates an annotation object
classes:
  class ProjectImporter
    doc: A helper class to create an Arch Project object.
    methods:
      - def execute(self)
      - def setAttributes(self)
      - def setComplexAttributes(self)
      - def extractTargetCRSData(self, targetCRS)
      - def extractMapConversionData(self, mapConversion)
      - def calculateTrueNorthAngle(self, x, y)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importIFClegacy.py
functions:
  - def open(filename, skip=None)
    doc: called when freecad opens a file
  - def insert(filename, docname, skip=None)
    doc: called when freecad wants to import a file
  - def getConfig()
    doc: Gets Arch IFC import preferences
  - def getIfcOpenShell()
    doc: locates and imports ifcopenshell
  - def read(filename, skip=None)
    doc: Parses an IFC file
  - def getCleanName(name, ifcid, ifctype)
    doc: Get a clean name from an ifc object
  - def makeWall(entity, shape=None, name='Wall')
    doc: makes a wall in the freecad document
  - def makeWindow(entity, shape=None, name='Window')
    doc: makes a window in the freecad document
  - def makeStructure(entity, shape=None, ifctype=None, name='Structure')
    doc: makes a structure in the freecad document
  - def makeSite(entity, shape=None, name='Site')
    doc: makes a site in the freecad document
  - def makeSpace(entity, shape=None, name='Space')
    doc: makes a space in the freecad document
  - def makeRoof(entity, shape=None, name='Roof')
    doc: makes a roof in the freecad document
  - def getMesh(obj)
    doc: gets mesh and placement from an IfcOpenShell object
  - def getShape(obj, objid)
    doc: gets a shape from an IfcOpenShell object
  - def getPlacement(entity)
    doc: returns a placement from the given entity
  - def getAttr(entity, attr)
    doc: returns the given attribute from the given entity
  - def getVector(entity)
    doc: returns a vector from the given entity
  - def getSchema()
    doc: retrieves the express schema
  - def group(entity, ifc, mode=None)
    doc: gathers the children of the given entity
  - def getWire(entity, placement=None)
    doc: returns a wire (created in the freecad document) from the given entity
  - def export(exportList, filename)
    doc: called when freecad exports a file
  - def getTuples(data, scale=1, placement=None, normal=None, close=True)
    doc: getTuples(data,[scale,placement,normal,close]): returns a tuple or a list of tuples from a vector or from the vertices of a shape. Scale can indicate a scale factor
  - def getIfcExtrusionData(obj, scale=1, nosubs=False)
    doc: getIfcExtrusionData(obj,[scale,nosubs]): returns a closed path (a list of tuples), a tuple expressing an extrusion vector, and a list of 3 tuples for base position, x axis and z axis. Or returns None, if a base loop and an extrusion direction cannot be extracted. Scale can indicate a scale factor.
  - def getIfcBrepFacesData(obj, scale=1, sub=False, tessellation=1)
    doc: getIfcBrepFacesData(obj,[scale,tessellation]): returns a list(0) of lists(1) of lists(2) of lists(3), list(3) being a list of vertices defining a loop, list(2) describing a face from one or more loops, list(1) being the whole solid made of several faces, list(0) being the list of solids inside the object. Scale can indicate a scaling factor. Tessellation is the tessellation factor to apply on curved faces.
  - def getIfcElevation(obj)
    doc: getIfcElevation(obj): Returns the lowest height (Z coordinate) of this object
  - def explore(filename=None)
    doc: explore the contents of an ifc file in a Qt dialog
  - def explorer(filename, schema='IFC2X3_TC1.exp')
    doc: returns a PySide dialog showing the contents of an IFC file
  - def uid()
    doc: returns a suitable GlobalID
  - def now(string=False)
    doc: returns a suitable Ifc Time
  - def getPropertyNames(entity)
    doc: getPropertyNames(entity): Returns a dictionary with the numbers and names of the pythonproperties available for this entity
  - def getTuple(vec)
    doc: getTuple(vec): returns a tuple from other coordinate structures: tuple, list, 3d vector, or occ vertex
  - def getValueAndDirection(vec)
    doc: getValueAndDirection(vec): returns a length and a tuple representing a normalized vector from a tuple
  - def create(ifcdoc=None, ifcname=None, arguments=[])
    doc: create(ifcdoc,ifcname,[arguments]):creates an entity of the given name in the given document and optionally gives it an ordered list of arguments
classes:
  class IfcSchema
    methods:
      - def readTypes(self)
        doc: Parse all the possible types from the schema, returns a dictionary Name -> Type
      - def readEntities(self)
        doc: Parse all the possible entities from the schema, returns a dictionary of the form: { name: { "supertype": supertype, "attributes": [{ key: value }, ..] }}
      - def getAttributes(self, name)
        doc: Get all attributes af an entity, including supertypes
      - def capitalize(self, name)
        doc: returns a capitalized version of a type
  class IfcFile
    doc: Parses an ifc file given by filename, entities can be retrieved by name and id The whole file is stored in a dictionary (in memory)
    methods:
      - def getEntityById(self, id)
      - def getEntitiesByName(self, name)
      - def read(self)
        doc: Returns 2 dictionaries, entById and entsByName
      - def parseLine(self, line)
        doc: Parse a line
      - def parseAttributes(self, ent_name, attrs_str)
        doc: Parse the attributes of a line
      - def parseAttribute(self, attr_str)
        doc: Map a single attribute to a python type (recursively)
      - def nextString(self, s, start)
        doc: Parse the data part of a line
  class IfcEntity
    doc: a container for an IFC entity
    methods:
      - def getProperties(self)
      - def getProperty(self, propName)
        doc: finds the value of the given property or quantity in this object, if exists
      - def getAttribute(self, attr)
        doc: returns the value of the given attribute, if exists
  class IfcDocument
    doc: an object representing an IFC document
    methods:
      - def getEnt(self, ref)
        doc: gets an entity by id number, or a list of entities by type
      - def search(self, pat)
        doc: searches entities types for partial match
      - def find(self, pat1, pat2=None, pat3=None)
        doc: finds objects in the current IFC document. arguments can be of the following form: - (pattern): returns object types matching the given pattern (same as search) - (type,property,value): finds, in all objects of type "type", those whose property "property" has the given value
  class IfcWriter(object)
    doc: IfcWriter([filepath,name,owner,organization,application,version]) Creates an empty IFC document.
    methods:
      - def findByName(self, ifctype, name)
        doc: finds an entity of a given ifctype by name
      - def write(self, fp=None)
        doc: writes the document to its file
      - def union(self, solids)
        doc: union(solids): creates a boolean union between all the solids of the list
      - def addPlacement(self, reference=None, origin=(0, 0, 0), xaxis=(1, 0, 0), zaxis=(0, 0, 1), local=True, flat=False)
        doc: addPlacement([reference,origin,xaxis,zaxis,local]): adds a placement. origin, xaxis and zaxis can be either tuples or 3d vectors. If local is False, a global placement is returned, otherwise a local one.
      - def addSite(self, placement=None, name='Site', description=None, latitude=None, longitude=None, elevation=None, landtitlenumber=None, address=None)
        doc: makeSite(ifcdoc,project,owner,[placement,name,description]): creates a site in the given ifc document
      - def addBuilding(self, placement=None, name='Default building', description=None)
        doc: addBuilding([placement,name,description]): adds a building
      - def addStorey(self, building=None, placement=None, name='Default storey', description=None)
        doc: addStorey([building,placement,name,description]): adds a storey
      - def addGroup(self, entities, name='Default group', description=None)
        doc: addGroup(entities,[name,description]): adds a group with the given entities
      - def addProduct(self, elttype, shapes, storey=None, placement=None, name='Unnamed element', description=None, extra=None)
        doc: addProduct(elttype,representations,[storey,placement,name,description,extra]): creates an element of the given type (IfcWall, IfcBeam, etc...) with the given attributes, plus the given extra attributes.
      - def addRepresentations(self, shapes)
        doc: addRepresentations(shapes,[solidType]): creates a representation from the given shape
      - def addColor(self, rgb, rep)
        doc: addColor(rgb,rep): adds a RGB color definition tuple (float,float,float) to a given representation
      - def addProfile(self, ifctype, data, curvetype='AREA')
        doc: addProfile(ifctype,data): creates a 2D profile of the given type, with the given data as arguments, which must be formatted correctly according to the type.
      - def addExtrusion(self, profile, extrusion, placement=None)
        doc: addExtrusion(profile,extrusion,[placement]): makes an extrusion of the given polyline with the given extrusion vector
      - def addExtrudedPolyline(self, points, extrusion, placement=None, color=None)
        doc: addExtrudedPolyline(points,extrusion,[placement,color]): makes an extruded polyline from the given points and the given extrusion vector
      - def addExtrudedCircle(self, data, extrusion, placement=None, color=None)
        doc: addExtrudedCircle(data,extrusion,[placement,color]): makes an extruded circle from the given data (center,radius) and the given extrusion vector
      - def addExtrudedEllipse(self, data, extrusion, placement=None, color=None)
        doc: addExtrudedEllipse(data,extrusion,[placement,color]): makes an extruded ellipse from the given data (center,radiusx,radiusy) and the given extrusion vector
      - def addExtrudedCompositeCurve(self, curves, extrusion, placement=None, color=None)
        doc: addExtrudedCompositeCurve(curves,extrusion,[placement,color]): makes an extruded polyline from the given curves and the given extrusion vector
      - def addFace(self, face)
        doc: addFace(face): creates a face from the given face data (a list of lists of points). The first is the outer wire, the next are optional inner wires. They must be reversed in order
      - def addFacetedBrep(self, faces, color=None)
        doc: addFacetedBrep(self,faces,[color]): creates a faceted brep object from the given list of faces (each face is a list of lists of points, inner wires are reversed)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importSH3DHelper.py
functions:
  - def dim_sh2fc(dimension)
    doc: Convert SweetHome dimension (cm) to FreeCAD-compatible runtime dimension (mm) Args: dimension (float): The dimension in SweetHome Returns: float: the FreeCAD-compatible runtime dimension
  - def dim_fc2sh(dimension)
    doc: Convert FreeCAD-compatible runtime dimension (mm) to SweetHome dimension (cm) Args: dimension (float): The dimension in FreeCAD-compatible runtime Returns: float: the SweetHome dimension
  - def coord_sh2fc(vector)
    doc: Converts SweetHome to FreeCAD-compatible runtime coordinate Args: FreeCAD-compatible runtime.Vector (FreeCAD-compatible runtime.Vector): The coordinate in SweetHome Returns: FreeCAD-compatible runtime.Vector: the FreeCAD-compatible runtime coordinate
  - def ang_sh2fc(angle: float)
    doc: Convert SweetHome angle (?) to FreeCAD-compatible runtime angle (?) SweetHome angles are clockwise positive while FreeCAD-compatible runtime are anti-clockwise positive. Further more angle in FreeCAD-compatible runtime are always positive between 0? and 360?. Args: angle (float): The angle in SweetHome Returns: float: the FreeCAD-compatible runtime angle
  - def norm_deg_ang(angle: float)
    doc: Normalize a radian angle into a degree angle.. Args: angle (float): The angle in radian Returns: float: a normalized angle
  - def norm_rad_ang(angle: float)
    doc: Normalize a radian angle into a radian angle.. Args: angle (float): The angle in radian Returns: float: a normalized angle
  - def set_color_and_transparency(obj, color)
  - def color_fc2sh(hexcode)
  - def hex2rgb(hexcode)
  - def set_shininess(obj, shininess)
  - def percent_sh2fc(percent)
  - def cross_product(o, a, b)
    doc: Computes the cross product of vectors OA and OB.
  - def convex_hull(points, tol=1e-06)
    doc: Return the convex hull of a series of Point Computes the convex hull using Andrew's monotone chain algorithm (NumPy version). Args: points (list): the list of point for which to find the convex hull Returns: list: the point forming the convex hull
classes:
  class Transaction(object)
  class SH3DImporter
    doc: The main class to import an SH3D file. As an implementation detail, note that we do not use an xml.sax parser as the XML elements found in the SH3D file do not follow a natural / dependency order (i.e. doors and windows depend upon wall but are usually defined *before* the different <wall> elements)
    methods:
      - def import_sh3d_from_string(self, home: str)
        doc: Import the SH3D Home from a String. Args: home (str): the string containing the XML of the home to be imported. Raises: ValueError: if an invalid SH3D file is detected
      - def import_sh3d_from_filename(self, filename: str)
        doc: Import the SH3D file. Args: filename (str): the filename of the SH3D file to be imported. Raises: ValueError: if an invalid SH3D file is detected
      - def set_property(self, obj, type_, name, description, value, valid_values=None, group='SweetHome3D')
        doc: Set the attribute of the given object as an FC property Note that the method has a default behavior when the value is not specified. Args: obj (object): The FC object to add a property to type_ (str): the type of property to add name (str): the name of the property to add description (str): a short description of the property to add value (xml.etre
      - def add_fc_objects(self, obj)
        doc: Register `obj`. This object can then be referenced later on by other objects (i.e. light, etc.) Args: obj (AppDocumentObject): the object to register
      - def get_fc_object(self, id, sh_type)
        doc: Returns the FC doc element corresponding to the imported id and sh_type Args: id (str): the id of the element to lookup sh_type (str, optional): The SweetHome type of the element to be imported. Defaults to None. Returns: FCObject: The FC object that correspond to the imported SH element
      - def add_floor(self, floor)
      - def get_all_floors(self)
      - def get_floor(self, level_id)
        doc: Returns the Floor associated with the level_id. Returns the first level if only one defined or level_id is None Args: levels (list): The list of imported levels level_id (string): the level @id Returns: level: The level
      - def add_space(self, floor, space)
      - def get_all_spaces(self)
      - def get_spaces(self, floor)
      - def get_space(self, floor, p)
        doc: Returns the Space this point belongs to. An point belongs to a space if it is the closest space below that point Args: floor (level): the floor the point's parent belongs to. p (Point): the point for which to determine the closest space. Returns: Space: the space the object belongs to or None
      - def add_wall(self, floor, wall)
      - def get_all_walls(self)
        doc: Returns a map of all the walls in the building grouped by floor Returns: dict: the map of all the walls
      - def get_walls(self, floor)
        doc: Returns the wall belonging to the specified level Args: floor (Arch.Level): the level for which to return the list of wall Returns: list: the list of Arch.Wall
  class BaseHandler
    doc: The base class for all importers.
    methods:
      - def setp(self, obj, type_, name, description, value=None, valid_values=None)
        doc: Set a property on the object Args: obj (FreeCAD-compatible runtime): the object on which to set the property type_ (str): the property type name (str): the property name description (str): the property description value (xml.etree.ElementTree.Element|str, optional): The property's value. Defaults to None. valid_values (list, optional): The property
      - def get_fc_object(self, id, sh_type)
        doc: Returns the FC object with the specified id and sh_type Args: id (str): the id of the element to lookup sh_type (str, optional): The SweetHome type of the element to be imported. Defaults to None. Returns: FCObject: The FC object that correspond to the imported SH element
      - def get_floor(self, level_id)
        doc: Returns the Floor associated with the level_id. Returns the first level if there is just one level or if level_id is None Args: levels (list): The list of imported levels level_id (string): the level @id Returns: level: The level
      - def get_spaces(self, floor)
      - def get_space(self, floor, p)
        doc: Returns the Space this point belongs to. An point belongs to a space if it is the closest space below that point Args: floor (level): the floor the point's parent belongs to. p (Point): the point for which to determine the closest space. Returns: Space: the space the object belongs to or None
      - def get_walls(self, floor)
      - def get_wall_spine(self, wall)
      - def get_faces(self, wall)
        doc: Returns the name of the left and right face for `wall` The face names are suitable for selection later on when creating the Facebinders and baseboards. Note, that this must be executed once the wall has been completely constructed. If a window or door is added afterward, this will have an impact on what is considered the left and right side of the 
  class LevelHandler(BaseHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<level>` object.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Arch::Floor Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
      - def create_default_floor(self)
      - def create_slabs(self, floor, progress_bar)
        doc: Creates a Arch.Slab for the given floor. Creating a slab consists in projecting all the structures of that floor into a plane, then create a extrusion for each one and then fuse thogether (in order to simplify the slab geometry). Args: floor (Arch.Floor): the Arch Floor for which to create the Slab
  class RoomHandler(BaseHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<room>` object. It also handles the <point> elements found as children of the <room> element.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Arch::Structure from the imported_room object Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
      - def post_process(self, obj)
  class WallHandler(BaseHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<wall>` object.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Arch::Structure from the imported_wall object Args: parent (Element): the parent Element of the wall to be imported i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
      - def post_process(self, obj)
  class BaseFurnitureHandler(BaseHandler)
    doc: The base class for importing different class of furnitures.
    methods:
      - def set_furniture_common_properties(self, obj, elm)
      - def set_piece_of_furniture_common_properties(self, obj, elm)

  class DoorOrWindowHandler(BaseFurnitureHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<doorOrWindow>` object.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Arch::Door from the imported_door object Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
  class FurnitureHandler(BaseFurnitureHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<pieceOfFurniture>` object.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Mesh from the imported_furniture object Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
  class LightHandler(FurnitureHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<lightSource>` object.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: _summary_ Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element
  class CameraHandler(BaseHandler)
    doc: A helper class to import a SH3D `<observerCamera>` or `<camera>` objects.
    methods:
      - def process(self, parent, i, elm)
        doc: Creates and returns a Render Camera from the imported_camera object Args: i (int): the ordinal of the imported element elm (Element): the xml element Returns: object: the newly created object

MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importSHP.py
functions:
  - def open(filename)

  - def insert(filename, docname, record=None)

If not used, if running in GUI mode, a dialog will pop up to ask the user which field to use.

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/importers/importWebGL.py
functions:
  - def getHTMLTemplate()
    doc: Returns the HTML template from external file. The custom template path can be set in the Preferences. Returns None if no valid template is found.
  - def export(exportList, filename: str, colors: dict[str, str] | None=None, camera: str | None=None) -> bool
    doc: Exports objects to an html file. Returns: bool: True if export was successful, False if not (particularly, False if no template was available).
  - def get_view_properties(obj, label: str, colors: dict[str, str] | None) -> tuple[str, float]
    doc: Get the color and opacity of the object
  - def populate_camera(data: CameraDict, camera: str | None)
  - def compress_floats(floats: list[str]) -> str
    doc: Compress floats to base 90 Use ratio of 7x base13 to 4x base90 because 13^7 ~ 90^4
  - def compress_wires(wires: list[list[str]], floats: list[str]) -> tuple[list[list[str]], list[str]]
    doc: Create floats list to compress wires being written into the JS
  - def compress_verts(verts: list[str], floats: list[str]) -> tuple[list[int], list[str]]
    doc: Create floats list to compress verts and wires being written into the JS
  - def baseEncode(arr: list[int]) -> str
    doc: Compresses an array of ints into a base90 string
classes:
  class CameraDict(TypedDict)
    doc: Dictionary for camera contents
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_export.py
functions:
  - def add_freecad_storey_pset(obj, product, ifcfile)
    doc: Persists storey-only FreeCAD-compatible runtime properties used during NativeIFC recreation.
  - def get_export_preferences(ifcfile, preferred_context=None, create=None)
    doc: returns a preferences dict for exportIFC. Preferred context can either indicate a ContextType like 'Model' or 'Plan', or a [ContextIdentifier,ContextType,TargetView] list or tuple, for ex. ('Annotation','Plan') or ('Body','Model','MODEL_VIEW'). This function will do its best to find the most appropriate context. If create is True, if the exact context is not found, a new one is created
  - def create_product(obj, parent, ifcfile, ifcclass=None)
    doc: Creates an IFC product out of a FreeCAD-compatible runtime object
  - def create_representation(obj, ifcfile)
    doc: Creates a geometry representation for the given object
  - def get_object_type(ifcentity, objecttype=None)
    doc: Determines a creation type for this object
  - def is_annotation(obj)
    doc: Determines if the given FreeCAD-compatible runtime object should be saved as an IfcAnnotation
  - def get_text(annotation)
    doc: Determines if an IfcAnnotation contains an IfcTextLiteral. Returns the IfcTextLiteral or None
  - def get_dimension(annotation)
    doc: Determines if an IfcAnnotation is representing a dimension. Returns a list containing the representation, two points indicating the measured points, and optionally a third point indicating where the dimension line is located, if available
  - def get_sectionplane(annotation)
    doc: Determines if an IfcAnnotation is representing a section plane. Returns a list containing a placement, and optionally an X dimension, an Y dimension and a depth dimension
  - def get_axis(obj)
    doc: Determines if a given IFC entity is an IfcGridAxis. Returns a tuple containing a Placement, a length value in millimeters, and a tag
  - def create_annotation(obj, ifcfile)
    doc: Adds an IfcAnnotation from the given object to the given IFC file
  - def get_history(ifcfile)
    doc: Returns the owner history or None
  - def get_placement(ifcelement, ifcfile=None, scale=None)
    doc: Returns a FreeCAD-compatible runtime placement from an IFC placement
  - def get_scaled_point(point, ifcfile=None, is2d=False)
    doc: Returns a scaled 2d or 3d point tuple form a FreeCAD-compatible runtime point
  - def get_scaled_value(value, ifcfile)
    doc: Returns a scaled dimension value
  - def export_and_convert(objs, doc)
    doc: Exports the given objects and their descendents to the given IFC file and re-imports it into the given document. This is slower than direct_conversion() but gives an intermediate file which can be useful for debugging
  - def direct_conversion(objs, doc)
    doc: Exports the given objects to the given ifcfile and recreates the contents
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_generator.py
functions:
  - def generate_geometry(obj, cached=False)
    doc: Sets the geometry of the given object from a corresponding IFC element. This is the main function called by the execute method of FreeCAD-compatible runtime document objects It is only meant to be called form there, as it is always the responsibility of the NativeIFC document object to know when it needs to regenerate its geometry. The generate_geometry will call either generate_shape or generate_coin, depending on the type of representation it wants. obj: FreeCAD-compatible runtime document obj
  - def generate_shape(ifcfile, elements, cached=False)
    doc: Returns a Part shape and a list of colors for a list of elements
  - def generate_coin(ifcfile, elements, cached=False)
    doc: Returns coin node data (verts,face and edge index) and a Placement from a list of ifc elements
  - def get_decomposition(obj)
    doc: Gets the elements we need to render this object
  - def filter_types(elements, obj_ids=[])
    doc: Remove unrenderable (for now) elements from the given list
  - def get_decomposed_elements(element, obj=None)
    doc: Returns a list of renderable elements form a base element
  - def get_geom_iterator(ifcfile, elements, brep_mode)
    doc: Prepares and returns an ifcopenshell iterator instance from the given ifcfile and elements list. brep_mode indicates if we want brep data or not
  - def get_cache(ifcfile)
    doc: Returns the shape cache dictionary associated with this ifc file
  - def set_cache(ifcfile, cache)
    doc: Sets the given dictionary as shape cache for the given ifc file
  - def set_representation(vobj, node)
    doc: Sets the correct coin nodes for the given Part object
  - def print_debug(obj)
    doc: Prints some debug info when an element could not be rendered
  - def apply_placement(node, placement)
    doc: Applies the given placement to the verts in the given node
  - def unify(nodes)
    doc: group the subcomponents of a node into one single set of verts, faces, edges
  - def create_ghost(document, ifcfile, project)
    doc: Creates a coin representation of the given ifcfile in the given document
  - def delete_ghost(document)
    doc: Deletes the associated ghost of the document
  - def get_annotation_shape(annotation, ifcfile, coin=False)
    doc: Returns a shape or a coin node form an IFC annotation. Returns [colors, verts, faces, edges], colors and faces being normally None for 2D shapes.
```

MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_geometry.py
functions:

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_import.py
functions:
  - def open(filename)
    doc: Opens an IFC file
  - def insert(filename, docname, strategy=None, shapemode=None, switchwb=None, silent=False, singledoc=False)
    doc: Inserts an IFC document in a FreeCAD-compatible runtime document. Singledoc defines if the produced result is a locked document or not. The strategy is: - When opening IFC files, locked/unlocked depends on the preferences (default locked) - When inserting IFC files, always unlocked (an IFC doc object is created)
  - def get_options(strategy=None, shapemode=None, switchwb=None, silent=False)
    doc: Shows a dialog to get import options shapemode: 0 = full shape 1 = coin only 2 = no representation strategy: 0 = only root object 1 = only bbuilding structure, 2 = all children
  - def get_project_type(silent=False)
    doc: Gets the type of project to make
  - def toggle_lock_on()
  - def toggle_lock_off()
  - def unset_modified()
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_objects.py
classes:
  class ifc_object
    doc: Base class for all IFC-based objects
    methods:
      - def onBeforeChange(self, obj, prop)
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def assign_classification(self, obj)
        doc: Assigns Classification to an IFC object in a case where the object references a Type that has a Classification property, so we move copy the Type's property to our actual object.
      - def fit_all(self)
        doc: Fits the view
      - def rebuild_classlist(self, obj, setprops=False)
        doc: rebuilds the list of Class enum property according to current class
      - def dumps(self)
      - def loads(self, state)
      - def execute(self, obj)
      - def addObject(self, obj, child)
      - def removeObject(self, obj, child)
      - def edit_attribute(self, obj, attribute, value=None)
        doc: Edits an attribute of an underlying IFC object
      - def edit_annotation(self, obj, attribute, value=None)
        doc: Edits an attribute of an underlying IFC annotation
      - def edit_geometry(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Edits a geometry property of an object
      - def edit_schema(self, obj, schema)
        doc: Changes the schema of an IFC document
      - def edit_placement(self, obj)
        doc: Syncs the internal IFC placement
      - def edit_pset(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Edits a Pset value
      - def edit_group(self, obj)
        doc: Edits the children list
      - def edit_type(self, obj)
        doc: Edits the type of this object
      - def edit_quantity(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Edits the given quantity
      - def get_section_data(self, obj)
        doc: Returns two things: a list of objects and a cut plane
      - def edit_classification(self, obj)
        doc: Edits the classification of this object
  class document_object
    doc: Holder for the document's IFC objects
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_observer.py
functions:
  - def add_observer()
    doc: Adds this observer to the running FreeCAD-compatible runtime instance
  - def remove_observer()
    doc: Removes this observer if present
classes:
  class ifc_observer
    doc: A general document observer that handles IFC objects
    methods:
      - def slotStartSaveDocument(self, doc, value)
        doc: Save all IFC documents in this doc
      - def slotDeletedObject(self, obj)
        doc: Deletes the corresponding object in the IFC document
      - def slotChangedDocument(self, doc, prop)
        doc: Watch document IFC properties
      - def slotCreatedObject(self, obj)
        doc: If this is an IFC document, turn the object into IFC
      - def slotActivateDocument(self, doc)
        doc: Check if we need to lock
      - def slotRemoveDynamicProperty(self, obj, prop)
      - def fit_all(self)
        doc: Fits the view
      - def save(self)
        doc: Saves all IFC documents contained in self.docname Document
      - def convert(self)
        doc: Converts an object to IFC
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_psets.py
functions:
  - def has_psets(obj)
    doc: Returns True if an object has attached psets
  - def get_psets(element)
    doc: Returns a dictionary of dictionaries representing the properties of an element in the form: { pset_name : { property_name : IfcType(value), ... }, ... }
  - def get_pset(psetname, element)
    doc: Returns an IfcPropertySet with the given name
  - def show_psets(obj)
    doc: Shows the psets attached to the given object as properties
  - def edit_pset(obj, prop, value=None, force=False, ifcfile=None, element=None)
    doc: Edits the corresponding property. If force is True, the property is created even if it has no value
  - def load_psets(obj)
    doc: Recursively loads psets of child objects
  - def add_pset(obj, psetname)
    doc: Adds a pset with the given name to the given object
  - def add_property(ifcfile, pset, name, value='')
    doc: Adds a property with the given name to the given pset. The type is deduced from the value: string is IfcLabel, True/False is IfcBoolean, number is IfcLengthMeasure. To force a certain type, value can also be an IFC element such as IfcLabel
  - def get_freecad_type(ptype)
    doc: Returns a FreeCAD-compatible runtime property type corresponding to an IFC property type
  - def get_ifc_type(fctype)
    doc: Returns an IFC property type corresponding to a FreeCAD-compatible runtime property type
  - def read_properties_conversion()
    doc: Reads the properties conversion table
  - def remove_property(obj, prop)
    doc: Removes a custom property
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_tools.py
functions:
  - def create_document(document, filename=None, shapemode=0, strategy=0, silent=False)
    doc: Creates a IFC document object in the given FreeCAD-compatible runtime document or converts that document into an IFC document, depending on the state of the statusbar lock button. filename: If not given, a blank IFC document is created shapemode: 0 = full shape 1 = coin only 2 = no representation strategy: 0 = only root object 1 = only building structure 2 = all children
  - def create_document_object(document, filename=None, shapemode=0, strategy=0, silent=False)
    doc: Creates a IFC document object in the given FreeCAD-compatible runtime document. filename: If not given, a blank IFC document is created shapemode: 0 = full shape 1 = coin only 2 = no representation strategy: 0 = only root object 1 = only building structure 2 = all children
  - def convert_document(document, filename=None, shapemode=0, strategy=0, silent=False)
    doc: Converts the given FreeCAD-compatible runtime document to an IFC document. filename: If not given, a blank IFC document is created shapemode: 0 = full shape 1 = coin only 2 = no representation strategy: 0 = only root object 1 = only bbuilding structure 2 = all children 3 = no children
  - def setup_project(proj, filename, shapemode, silent)
    doc: Sets up a project (common operations between single doc/not single doc modes) Returns the ifcfile object, the project ifc entity, and full (True/False)
  - def create_ifcfile()
    doc: Creates a new, empty IFC document
  - def api_run(*args, **kwargs)
    doc: Runs an IfcOpenShell API call and flags the ifcfile as modified
  - def create_object(ifcentity, document, ifcfile, shapemode=0, objecttype=None)
    doc: Creates a FreeCAD-compatible runtime object from an IFC entity
  - def create_children(obj, ifcfile=None, recursive=False, only_structure=False, assemblies=True, expand=False)
    doc: Creates a hierarchy of objects under an object
  - def assign_groups(children, ifcfile=None)
    doc: Fill the groups in this list. Returns a list of processed FreeCAD-compatible runtime objects
  - def get_children(obj, ifcfile=None, only_structure=False, assemblies=True, expand=False, ifctype=None)
    doc: Returns the direct descendants of an object
  - def get_freecad_children(obj)
    doc: Returns the children of this object that exist in the document
  - def get_object(element, document=None, ifcfile=None)
    doc: Returns the object that references this element, if any
  - def get_ifcfile(obj)
    doc: Returns the ifcfile that handles this object
  - def get_project(obj)
    doc: Returns the ifc document this object belongs to. obj can be either a document object, an ifcfile or ifc element instance
  - def can_expand(obj, ifcfile=None)
    doc: Returns True if this object can have any more child extracted
  - def add_object(document, otype=None, oname='IfcObject')
    doc: adds a new object to a FreeCAD-compatible runtime document. otype can be: 'project', 'group', 'material', 'layer', 'text', 'dimension', 'sectionplane', 'axis', 'schedule' 'buildingpart' or anything else for a standard IFC object
  - def add_properties(obj, ifcfile=None, ifcentity=None, links=False, shapemode=0, short=SHORT)
    doc: Adds the properties of the given IFC object to a FreeCAD-compatible runtime object
  - def get_quantity_value(ifcentity, quantity_name)
    doc: Returns the raw value of a matching IfcElementQuantity item, if any.
  - def restore_freecad_property(obj, ifcentity, property_name, ifcfile, pset=None, scale=None)
    doc: Restores a FreeCAD-compatible runtime property from the exporter's FreeCAD-compatible runtimePropertySet.
  - def restore_spatial_data(obj, ifcentity, ifcfile)
    doc: Restores placement and level metadata not covered by geometry import.
  - def remove_unused_properties(obj)
    doc: Remove IFC properties if they are not part of the current IFC class
  - def get_ifc_classes(obj, baseclass)
    doc: Returns the active-schema IFC classes that can reclassify this object.
  - def get_ifc_element(obj, ifcfile=None)
    doc: Returns the corresponding IFC element of an object
  - def has_representation(element)
    doc: Tells if an elements has an own representation
  - def filter_elements(elements, ifcfile, expand=True, spaces=False, assemblies=True)
    doc: Filter elements list of unwanted classes
  - def set_attribute(ifcfile, element, attribute, value)
    doc: Sets the value of an attribute of an IFC element
  - def set_colors(obj, colors)
    doc: Sets the given colors to an object
  - def get_body_context_ids(ifcfile)
  - def get_plan_contexts_ids(ifcfile)
  - def get_freecad_matrix(ios_matrix)
    doc: Converts an IfcOpenShell matrix tuple into a FreeCAD-compatible runtime matrix
  - def get_ios_matrix(m)
    doc: Converts a FreeCAD-compatible runtime placement or matrix into an IfcOpenShell matrix tuple
  - def get_scale(ifcfile)
    doc: Returns the scale factor to convert any file length to mm
  - def set_placement(obj)
    doc: Updates the internal IFC placement according to the object placement
  - def set_axis_points(obj, element, ifcfile)
    doc: Sets the points of an axis from placement and length
  - def save_ifc(obj, filepath=None)
    doc: Saves the linked IFC file of a project, but does not mark it as saved
  - def save(obj, filepath=None)
    doc: Saves the linked IFC file of a project and set its saved status
  - def aggregate(obj, parent, mode=None)
    doc: Takes any FreeCAD-compatible runtime object and aggregates it to an existing IFC object. Mode can be 'opening' to force-create a subtraction
  - def deaggregate(obj, parent)
    doc: Removes a FreeCAD-compatible runtime object form its parent
  - def get_ifctype(obj)
    doc: Returns a valid IFC type from an object
  - def get_subvolume(obj)
    doc: returns a subface + subvolume from a window object
  - def create_relationship(old_obj, obj, parent, element, ifcfile, mode=None)
    doc: Creates a relationship between an IFC object and a parent IFC object
  - def get_elem_attribs(ifcentity)
  - def migrate_schema(ifcfile, schema)
    doc: migrates a file to a new schema
  - def remove_ifc_element(obj, delete_obj=False)
    doc: removes the IFC data associated with an object. If delete_obj is True, the FreeCAD-compatible runtime object is also deleted
  - def get_orphan_elements(ifcfile)
    doc: returns a list of orphan products in an ifcfile
  - def get_group(project, name)
    doc: returns a group of the given type under the given IFC project. Creates it if needed
  - def load_orphans(obj)
    doc: loads orphan objects from the given project object
  - def remove_tree(objs)
    doc: Removes all given objects and their children, if not used by others
  - def recompute(children)
    doc: Temporary function to recompute objects. Some objects don't get their shape correctly at creation
```

MODULE src/Mod/BIM/nativeifc/ifc_viewproviders.py
functions:
  - def overlay(icon1, icon2)

    doc: Returns an icon for a view provider
classes:
  class ifc_vp_object
    doc: Base class for all blenderbim view providers
    methods:
      - def attach(self, vobj)
      - def getDisplayModes(self, obj)
      - def getDefaultDisplayMode(self)
      - def setDisplayMode(self, mode)
      - def onChanged(self, vobj, prop)
      - def updateData(self, obj, prop)
      - def getIcon(self)
      - def claimChildren(self)
      - def setupContextMenu(self, vobj, menu)
      - def hasChildren(self, obj)
        doc: Returns True if this IFC object can be decomposed
      - def expandChildren(self, obj=None)
        doc: Creates children of this object
      - def collapseChildren(self)
        doc: Collapses the children of this object
      - def getOwnChildren(self, obj)
        doc: Recursively gets the children only used by this object
      - def switchShape(self)
        doc: Switch this object between shape and coin
      - def switchCoin(self)
        doc: Switch this object between coin and no representation
      - def addGeometryProperties(self)
        doc: Adds geometry properties to this object
      - def addMaterial(self)
        doc: Adds a material to this object
      - def showTree(self)
        doc: Shows a dialog with a geometry tree for the object
      - def showProps(self)
        doc: Expands property sets
      - def canDragObjects(self)
        doc: Whether children can be removed by d&d
      - def canDropObjects(self)
        doc: Whether objects can be added here by d&d or drop only
      - def canDragObject(self, dragged_object)
        doc: Whether the given object can be removed by d&d
      - def canDropObject(self, incoming_object)
        doc: Whether the object can be dropped here by d&d or drop only
      - def dragObject(self, vobj, dragged_object)
        doc: Remove a child from the view provider by d&d
      - def dropObject(self, vobj, incoming_object)
        doc: Add an object to the view provider by d&d
      - def onDrop(self, incoming_object)
        doc: Delayed action to be taken when dropping an object
      - def activate(self)
        doc: Marks this container as active
      - def createGroup(self)
        doc: Creates a group under this object
      - def doubleClicked(self, vobj)
        doc: On double-click
      - def expandProperties(self, vobj)
        doc: Expands everything that needs to be expanded
      - def convertToType(self)
        doc: Converts this object to a type
  class ifc_vp_document(ifc_vp_object)
    doc: View provider for the IFC document object
    methods:
      - def getIcon(self)
      - def setupContextMenu(self, vobj, menu)
      - def save(self)
        doc: Saves the associated IFC file
      - def saveas(self)
        doc: Saves the associated IFC file to another file
      - def replace_file(self, obj, newfile)
        doc: Asks the user if the attached file path needs to be replaced
      - def schema_warning(self)
      - def diff(self)
  class ifc_vp_group
    doc: View provider for the IFC group object
    methods:
      - def attach(self, vobj)
      - def getIcon(self)
  class ifc_vp_material
    doc: View provider for the IFC group object
    methods:

        doc: Expands property sets

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/PathTestUtils.py
classes:
  class PathTestBase(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Base test class with some additional asserts.
    methods:
      - def assertRoughly(self, f1, f2, error=1e-05)
        doc: Verify that two float values are approximately the same.
      - def assertCoincide(self, pt1, pt2, error=0.0001)
        doc: Verify that two points coincide - roughly speaking.
      - def assertPlacement(self, p1, p2)
        doc: Verify that two placements are roughly identical.
      - def assertLine(self, edge, pt1, pt2)
        doc: Verify that edge is a line from pt1 to pt2.
      - def assertLines(self, edgs, tail, points)
        doc: Verify that the edges match the polygon resulting from points.
      - def assertArc(self, edge, pt1, pt2, direction='CW')
        doc: Verify that edge is an arc between pt1 and pt2 with the given direction.
      - def assertCircle(self, edge, pt, r)
        doc: Verify that edge is a circle at given location.
      - def assertCurve(self, edge, p1, p2, p3)
        doc: Verify that the edge goes through the given 3 points, representing start, mid and end point respectively.
      - def assertCylinderAt(self, solid, pt, r, h)
        doc: Verify that solid is a cylinder at the specified location.
      - def assertConeAt(self, solid, pt, r1, r2, h)
        doc: Verify that solid is a cone at the specified location.
      - def assertCommandEqual(self, c1, c2)
        doc: Verify that the 2 commands are equivalent.
      - def assertEqualLocale(self, s1, s2)
        doc: Verify that the 2 strings are equivalent, but converts eventual , into . for the first string that may be affected by locale.
      - def assertEdgeShapesMatch(self, e1, e2)
        doc: Verify that 2 edges have the same shape, regardless of orientation.
      - def assertPointsMatch(self, pts0, pts1)
        doc: Verify that two arrays of points are the same, including their order.
      - def assertSuccessfulRecompute(self, doc, *objs, msg=None)
        doc: Asserts that the given objects can be successfully recomputed.
  class PathTestWithAssets(PathTestBase)
    doc: A base class that creates an AssetManager, so tests can easily fetch test data. Examples: toolbit = self.assets.get("toolbit://ballend") toolbit = self.assets.get("toolbitshape://chamfer")
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestAreaOperations.py
classes:
  class TestAreaOperations(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Direct tests for Area boolean and offset operations exposed to Python.
    methods:
      - def create_square(self, x, y, size)
        doc: Helper: Create a square Area at position (x,y) with given size.
      - def create_circle(self, cx, cy, radius, segments=16)
        doc: Helper: Create a circular Area (approximated as polygon).
      - def assertAreaNear(self, area_obj, expected_area, tolerance=None, msg=None)
        doc: Helper: Assert area is within tolerance of expected value. Default tolerance is 1% of expected_area.
      - def test_union_overlapping_squares(self)
        doc: Test union of two overlapping squares.
      - def test_union_separate_squares(self)
        doc: Test union of two non-overlapping squares.
      - def test_intersect_overlapping_squares(self)
        doc: Test intersection of two overlapping squares.
      - def test_intersect_no_overlap(self)
        doc: Test intersection of two non-overlapping squares (edge case).
      - def test_subtract_hole(self)
        doc: Test subtracting small square from large square (creates hole).
      - def test_subtract_complete(self)
        doc: Test subtracting identical square.
      - def test_offset_inward(self)
        doc: Test offsetting a square inward.
      - def test_offset_outward(self)
        doc: Test offsetting a square outward (negative offset).
      - def test_offset_circle(self)
        doc: Test offsetting a circle.
      - def test_thicken(self)
        doc: Test thickening an area.
      - def test_reorder(self)
        doc: Test Reorder doesn't break the area.
      - def test_fitarcs(self)
        doc: Test FitArcs doesn't break the area.
      - def test_multiple_holes(self)
        doc: Test square with multiple holes.
      - def test_getcurves(self)
        doc: Test getCurves method returns curve list.
      - def test_nearestpoint(self)
        doc: Test NearestPoint method.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestCAMSanity.py
classes:
  class TestCAMSanity(PathTestBase)
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def test00(self)
        doc: Test no output location
      - def test010(self)
        doc: Test CAMSanity using a DummyImageBuilder. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd) and a DummyImageBuilder. When: CAMSanity is instantiated with a temp output file. Then: The instance is created with the correct filelocation and output_file attributes.
      - def test020(self)
        doc: Test erroneous output location
      - def test050(self)
        doc: Test base data
      - def test060(self)
        doc: Test design data
      - def test070(self)
        doc: Test tool data
      - def test080(self)
        doc: Test run data
      - def test090(self)
        doc: Test output data
      - def test100(self)
        doc: Test fixture data
      - def test110(self)
        doc: Test stock data
      - def test120(self)
        doc: Test squawk data
      - def test130(self)
        doc: Test tool data
      - def test140(self)
        doc: Test get_output_report returns a non-empty HTML string. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd) and a DummyImageBuilder. When: get_output_report() is called on a CAMSanity instance. Then: The result is a non-empty string containing HTML content.
      - def test200_zero_feedrate_squawk(self)
        doc: Zero feedrate on a tool controller should produce a WARNING squawk. Given: A job with one tool controller whose HorizFeed.Value is 0.0. When: _toolData() is called. Then: squawkData contains a WARNING entry mentioning "feedrate". Example: TC.HorizFeed.Value = 0.0 -> squawkData contains {"squawkType": "WARNING", "Note": "... feedrate ..."}
      - def test210_zero_spindle_squawk(self)
        doc: Zero spindle speed on a tool controller should produce a WARNING squawk. Given: A job with one tool controller whose SpindleSpeed is 0.0. When: _toolData() is called. Then: squawkData contains a WARNING entry mentioning "spindlespeed". Example: TC.SpindleSpeed = 0.0 -> squawkData contains {"squawkType": "WARNING", "Note": "... spindlespeed ..."}
      - def test220_unused_tool_controller_squawk(self)
        doc: A tool controller not used by any operation should produce a WARNING squawk. Given: A job with one tool controller and no operations referencing it. When: _toolData() is called. Then: squawkData contains a WARNING entry mentioning "not used". Example: job.Operations.Group = [] with one TC present -> squawkData contains {"squawkType": "WARNING", "Not
      - def test230_duplicate_tool_number_squawk(self)
        doc: Two tool controllers sharing the same tool number should produce a CAUTION squawk. Given: A job with two tool controllers assigned tool number 1 but different ShapeTypes. When: _toolData() is called. Then: squawkData contains a CAUTION entry about multiple tools sharing a number. Example: TC1.ToolNumber=1 ShapeType="endmill", TC2.ToolNumber=1 Shape
      - def test240_not_postprocessed_squawk(self)
        doc: A job with no prior post-processing should produce a squawk in _outputData(). Given: A job whose LastPostProcessOutput is an empty string. When: _outputData() is called. Then: squawkData contains an entry mentioning "post-process". Example: job.LastPostProcessOutput = "" -> squawkData contains {"Note": "The Job has not been post-processed"}
      - def test250_no_operations_squawk(self)
        doc: A job with no operations should produce a WARNING from _validate_job_structure(). Given: A job where the Operations attribute is None (falsy). When: _validate_job_structure() is called. Then: The returned list contains a WARNING squawk mentioning "operation". Example: job.Operations = None -> [{"squawkType": "WARNING", "Note": "No operations found i
      - def test260_no_base_geometry_squawk(self)
        doc: A job with no model/base geometry should produce a WARNING from _validate_job_structure(). Given: A job that has operations but whose Model attribute is None (falsy). When: _validate_job_structure() is called. Then: The returned list contains a WARNING squawk mentioning "model" or "base". Example: job.Operations = MagicMock(), job.Model = None -> [{
      - def test270_validate_for_postprocessing_returns_tuple(self)
        doc: validate_for_postprocessing() should return a (bool, list, list) tuple. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd). When: validate_for_postprocessing() is called on a CAMSanity instance. Then: - Returns a 3-tuple: (has_critical, all_squawks, critical_squawks) - has_critical is a bool equal to (len(critical_squawks) > 0) - all_squawks and critical_squaw
      - def test280_validate_job_static(self)
        doc: validate_job() static method should return (all_squawks, critical_squawks) without images. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd). When: CAMSanity.validate_job(job) is called as a static method. Then: - Returns a 2-tuple: (all_squawks, critical_squawks) - Both are lists of squawk dicts - All critical squawks have squawkType "WARNING" or "CAUTION" -
      - def test300_get_all_squawks_completeness(self)
        doc: get_all_squawks() should collect squawks from all validation sections. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd) with a DummyImageBuilder. When: get_all_squawks() is called on a CAMSanity instance. Then: - Returns a 2-tuple: (all_squawks, critical_squawks) - critical_squawks contains only WARNING or CAUTION squawks - Every item in critical_squawks is 
      - def test310_validate_job_end_to_end(self)
        doc: validate_job() end-to-end: works without a pre-existing output path. Given: A valid CAM job (boxtest.fcstd). When: CAMSanity.validate_job(job) is called with no other setup. Then: - Returns (all_squawks, critical_squawks) - All critical squawks are WARNING or CAUTION - All critical squawks appear in all_squawks - squawkType and Note keys are presen
      - def test320_postprocessor_sanity_checks_integration(self)
        doc: Postprocessor sanity checks: collected and integrated into validation. Given: A job with a machine that has postprocessor sanity checks. When: CAMSanity.validate_job() is called. Then: Postprocessor squawks appear in validation results.
      - def test321_postprocessor_sanity_checks_error_handling(self)
        doc: Postprocessor sanity checks: graceful handling of exceptions. Given: A postprocessor that raises an exception in get_sanity_checks(). When: CAMSanity.validate_job() is called. Then: Exception is caught and validation continues.
      - def test322_postprocessor_sanity_checks_no_machine(self)
        doc: Postprocessor sanity checks: job without machine. Given: A job with no machine assigned. When: CAMSanity.validate_job() is called. Then: No postprocessor checks are performed, but validation continues.
      - def test323_generic_plasma_sanity_checks_integration(self)
        doc: GenericPlasma postprocessor sanity checks: verify get_sanity_checks is called. Given: A job with a machine whose postprocessor_file_name is "generic_plasma". When: CAMSanity.validate_job() is called. Then: GenericPlasma get_sanity_checks() method is called and test squawk appears.
```

MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestCentroidLegacyPost.py
classes:
  class TestCentroidLegacyPost(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test suite for the Centroid legacy postprocessor. This class verifies the functionality of the centroid_legacy_post postprocessor, which generates G-code for Centroid CNC controllers. Tests cover: - Output generation with various options (header, comments, editor suppression) - Command formatting and coordinate precision control - Line numbering - Preamble and postamble generation - Unit conversion (metric/imperial) - Modal command suppression - Axis modal (coordinate suppression for unchanged a
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def test000(self)
        doc: Test Output Generation. Empty path. Produces only the preamble and postable.
      - def test010(self)
        doc: Test command Generation. Test Precision
      - def test020(self)
        doc: Test Line Numbers
      - def test030(self)
        doc: Test Pre-amble
      - def test040(self)
        doc: Test Post-amble
      - def test050(self)
        doc: Test inches
      - def test060(self)
        doc: Test test modal Suppress the command name if the same as previous
      - def test070(self)
        doc: Test axis modal Suppress the axis coordinate if the same as previous
      - def test080(self)
        doc: Test tool change
      - def test090(self)
        doc: Test comment
```
