```text
classes:
  class TestDrillCycleExpander(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Test the DrillCycleExpander class with Path.Command objects.
    methods:
      - def test_00_error_r_less_than_z(self)
        doc: Test error condition when R < Z.
      - def test_01_modal_retract_mode(self)
        doc: Test that G98/G99 modal commands are processed and filtered out
      - def test_02_position_tracking(self)
        doc: Test that position is tracked correctly
      - def test_03_expand_path_object(self)
        doc: Test expanding a complete Path object
      - def test_04_g81_with_g98(self)
        doc: Test 1: Basic G81 (simple drill) with G98 retract
      - def test_05_g81_with_g99(self)
        doc: Test 2: G81 with G99 retract (retract to R instead of initial Z)
      - def test_06_g82(self)
        doc: Test 3: G82 (drill with dwell)
      - def test_07_g83(self)
        doc: Test 4: G83 (peck drill) with 3 pecks
      - def test_08_preliminary_moves(self)
        doc: Test preliminary motion according to LinuxCNC specification
      - def test_09_preliminary_moves_z_below_r(self)
        doc: Test preliminary motion when Z starts below R
      - def test_10_g73(self)
        doc: Test 6: G73 (chip breaking drill) with small retracts
      - def test_11_cycle_multiple_positions(self)
        doc: Test 5: Modal cycle with multiple positions (G81)
```

```text
      - def test_optimize_multi_axis_no_collapse(self)

        doc: Test collapsing rapid moves within rotary axis group (A,B,C).
      - def test_optimize_mixed_axes_no_collapse(self)
        doc: Test that mixed linear/rotary changes don't collapse.
```

```text
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def test00_property_schema(self)
        doc: Test that GenericPlasma has the correct property schema with plasma-specific properties. INPUT: - Function: get_property_schema() - Parameters: None - Input data: GenericPlasma postprocessor instance EXPECTED OUTPUT: - Returns schema with pierce_delay, cooling_delay, marking_delay, torch_zaxis_control, force_rapid_feeds - Properties should have cor
      - def test01_pierce_delay_injection(self)
        doc: Test that pierce delay is correctly injected after M3/M4 commands. INPUT: - Function: _inject_pierce_delay() - Parameters: postables with M3 command - Input data: Path containing M3 torch ignition command EXPECTED OUTPUT: - G4 dwell command inserted after M3 with correct P parameter - Delay duration matches pierce_delay property value in seconds - 
      - def test02_cooling_delay_injection(self)
        doc: Test that cooling delay is correctly injected after M5 commands. INPUT: - Function: _inject_cooling_delay() - Parameters: postables with M5 command - Input data: Path containing M5 torch extinguish command EXPECTED OUTPUT: - G4 dwell command inserted after M5 with correct P parameter - Delay duration matches cooling_delay property value in seconds 
      - def test03_torch_z_axis_control_enabled(self)
        doc: Test torch Z-axis control when enabled - M3/M5 inserted based on Z movement. INPUT: - Function: _inject_torch_control() - Parameters: postables with Z movements - Input data: Path with Z- movement to cut height, then Z+ retraction EXPECTED OUTPUT: - M3 inserted before Z- movement when torch_zaxis_control=True - M5 inserted after Z+ movement when to
      - def test04_torch_z_axis_control_disabled(self)
        doc: Test that torch Z-axis control is disabled when property is False. INPUT: - Function: _inject_torch_control() - Parameters: postables with Z movements - Input data: Path with Z movements, torch_zaxis_control=False EXPECTED OUTPUT: - No M3/M5 commands automatically inserted based on Z movement - Original path commands pass through unchanged - This a
      - def test05_mark_entry_only_mode(self)
        doc: Test mark entry only mode - only first entry point is marked. INPUT: - Function: _inject_mark_entry_only() - Parameters: postables with multiple Z- movements - Input data: Path with multiple cutting passes, mark_entry_only=True EXPECTED OUTPUT: - Only first Z- movement to cut height is processed with torch mark - Subsequent Z- movements are skipped
      - def test06_force_rapid_feeds(self)
        doc: Test force rapid feeds functionality - removes F parameters from movement commands. INPUT: - Function: _force_rapid_feeds() - Parameters: postables with movement commands containing F parameters - Input data: Path with G0/G1/G2/G3 commands having feed rates EXPECTED OUTPUT: - All F parameters removed from movement commands - Non-movement commands u
      - def test07_common_property_overrides(self)
        doc: Test that GenericPlasma correctly overrides common postprocessor properties. INPUT: - Function: get_common_property_schema() - Parameters: None - Input data: GenericPlasma postprocessor instance EXPECTED OUTPUT: - file_extension defaults to "nc" - supports_tool_radius_compensation defaults to True - preamble and postamble have plasma-specific defau
      - def test08_zero_delay_values(self)
        doc: Test that zero or negative delay values don't inject G4 commands. INPUT: - Function: _inject_pierce_delay() and _inject_cooling_delay() - Parameters: postables with M3/M5 commands - Input data: pierce_delay=0, cooling_delay=-100 EXPECTED OUTPUT: - No G4 commands injected when delay values are <= 0 - M3/M5 commands pass through unchanged - This prev
      - def test_actual_machine(self)
        doc: Our specific `postprocessor_properties` were seen
```

```text
classes:
  class TestGenericPost(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test the generic postprocessor.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.

        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def test000(self)
        doc: Test Output Generation. Empty path. Produces only the preamble and postable.
      - def test010(self)
        doc: Test command Generation. Test Precision
      - def test020(self)
        doc: Test Line Numbers
      - def test030(self)
        doc: Test Pre-amble
      - def test040(self)

        doc: Test tool change

        doc: Test comment
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestLinuxCNCPost.py
classes:
  class TestLinuxCNCPost(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test LinuxCNC-specific features of the linuxcnc_post.py postprocessor. This test suite focuses on LinuxCNC-specific functionality such as path blending modes. Generic postprocessor functionality is tested in TestGenericPost.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def test_blend_mode_exact_path(self)
        doc: Test EXACT_PATH blend mode outputs G61.
      - def test_blend_mode_exact_stop(self)
        doc: Test EXACT_STOP blend mode outputs G61.1.
      - def test_blend_mode_blend_default(self)
        doc: Test BLEND mode with default tolerance (0) outputs G64.
      - def test_blend_mode_blend_with_tolerance(self)
        doc: Test BLEND mode with tolerance outputs G64 P<tolerance>.
      - def test_blend_mode_blend_with_custom_tolerance(self)
        doc: Test BLEND mode with custom tolerance value.
      - def test_blend_mode_in_preamble_position(self)
        doc: Test that blend mode command appears in correct position in preamble.
      - def test_blend_tolerance_zero_equals_no_tolerance(self)
        doc: Test that blend tolerance of 0 outputs G64 without P parameter.
      - def test_blend_interaction_with_preamble_argument(self)
        doc: Test blend mode appears after units command in preamble.
      - def test_rigid_tapping_g84_basic(self)
        doc: Test G84 rigid tapping conversion to G33.1 sequence. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 F1.5 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.5000 Z-10.0000 M4 G33.1 K1.5000 Z0.0000 M3
      - def test_rigid_tapping_g74_basic(self)
        doc: Test G74 rigid tapping conversion to G33.1 sequence. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G74 Z-10 F1.5 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.5000 Z-10.0000 M3 G33.1 K1.5000 Z0.0000 M4
      - def test_rigid_tapping_pitch_conversion(self)
        doc: Test pitch (F) parameter conversion to K parameter. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 F1.25 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.2500 Z-10.0000
      - def test_rigid_tapping_with_retract_height(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping with retract height (R parameter). Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-15 R5 F1.25 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.2500 Z-15.0000 M4 G33.1 K1.2500 Z5.0000 M3
      - def test_rigid_tapping_with_coordinates(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping preserves X and Y coordinates. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 X10 Y20 Z-10 F1.5 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.5000 X10.0000 Y20.0000 Z-10.0000
      - def test_rigid_tapping_with_dwell(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping with dwell (P parameter). Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 F1.5 P0.5 (rigid=True) AFTER: G33.1 K1.5000 Z-10.0000 M5 G04 P0.50 M4 G33.1 K1.5000 Z0.0000 M3
      - def test_rigid_tapping_imperial_units(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping unit conversion to imperial. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 F1.5 (rigid=True) in imperial units AFTER: G33.1 K0.0591 Z-0.3937
      - def test_rigid_tapping_block_delete(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping with block delete annotation. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 F1.5 (rigid=True, blockdelete=True) AFTER: /G33.1 K1.5000 Z-10.0000 /M4 /G33.1 K1.5000 Z0.0000 /M3
      - def test_rigid_tapping_missing_pitch_fallback(self)
        doc: Test rigid tapping falls back to parent when pitch missing. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G84 Z-10 (rigid=True, no F parameter) AFTER: [standard G84 conversion, not G33.1]
      - def test_rigid_tapping_suppresses_g80(self)
        doc: Test G80 is suppressed for rigid tapping operations. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G80 (operation=tapping, rigid=True) AFTER: None (command suppressed)
      - def test_rigid_tapping_suppresses_g98(self)
        doc: Test G98 is suppressed for rigid tapping operations. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G98 (operation=tapping, rigid=True) AFTER: None (command suppressed)
      - def test_rigid_tapping_suppresses_g99(self)
        doc: Test G99 is suppressed for rigid tapping operations. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G99 (operation=tapping, rigid=True) AFTER: None (command suppressed)
      - def test_non_rigid_tapping_not_suppressed(self)
        doc: Test G80/G98/G99 are not suppressed for non-rigid tapping. Expected behavior: BEFORE: G80 (operation=tapping, rigid=False) AFTER: G80 (command not suppressed)
      - def test_schema_defaults_applied_for_sparse_config(self)
        doc: Test that LinuxCNC schema defaults are applied when postprocessor_properties is sparse (simulating a real .fcm file that only stores user-changed values). LinuxCNC overrides get_common_property_schema() to set: preamble = "G17 G54 G40 G49 G80 G90" postamble = "M05\nG17 G54 G90 G80 G40\nM2" safetyblock = "G40 G49 G80" INPUT: - LinuxCNC postprocessor
      - def test_schema_defaults_blocks_appear_in_output(self)
        doc: Test that LinuxCNC schema default blocks actually appear in the G-code output when the .fcm file omits them. This simulates the real-world bug: user's .fcm has only file_extension and rotary move properties, but the machine editor shows preamble, postamble, and safetyblock with their schema defaults. After postprocessing, those blocks must appear i
```

```text
classes:
  class TestMachineDataclass(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test the unified Machine dataclass
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: Set up test fixtures
      - def test_default_initialization(self)

      - def test_configuration_units_property(self)

  class TestOutputOptions(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test OutputOptions dataclass
    methods:

        doc: Test OutputOptions equality comparison
  class TestProcessingOptions(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test ProcessingOptions dataclass

        doc: Test ProcessingOptions equality comparison
  class TestToolhead(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test Toolhead dataclass
    methods:

        doc: Test Toolhead initialization with defaults
      - def test_toolhead_serialization(self)
        doc: Test to_dict and from_dict
  class TestMachineFactory(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test MachineFactory class for loading/saving configurations
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: Set up test fixtures with temporary directory
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: Clean up temporary directory
      - def test_set_and_get_config_directory(self)
        doc: Test setting and getting configuration directory
      - def test_save_and_load_configuration(self)
        doc: Test saving and loading a machine configuration
      - def test_save_configuration_auto_filename(self)
        doc: Test saving with automatic filename generation
      - def test_load_nonexistent_file(self)
        doc: Test loading a file that doesn't exist
      - def test_create_default_machine_data(self)
        doc: Test creating default machine data dictionary
      - def test_list_configuration_files(self)
        doc: Test listing available configuration files
      - def test_list_configurations(self)
        doc: Test listing configuration names
      - def test_delete_configuration(self)
        doc: Test deleting a configuration file
      - def test_get_builtin_config(self)
        doc: Test getting built-in machine configurations
      - def test_get_builtin_config_invalid_type(self)
        doc: Test getting built-in config with invalid type
      - def test_serialization_roundtrip(self)
        doc: Test full serialization roundtrip with complex machine
      - def test_register_addon_machine_dir(self)
        doc: register_addon_machine_dir() adds path to _addon_machine_dirs without duplicates. Given: a temporary directory not yet registered When: register_addon_machine_dir() is called twice with the same path Then: _addon_machine_dirs contains it exactly once
      - def test_list_addon_templates(self)
        doc: list_addon_templates() returns machines from registered addon dirs. Given: a registered addon directory containing one .fcm file with name "Addon Machine" When: list_addon_templates() is called Then: "Addon Machine" appears in the returned display names
      - def test_get_machine_from_addon(self)
        doc: get_machine() can load a machine from a registered addon directory. Given: a registered addon directory containing a valid .fcm file for "Addon Test Machine" When: get_machine("Addon Test Machine") is called Then: a Machine object with name "Addon Test Machine" is returned
      - def test_list_configurations_includes_addon(self)
        doc: list_configurations() includes addon machine names. Given: a registered addon directory with a machine named "Community Mill" When: list_configurations() is called Then: "Community Mill" appears in the returned list
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestMarlinPost.py
classes:
  class TestMarlinPost(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test Marlin-specific features of the marlin_post.py postprocessor. The only Marlin-specific behaviour is G4 dwell P->S conversion. All other functionality is inherited from the base PostProcessor.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def test_dwell_p_converted_to_s(self)
        doc: G4 P<seconds> should be output as G4 S<seconds> for Marlin.
      - def test_dwell_s_passthrough(self)
        doc: G4 S<seconds> should pass through unchanged.
      - def test_dwell_fractional_seconds(self)
        doc: G4 with fractional seconds should preserve precision.
      - def test_dwell_in_full_export(self)
        doc: G4 dwell in full export2 pipeline should use S not P.
      - def test_spindle_wait_uses_s(self)
        doc: Spindle wait (G4 after M3) should use S parameter for Marlin.
      - def test_default_file_extension(self)
        doc: Marlin default file extension should be 'gcode'.
      - def test_default_preamble(self)
        doc: Marlin default preamble should include G90, G21, G17.
      - def test_default_postamble(self)
        doc: Marlin default postamble should be M5.
      - def test_no_custom_properties(self)
        doc: Marlin should have no custom properties beyond common ones.
      - def test_tool_change_disabled(self)
        doc: Marlin should have tool change disabled by default.
      - def test_tlo_disabled(self)
        doc: Marlin should have tool length offset disabled by default.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestOpenSBPPost.py
functions:
  - def PPMachineState(init: dict)
classes:
  class TestOpenSBPPost(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test OpenSBP-specific features of the opensbp_post.py postprocessor. OpenSBP uses native ShopBot commands instead of standard G-code. These tests verify command conversion for the major command categories.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def any_line_startswith(self, gcode, lines)
        doc: Test if it has at least one
      - def no_unnumbered(self, gcode, lines)
        doc: Test if it has at least one, and all one of the `gcode` lines are numbered
      - def test_default_file_extension(self)
        doc: Default output file extension is 'sbp'.
      - def test_rapid_x(self)
        doc: G0 X-only move
      - def test_rapid_y(self)
        doc: G0 Y-only
      - def test_rapid_z(self)
        doc: G0 Z-only
      - def test_rapid_xy(self)
        doc: G0 XY move
      - def test_rapid_xyz(self)
        doc: G0 XYZ move
      - def test_rapid_xz(self)
        doc: G0 XZ move
      - def test_rapid_yz(self)
        doc: G0 YZ move
      - def test_linear_x(self)
        doc: G1 X-only
      - def test_linear_y(self)
        doc: G1 Y-only
      - def test_linear_z_only_produces_mz(self)
        doc: G1 Z-only
      - def test_linear_xy(self)
        doc: G1 XY move
      - def test_linear_xyz(self)
        doc: G1 XYZ move
      - def test_linear_xz(self)
        doc: G1 XZ move
      - def test_linear_with_feedrate(self)
        doc: G1 with F parameter F in Path.Command is in FreeCAD-compatible runtime base units: mm/sec. ShopBot g-code expects {unit}/min - time-unit conversion needed.
      - def test_rapid_with_feedrate_outputs(self)
        doc: G0 with F parameter outputs
      - def test_speed_value_imperial_conversion(self)
        doc: F value is divided by 25.4 for imperial output. F=25.4*2 mm/sec -> 120.0 in/min for ShopBot imperial output.
      - def test_js_speed_value_metric_passthrough(self)
        doc: Jog speed to mm/min
      - def test_helical_arc_includes_plunge(self)
        doc: Helical arc (G2/G3 with Z) adds a plunge parameter as the 9th field. BEFORE: G2 X10 Y0 I5 J0 Z-5 (current Z=0) AFTER: CG,,10.0000,0.0000,5.0000,0.0000,L,1,5.0000
      - def test_arc_no_gcode_in_output(self)
        doc: Helix output must not contain G2 or G3.
      - def test_helix_required_params(self)
        doc: Helix requires all params, either explicit or modal
      - def test_helix_bad_z(self)
        doc: Helix in negative direction is ok
      - def test_helix_units(self)
        doc: Helix has to use units
      - def test_tool_change_manual_includes_pause(self)
        doc: Manual tool change (no ATC) emits PAUSE for operator intervention. BEFORE: M6 T2 AFTER: 'Manual tool change to T2 >&ToolName=2 >&Tool=2 >PAUSE
      - def test_tool_change_automatic_no_pause(self)
        doc: Automatic tool change (ATC enabled) does not emit PAUSE. BEFORE: M6 T3 AFTER: >&ToolName=3 >&Tool=3
      - def test_tool_change_sets_tool_name(self)
        doc: Tool change always sets >&ToolName variable.
      - def test_spindle_on_manual_emits_pause(self)
        doc: M3 without automatic spindle control emits manual prompt and PAUSE. BEFORE: M3 S18000 AFTER: 'Set spindle to 18000 RPM and start manually >PAUSE
      - def test_spindle_on_automatic_emits_tr(self)
        doc: M3 with automatic spindle control emits TR (speed), C6 (on), and PAUSE (wait). BEFORE: M3 S18000 AFTER: >TR,18000 >PAUSE 2
      - def test_spindle_off_manual_emits_pause(self)
        doc: M5 without automatic spindle emits manual prompt and PAUSE. BEFORE: M5 AFTER: 'Turn spindle OFF manually >PAUSE
      - def test_spindle_off_automatic_emits_tr0(self)
        doc: M5 with automatic spindle control emits TR,0. BEFORE: M5 AFTER: TR,0
      - def test_spindle_no_gcode_in_output(self)
        doc: Spindle output must not contain M3, M4, or M5.
      - def test_fixture_commands_suppressed(self)
        doc: G54-G59 fixture offsets are suppressed (return None). OpenSBP has no work coordinate system concept. Tolerate G54, others are illegal
      - def test_linear_move_imperial_conversion(self)
        doc: With imperial output units, coordinate values are divided by 25.4. BEFORE: G1 X25.4 Y50.8 (metric input)
      - def test_rapid_move_imperial_conversion(self)
        doc: With imperial output, rapid move coordinates are divided by 25.4.
      - def test_full_export_no_crash(self)
        doc: export2() doesn't crash Turn on all options for all code-paths
      - def test_comments(self)
      - def test_line_numbering(self)
        doc: Native shopbot must not get line-numbers
      - def test_line_numbering2(self)
        doc: Certain pass-through MUST be line-numbered
      - def test_gcode_passthrough(self)
      - def test_converted_to_native(self)
      - def test_full_export_duplicates(self)
        doc: G01 is subject to duplicate removal, but CG is not (relative Z) And, should remove duplicates even if different line-numbers
      - def test_G0_and_G1(self)
        doc: G0 and G1 are passed through
      - def test_G4(self)
        doc: G4 passed through
      - def test_G2_G3_noz(self)
        doc: G2 and G3 are passed through if no delta Z
      - def test_G20_G21(self)
        doc: G20/G21 are passed through
      - def test_M0_and_M1(self)
        doc: stop lines are passed through
      - def test_M8_and_M9(self)
        doc: coolant control is passed through, but line-numbered
      - def test_program_modal_numbered(self)
        doc: duplicate commands that leave Z as the head need to be numbered
      - def test_G82(self)
        doc: for the dwell
      - def test_drill_cycles_translated(self)
        doc: by default, expanded
      - def test_empty_path(self)
      - def test_convert_probe_open(self)
        doc: Test the _convert_probe_open functions
      - def test_convert_probe(self)
        doc: Test the _convert_probe_convert functions
      - def test_convert_probe_close(self)
        doc: Test the _convert_probe_convert_close functions
      - def test_todo(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathAdaptive.py
functions:
  - def getPathBoundaries(paths, zLevels)
    doc: getPathBoundaries(paths, zLevels): Takes the list of paths and list of Z depths of interest, and finds the bounding box of the paths at each depth. A dictionary of depth: {"min": (x,y), "max": (x,y)} entries is returned. NOTE: You'd think that using Path.BoundBox would give us what we want, but... no, for whatever reason it appears to always extend to (0,0,0)
  - def setDepthsAndHeights(op, strDep=20.0, finDep=0.0)
    doc: setDepthsAndHeights(op, strDep=20.0, finDep=0.0)... Sets default depths and heights for `op` passed to it
  - def getGcodeMoves(cmdList, includeRapids=True, includeLines=True, includeArcs=True)
    doc: getGcodeMoves(cmdList, includeRapids=True, includeLines=True, includeArcs=True)... Accepts command dict and returns point string coordinate.
  - def pathOriginatesInBox(cmd, minPoint, maxPoint)
classes:
  class TestPathAdaptive(PathTestBase)
    doc: Unit tests for the Adaptive operation.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def initClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def checkAdaptiveErrors(self, adaptiveOutput)
        doc: Check error flags in C++ AdaptiveOutput object.
      - def testClearInside(self)
        doc: testClearInside() Test C++ Adaptive2d clearing inside a simple rectangle.
      - def testClearInsideWithRestMachining(self)
        doc: testClearInsideWithRestMachining() Test C++ Adaptive2d rest machining with pre-cleared area.
      - def testClearOutside(self)
        doc: testClearOutside() Test C++ Adaptive2d clearing outside a simple rectangle.
      - def testProfilingInside(self)
        doc: testProfilingInside() Test C++ Adaptive2d profiling inside a rectangle.
      - def testProfilingOutside(self)
        doc: testProfilingOutside() Test C++ Adaptive2d profiling outside a rectangle.
      - def testFaceSingleSimple(self)
        doc: testFaceSingleSimple() Verify path generated on Face3.
      - def testFacesMergedDifferentZ(self)
        doc: testFacesMergedDifferentZ() Verify path generated on adjacent, combined Face3 and Face10. The Z heights are different.
      - def testFacesMergedDifferentZUseOutline(self)
        doc: testFacesMergedDifferentZUseOutline() Verify path generated on adjacent, combined Face3 and Face10. The Z heights are different.
      - def testOutlineDifferentZDiscontinuousEdges(self)
        doc: testOutlineDifferentZDiscontinuous() Verify path generated non-closed edges with differing Z-heights that are closed with Z=1 projection: "Edge9", "Edge2", "Edge8", "Edge15", "Edge30", "Edge31", "Edge29", "Edge19".
      - def testOutlineDifferentZContinuousEdges(self)
        doc: test05() Verify path generated closed wire with differing Z-heights: "Edge13", "Edge7", "Edge9", "Edge2", "Edge8", "Edge15", "Edge30", "Edge31", "Edge29", "Edge19".
      - def testOutlineWithCutout(self)
        doc: testOutlineWithCutout() Verify path generated with outer and inner edge loops at same Z height: "Edge15", "Edge30", "Edge31", "Edge29", "Edge19", "Edge18", "Edge35", "Edge32", "Edge34", "Edge33".
      - def testFaceWithCutout(self)
        doc: testFaceWithCutout() Verify path generated on donut-shaped Face10.
      - def testModelStockAwareness(self)
        doc: testModelStockAwareness() Tests stock awareness- avoids cutting into the model regardless of bounding box selected.
      - def testZStockToLeave(self)
        doc: testZStockToLeave() Tests Z stock to leave- with 1mm Z stock to leave, machining at the top of the model should not touch the top model face
      - def testFullModelAdaptiveRoughing(self)
        doc: testFullModelAdaptiveRoughing() Tests full roughing- should machine entire model with no inputs
      - def testStockLimitsAwareness(self)
        doc: testStockLimitsAwareness() Tests stock handling- should rough full model, but not cut air excessively where there's not stock
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathFacingGenerator.py
classes:
  class TestPathFacingGenerator(PathTestBase)
    doc: Test facing generator.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: Set up test fixtures.
      - def test_spiral_reverse_toggles_start_corner_climb(self)
        doc: Spiral reverse toggles the starting corner while keeping winding (climb).
      - def test_spiral_reverse_toggles_start_corner_conventional(self)
        doc: Spiral reverse toggles the starting corner while keeping winding (conventional).
      - def test_directional_strategy_basic(self)
        doc: Test directional strategy basic functionality.
      - def test_directional_reverse_changes_start(self)
        doc: Directional reverse should start from the opposite side.
      - def test_zigzag_reverse_flips_first_pass_direction_climb(self)
        doc: Zigzag reverse should flip the first pass direction while preserving alternation (climb).
      - def test_zigzag_reverse_flips_first_pass_direction_conventional(self)
        doc: Zigzag reverse should flip the first pass direction while preserving alternation (conventional).
      - def test_zigzag_reverse_and_milling_combinations(self)
        doc: Test all four combinations of reverse and milling_direction for zigzag.
      - def test_directional_reverse_and_milling_combinations(self)
        doc: Test all four combinations of reverse and milling_direction for directional.
      - def test_bidirectional_reverse_and_milling_combinations(self)
        doc: Test all four combinations of reverse and milling_direction for bidirectional.
      - def test_directional_climb_vs_conventional(self)
        doc: Test directional with different milling directions.
      - def test_directional_retract_height(self)
        doc: Test retract height functionality in directional.
      - def test_zigzag_strategy_basic(self)
        doc: Test zigzag strategy basic functionality.
      - def test_zigzag_alternating_direction(self)
        doc: Test that zigzag alternates cutting direction.
      - def test_zigzag_with_retract_height(self)
        doc: Test zigzag with retract height.
      - def test_zigzag_arc_links(self)
        doc: Test zigzag arc linking generates proper G2/G3 commands.
      - def test_zigzag_arc_vs_straight_link_modes(self)
        doc: Test that arc and straight link modes produce different but valid toolpaths.
      - def test_zigzag_milling_direction(self)
        doc: Test zigzag with different milling directions.
      - def test_get_angled_polygon_zero_degrees(self)
        doc: Test get_angled_polygon with 0 degree rotation.
      - def test_get_angled_polygon_45_degrees(self)
        doc: Test get_angled_polygon with 45 degree rotation.
      - def test_analyze_rectangle_axis_aligned(self)
        doc: Test polygon geometry extraction with axis-aligned rectangle.
      - def test_analyze_rectangle_short_preference(self)
        doc: Test edge selection with short axis preference.
      - def test_analyze_rectangle_invalid_polygon(self)
        doc: Test edge selection with invalid polygon.
      - def test_spiral_conventional_milling(self)
        doc: Test spiral strategy with conventional milling direction.
      - def test_bidirectional_basic(self)
        doc: Test basic bidirectional strategy functionality.
      - def test_bidirectional_climb_milling(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy with climb milling direction.
      - def test_bidirectional_conventional_milling(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy with conventional milling direction.
      - def test_bidirectional_with_retract_height(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy - rapids stay at cutting height.
      - def test_bidirectional_alternating_positions(self)
        doc: Test that bidirectional strategy alternates between bottom and top positions.
      - def test_bidirectional_axis_preference_long(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy with angle for long axis.
      - def test_bidirectional_axis_preference_short(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy with angle for short axis.
      - def test_bidirectional_with_pass_extension(self)
        doc: Test bidirectional strategy with pass extension parameter.
      - def test_spiral_layer_calculation(self)
        doc: Test that spiral generates appropriate number of layers.
      - def test_spiral_milling_direction(self)
        doc: Test spiral with different milling directions.
      - def test_spiral_centered_on_origin(self)
        doc: Test spiral with rectangle centered on origin to debug overlapping passes.
      - def test_spiral_axis_preference_variations(self)
        doc: Test spiral with different axis preferences and milling directions.
      - def test_spiral_angled_rectangle(self)
        doc: Test spiral with angled rectangle to verify it follows polygon shape, not bounding box.
      - def test_spiral_continuous_cutting(self)
        doc: Test that spiral maintains continuous cutting motion throughout.
      - def test_spiral_rectangular_polygon(self)
        doc: Test spiral with rectangular (non-square) polygon.
      - def test_spiral_basic(self)
        doc: Test spiral basic functionality.
      - def test_spiral_continuous_pattern(self)
        doc: Test that spiral generates a continuous inward pattern without diagonal jumps.
      - def test_project_bounds_rectangular_wire(self)
        doc: project_bounds returns correct extents for a rectangular wire.
      - def test_project_bounds_circular_wire(self)
        doc: project_bounds must return the full circular extent, not just seam vertices.
      - def test_generate_t_values_circular_wire(self)
        doc: generate_t_values must produce multiple step positions spanning the circular wire.
      - def test_zigzag_circular_wire(self)
        doc: Zigzag generator must produce a valid toolpath for a circular boundary wire.
      - def test_bidirectional_circular_wire(self)
        doc: Bidirectional generator must produce a valid toolpath for a circular boundary wire.
      - def test_directional_circular_wire(self)
        doc: Directional generator must produce a valid toolpath for a circular boundary wire.
      - def test_spiral_circular_wire_via_angled_polygon(self)
        doc: Spiral strategy requires a rectangular boundary. When the stock has curved edges (e.g. cylindrical stock) MillFacing converts the boundary to a bounding rectangle via get_angled_polygon before calling any strategy. Given: A circular wire (radius 5, centred at 5,5). Action: Convert it to a rectangle with get_angled_polygon(wire, 0), then pass the re
      - def test_spiral_spline_wire_via_angled_polygon(self)
        doc: Same boundary-rectification path but with a spline wire. Given: A closed B-spline wire (irregular curved shape). Action: Convert via get_angled_polygon(wire, 0), pass to spiral(). Expect: - 4-edge closed rectangle that contains the spline bounding box. - spiral() produces a valid toolpath.
      - def test_spiral_circular_wire_angled_45(self)
        doc: Boundary rectification at a non-zero angle still yields a valid rectangular boundary for spiral. Given: A circular wire, angle = 45 degrees. Action: Convert via get_angled_polygon(wire, 45), pass to spiral(). Expect: - 4-edge closed rectangle. - spiral() at 45 degrees produces a valid toolpath.
```

```text
        doc: Verify Helix generates proper holes from model

        doc: Verify Helix generates proper holes for rotated clone base model
      - def testPathDirection(self)
        doc: Verify that the generated paths obeys the given parameters
      - def testRecomputeHelixFromV021(self)
        doc: Verify that we can still open and recompute a Helix created with older FreeCAD-compatible runtime
      - def assertPathDirection(self, path, expected_direction, msg=None)
        doc: Asserts that the given path goes into the expected direction. For the general case we'd need to check the sign of the second derivative, but as we know we work on a helix here, we can take a short cut and just look at the G2/G3 arc commands.
```

```text
classes:
  class TestPathLog(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Some basic tests for the logging framework.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def callerFile(self)
      - def callerLine(self)
      - def callerFunc(self)
      - def test00(self)
        doc: Check for proper module extraction.
      - def test01(self)
        doc: Check for proper function extraction.
      - def test10(self)
        doc: Verify default log levels is NOTICE.
      - def test11(self)
        doc: Verify setting global log level.
      - def test12(self)
        doc: Verify setting module log level.
      - def test13(self)
        doc: Verify setting other modul's log level doesn't change this one's.
      - def test14(self)
        doc: Verify resetting log level for module falls back to global level.
      - def test20(self)
        doc: Verify debug logs aren't logged by default.
      - def test21(self)
        doc: Verify debug logs are logged if log level is set to DEBUG.
      - def test30(self)
        doc: Verify log level ERROR.
      - def test31(self)
        doc: Verify log level WARNING.
      - def test32(self)
        doc: Verify log level NOTICE.
      - def test33(self)
        doc: Verify log level INFO.
      - def test34(self)
        doc: Verify log level DEBUG.
      - def test50(self)
        doc: Verify no tracking by default.
      - def test51(self)
        doc: Verify enabling tracking for module results in tracking.
      - def test52(self)
        doc: Verify untracking stops tracking.
      - def test53(self)
        doc: Verify trackAllModules works correctly.
      - def test60(self)
        doc: Verify track handles no argument.
      - def test61(self)
        doc: Verify track handles arbitrary argument types correctly.
      - def testzz(self)
        doc: Restoring environment after tests.
```

```text
  class TestGetClearedAreasWorkplane(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Verify getClearedAreas filters previous ops by Workplane. REST machining stores each op's Path in its own (rotated) workplane frame. Mixing frames produces meaningless cleared-area polygons, so previous ops with a different Workplane than the current op must be skipped. The previous ops are real Custom (user-gcode) operations rather than mocks: this exercises the genuine ``PathDressup.baseOp`` resolution used to detect the current op while iterating, and the real ToolController/Workplane propert
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def test_sameWorkplaneIncluded(self)
        doc: A previous op sharing the current op's Workplane is included.
      - def test_differentWorkplaneSkipped(self)
        doc: A previous op with a different Workplane is skipped.
      - def test_currentOpStopsIteration(self)
        doc: Ops at or after the current op in the list are not considered.
      - def test_inactiveOpSkipped(self)
        doc: Inactive previous ops are skipped regardless of Workplane.
      - def test_missingWorkplaneTreatedAsZUp(self)
        doc: An op without a Workplane property is treated as Z-up.
  class TestStripRotaryAxes(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Verify _stripRotaryAxes drops rotary parameters from path commands.
    methods:
      - def test_pureRotaryCommandDropped(self)
        doc: A G0 with only rotary parameters is removed entirely.
      - def test_mixedCommandKeepsLinearAxes(self)
        doc: A move that carries both linear and rotary axes keeps the linear ones.
      - def test_clearedAreaIgnoresLeadingRotation(self)
        doc: Cleared area on a stripped path matches the same path without the rotary G0. This is the property that makes REST machining correct in 3+2: the leading rotary command must not cause PathSegmentWalker to rotate the subsequent X/Y/Z positions.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathPocket.py
functions:
  - def countOffsetLoops(commands, pocket_depth)
    doc: Count the number of distinct offset loops by counting plunge moves. A plunge move is a G1 command that changes Z to the cutting depth. We detect this by looking for G1 commands that go to pocket_depth after being at a higher Z (after a rapid move). Args: commands: List of G-code commands pocket_depth: The Z height where cutting occurs Returns: Number of distinct offset loops
classes:
  class TestPathPocket(PathTestBase)
    doc: Unit tests for the Pocket operation.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each test() method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple test() methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def createPocketOperation(self, part_obj, pocket_bottom_z, label, tool_diameter, **kwargs)
        doc: Create a pocket operation with the given parameters. Args: part_obj: The part object containing the geometry pocket_bottom_z: Z height of the pocket bottom label: Label for the pocket operation (job name will be "Job_<label>") tool_diameter: Diameter of the cutting tool **kwargs: Properties to set on the pocket operation (e.g., StepOver=10, Clearin
      - def test_pocket_square_offset(self)

      - def test_pocket_square_grid(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathProfile.py
classes:
  class TestPathProfile(PathTestBase)
    doc: Unit tests for the Adaptive operation.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def initClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def test00(self)
        doc: test00() Empty test.
      - def test01(self)
        doc: test01() Verify path generated on Face18, outside, with tool compensation.
      - def test02(self)
        doc: test02() Verify path generated on Face18, outside, without compensation.
      - def test03(self)
        doc: test03() Verify path generated on Face18, outside, with compensation and extra offset -radius.
  class TestPathOpenProfile(PathTestBase)
    doc: Unit tests for profiling open edges (edges of geometry).
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Set up the triangle geometry and job for all tests in this class.
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... Cleanup after all tests in this class have been executed.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each test() method.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method.
      - def testOpenProfileSetup(self)

      - def test02(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotaryGenerators.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotaryGenerators(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Analytic-shape verification of the rotary drop-cutter sampler.
    methods:
      - def assertRadiiClose(self, expected, actual, tol)
        doc: Assert two radii grids are close, with NaN-aware comparison.
      - def test00_cylinder_constant_radius(self)
        doc: Cylinder of radius 10 around +X: r is 10 for all (x, theta).
      - def test01_sphere_radius_varies_with_x(self)
        doc: Unit sphere around origin: r(x) = sqrt(1 - x^2).
      - def test02_cone_radius_linear_in_x(self)
        doc: Truncated cone: r(x_min)=2, r(x_max)=8 -> linear in x.
      - def test03_y_axis_rotary(self)
        doc: Cylinder around +Y axis sampled with axis_vec=(0,1,0).
      - def test04_incremental_matches_fresh(self)
        doc: Production (incremental rotation) matches fresh-STL reference.
      - def test05_restore_stl_after_call(self)
        doc: sample() leaves the STL in (approximately) its original pose.
      - def test06_rejects_non_axis_aligned_axis(self)
        doc: Non-axis-aligned rotary axis raises NotImplementedError.
      - def test07_rejects_zero_axis(self)
        doc: Zero vector axis raises ValueError.
      - def test08_empty_inputs_return_empty(self)
        doc: Empty xs or thetas returns appropriately-shaped empty array.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotaryParallel.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotaryParallel(PathTestBase)
    doc: Generator-level unit tests for the Parallel rotary cut pattern.
    methods:
      - def test00_emits_motion_commands(self)
        doc: Generator produces a non-trivial number of motion commands. For a 20mm axial range, 2mm axial_stepover, radius 10: angular_step = 2 / 10 = 0.2 rad n_passes ~ ceil(2*pi / 0.2) + 1 ~ 33 samples/pass = ceil(20 / (2*0.25)) + 1 = 41 So there should be ~33 * 41 cutting G1s plus approach/retract.
      - def test01_first_cutting_g1_has_xyza(self)
        doc: The first cutting G1 must qualify X, Y, Z, and the rotary letter.
      - def test02_a_traverses_requested_range(self)
        doc: A values span [theta_start, theta_end] within stepover tolerance.
      - def test03_pass_count_matches_angular_stepover(self)
        doc: Distinct A values in cutting G1s match the expected pass count.
      - def test04_zigzag_x_direction_alternates(self)
        doc: Consecutive passes traverse X in opposite directions.
      - def test05_y_axis_emits_rotary_letter(self)
        doc: Y rotary axis: rotary letter is honored as the parameter key.
      - def test06_conventional_inverts_theta_direction(self)
        doc: Conventional cut walks A in the opposite direction from Climb.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotaryPostRegression.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotaryPostRegression(PathTestBase)
    doc: Multi-post regression for rotary surfacing output (Issue #3).
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def setUp(self)
      - def test00_linuxcnc_compound_emits_xyza(self)
        doc: LinuxCNC must not drop A-axis words from compound moves.
      - def test01_linuxcnc_compound_unwound_a(self)
        doc: Posted A values within a pass remain unwound (no 0/360 wrap).
      - def test02_grbl_compound_emits_xyza(self)
        doc: Grbl preserves A on compound XYZA moves consistent with its caps.
      - def test03_grbl_compound_unwound_a(self)
      - def test04_compound_round_trip_first_command(self)
        doc: Coordinate values on the first cutting move round-trip per post. Matches by A value across op.Path and posted G-code so it is robust to the post inserting/dropping non-cutting moves: pick the first G1 in op.Path with a non-zero A, look up the posted line carrying that same A, and compare X/Y/Z within format precision.
      - def test05_linuxcnc_split_emits_separate_axis_lines(self)
        doc: LinuxCNC posts split moves without merging or dropping the A line.
      - def test06_grbl_split_emits_separate_axis_lines(self)
      - def test07_split_unwound_a_preserved(self)
        doc: Even when emitted on dedicated lines, A is still unwound.
      - def test08_split_round_trip_axis_pairs(self)
        doc: Split A move + paired XYZ move round-trip the original compound.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotaryRings.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotaryRings(PathTestBase)
    doc: Unit tests for the Rings rotary-surface generator.
    methods:
      - def test00_emits_xyza_commands(self)
        doc: Generator returns enough commands and the first cut has XYZA.
      - def test01_ring_count_matches_formula(self)
        doc: Number of rings in output equals ceil(span/stepover)+1.
      - def test02_each_ring_sweeps_full_revolution(self)
        doc: Within each ring A spans ~theta_end - theta_start (== 2*pi).
      - def test03_x_monotonic_across_rings(self)
        doc: Each new ring's X is >= the previous ring's X (axial advance).
      - def test04_unwound_a_monotonic_across_rings(self)
        doc: A grows monotonically across rings (no 0/360 wrap).
      - def test05_rejects_invalid_inputs(self)
        doc: Bad arguments raise ValueError, not silent garbage.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotarySurface.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotarySurface(PathTestBase)
    doc: Integration tests for the Rotary Surface operation MVP.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def test00_emits_xyza_for_x_rotary(self)
      - def test01_unwound_a_is_monotonic(self)
        doc: A values within a single radial pass must never wrap (unwound).
      - def test02_start_angle_honored(self)
        doc: StartAngle is the unwound angular start; the spiral begins there.
      - def test03_y_axis_rotary_emits_b(self)
      - def test04_validation_rejects_box_stock(self)
      - def test05_validation_rejects_misaligned_stock(self)
      - def test07_step_down_creates_multiple_passes(self)
        doc: Non-zero StepDown produces multiple radial pass groups.
      - def test06_z_height_above_radius(self)
      - def test08_offcenter_part_warns(self)
        doc: Part centroid offset > LinearDeflection emits a warning.
      - def test09_angular_extents_warn_when_outside_limits(self)
        doc: Angular extents past machine rotary limits emit a warning. ``App::PropertyAngle`` clamps to ?360, so we exercise the warning by giving the Job a Machine whose rotary axis is narrower than that - a typical 4th-axis indexer with ?90 deg travel - and letting the default StopAngle=360 exceed it.
      - def test11_parallel_pattern_emits_xyza(self)
        doc: CutPattern=Parallel produces an XYZA G1 path on the cone fixture.
      - def test10_centerline_feed_clamp_logs(self)
        doc: rotary_spiral.generate logs when the rotary feed is clamped.
      - def test12_rings_pattern_emits_xyza(self)
        doc: CutPattern='Rings' produces XYZA G1 commands end-to-end.
      - def test13_feedmode_axial_only_emits_constant_feed(self)
        doc: AxialOnly: every cut F equals the tool-controller HorizFeed.
      - def test14_feedmode_surface_speed_varies_feed(self)
        doc: SurfaceSpeed: F scales with 1/r, so tapered radii give different F.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathRotationGenerator.py
classes:
  class TestPathRotationGenerator(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    methods:
      - def test00_identity_orientation(self)
        doc: Test solving for identity orientation (Z-up) on table-table machine. Expected behavior: Should return A=0, C=0 for Z-up orientation.
      - def test10_forty_five_degree_tilt(self)
        doc: Test solving for 45-degree tilt on table-table machine. Expected behavior: Should find valid A and C angles to achieve 45-degree orientation.
      - def test20_axis_limits_active(self)
        doc: Test solving with axis limits that constrain solution. Expected behavior: Should fail when desired orientation requires angles outside limits.
      - def test30_head_head_machine(self)
        doc: Test solving on head-head machine configuration. Expected behavior: Should correctly handle head rotary axes (direct tool rotation).
      - def test40_mixed_machine(self)
        doc: Test solving on mixed table-head machine. Expected behavior: Should correctly handle combination of table and head rotaries.
      - def test50_single_axis_solve(self)
        doc: Test solving with single rotary axis (3+1 indexing). Expected behavior: Should solve with only one rotary axis.
      - def test60_restrict_axes(self)
        doc: Test solving with restricted axis subset. Expected behavior: Should only solve for specified axes, ignoring others.
      - def test70_current_state_awareness(self)
        doc: Test that solver considers current state for minimal motion. Expected behavior: When multiple solutions exist, the solver should pick the one closest to the current state. Solving from two different starting states should yield different chosen solutions.
      - def test80_solution_preference_shortest(self)
        doc: Test shortest solution preference. Expected behavior: Should select solution with minimal total angular travel.
      - def test90_allow_flip_enabled(self)
        doc: Test flip solutions when allowed. Expected behavior: Should consider 180-degree equivalent solutions.
      - def test100_no_rotary_axes(self)
        doc: Test behavior when machine has no rotary axes. Expected behavior: Should fail gracefully with appropriate error message.
      - def test110_sequential_solves(self)
        doc: Test sequential solves to verify minimal-motion selection. Expected behavior: Series of solves should prefer minimal motion between solutions.
      - def test120_geometry_rotation_y_workplane(self)
        doc: Test that compute_rotation_matrix produces a rotation consistent with the solved angles for workplane Vector(0, 1, 0). The geometry rotation R must satisfy R.multVec(workplane) ? Z, i.e. it maps the workplane normal into the spindle axis.
      - def test130_geometry_rotation_neg_x_workplane(self)
        doc: Test geometry rotation for workplane Vector(-1, 0, 0). R.multVec((-1,0,0)) should ? (0,0,1).
      - def test140_geometry_rotation_diagonal_workplane(self)
        doc: Test geometry rotation for an arbitrary diagonal workplane. R.multVec(desired) should ? (0,0,1).
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPathThreadMilling.py
functions:
  - def radii(internal, major, minor, toolDia, toolCrest, cuttingAngle=60.0)
    doc: test radii function for simple testing
classes:
  class TestObject(object)
  class TestPathThreadMilling(PathTestBase)
    doc: Test thread milling basics.
    methods:
      - def assertRadii(self, have, want)
      - def assertList(self, have, want)
      - def assertSetupInternal(self, obj, c, begin, end)
      - def assertSetupExternal(self, obj, c, begin, end)
      - def test00(self)
        doc: Verify internal radii.
      - def test01(self)
        doc: Verify internal radii with tool crest.
      - def test02(self)
        doc: Verify crest compensation uses cutting angle, not hardcoded 60 deg. Bug: threadRadii() hardcodes SQRT_3_DIVIDED_BY_2 (the crest-to-tip factor for a 60 deg profile). For a non-60 deg profile the factor should be 1 / (2 * tan(cuttingAngle / 2)). For 60 deg ISO: factor = 1/(2*tan(30 deg)) = ?3/2 ? 0.86603 For 55 deg Whitworth: factor = 1/(2*tan(27.5 deg)) ? 0.96050 With m
      - def test03(self)
        doc: Verify crest compensation for external thread with non-60 deg angle. Same bug as test02 but for external threads. With major=20, minor=18, toolDia=2, crest=0.1 (external): H = 1.6, innerTip = 8.6 60 deg -> toolTip = 8.6 + 0.1*0.86603 = 8.68660 -> min_r = 9.68660 55 deg -> toolTip = 8.6 + 0.1*0.96050 = 8.69605 -> min_r = 9.69605 This test will FAIL until thr
      - def test10(self)
        doc: Verify internal thread passes.
      - def test20(self)
        doc: Verify external radii.
      - def test21(self)
        doc: Verify external radii with tool crest.
      - def test30(self)
        doc: Verify external thread passes.
      - def test40(self)
        doc: Verify internal right hand thread setup.
      - def test41(self)
        doc: Verify internal left hand thread setup.
      - def test50(self)
        doc: Verify exteranl right hand thread setup.
      - def test51(self)
        doc: Verify exteranl left hand thread setup.
```

```text
  class TestBuildPostList(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: The postlist is the list of postprocessable elements from the job. The list varies depending on -The operations -The tool controllers -The work coordinate systems (WCS) or 'fixtures' -How the job is ordering the output (WCS, tool, operation) -Whether or not the output is being split to multiple files This test case ensures that the correct sequence of postable objects is created. The list will be comprised of a list of tuples. Each tuple consists of (subobject string, [list of objects]) The subo
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)

      - def tearDown(self)

      - def test020(self)

      - def test050(self)

      - def test070(self)
        doc: Test that early_tool_prep inserts Tn prep commands after each M6 when enabled via machine. Given: A job split by tool (SplitOutput=True, OrderOutputBy=Tool) and a machine whose processing.early_tool_prep is True When: _buildPostList() is called (no explicit parameter) Then: The first M6 in the first group targets tool 5, and a T2 prep command appea
      - def test080(self)
        doc: Test early_tool_prep with combined output (SplitOutput=False). Given: A job with combined output (SplitOutput=False, OrderOutputBy=Tool) and a machine whose processing.early_tool_prep is True When: _buildPostList() is called Then: Two M6 commands appear (T5 then T2) and a T2 prep command appears after the first M6 and before the second M6. Example:
      - def test090_buildpostlist_returns_postable_instances(self)
        doc: Test that all items returned by _buildPostList are Postable instances. Given: A job with 3 operations, 2 tool controllers, and 2 fixtures When: _buildPostList() is called with no-split, order-by-operation Then: Every item in the flat sublist is an instance of PostList.Postable Example: postlist = pp._buildPostList() for _, sublist in postlist: for 
      - def test091_postable_standard_fields(self)
        doc: Test that every Postable exposes the required standard interface. Given: A job producing fixture, tool_controller, and operation items When: _buildPostList() is called Then: Each Postable has: - item_type: a non-empty string - label: a string - path: a Path.Path instance - source: the original object or None - data: a dict Example: item.item_type =
      - def test092_postable_item_types_correct(self)
        doc: Test that item_type is set correctly for each kind of postable. Given: A job ordered by fixture with 2 fixtures, producing fixture, TC, and op items When: _buildPostList() is called with split=True, order-by-fixture Then: The first item in the first group is item_type="fixture", and the set of item_types across all items includes "tool_controller" 
      - def test093_postable_path_is_copy(self)
        doc: Test that Postable wraps a copy of the source path, not the original object. Given: An operation with a non-empty path in the job When: _buildPostList() creates a Postable for that operation Then: postable.path is a different Python object from source.Path so mutations to postable.path do not touch the document object. Example: op_item.source.Path 
      - def test094_tool_controller_postable_has_tool_number(self)
        doc: Test that tool_controller Postables carry the tool number in data["tool_number"]. Given: A job with tool controllers whose ToolNumber values are 5 and 2 When: _buildPostList() is called Then: Each tool_controller Postable has data["tool_number"] matching ToolNumber. Example: tc_item.item_type == "tool_controller" tc_item.data["tool_number"] == 5
      - def test095_operation_postable_tool_controller_is_postable(self)
        doc: Test that accessing ToolController on an operation Postable returns a Postable, not the raw FreeCAD-compatible runtime document object. When legacy post scripts or other code accesses item.ToolController on an operation Postable, they must receive a Postable wrapper-not a live reference to the real TC document object. Returning the live object allo
      - def test096_operation_postable_tool_controller_is_isolated(self)
        doc: Test that the Postable ToolController returned from an operation Postable is a copy, so mutations do not propagate back to the real FreeCAD-compatible runtime TC document object. Given: An operation Postable whose ToolController attribute returns a Postable When: We replace the Postable TC's path with a new empty path Then: The original FreeCAD-com
      - def test097_buildpostlist_does_not_touch_operations(self)
        doc: Test that _buildPostList() does not mark operations as Touched (needing recompute). Post-processing is a read-only activity: it must produce G-code from the current operation state without side-effecting the FreeCAD-compatible runtime document. When operations are marked Touched after postprocessing, the user sees spurious "recompute required" prom
  class TestJobPropertyOverrides(unittest.TestCase)

      - def test_job_property_overrides_with_plasma(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPostGCodes.py
classes:
  class TestPostGCodes(PathTestUtils.PathTestBase)
    doc: Test the test_post.py postprocessor G codes.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def single_compare(self, path, expected, args, debug=False)
        doc: Perform a test with a single line of gcode comparison.
      - def multi_compare(self, path, expected, args, debug=False)
        doc: Perform a test with multiple lines of gcode comparison.
```

```text
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
        doc: setUpClass()... This method is called upon instantiation of this test class. Add code and objects here that are needed for the duration of the test() methods in this class. In other words, set up the 'global' test environment here; use the `setUp()` method to set up a 'local' test environment. This method does not have access to the class `self` re
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
        doc: tearDownClass()... This method is called prior to destruction of this test class. Add code and objects here that cleanup the test environment after the test() methods in this class have been executed. This method does not have access to the class `self` reference. This method is able to call static methods within this same class.
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: setUp()... This method is called prior to each `test()` method. Add code and objects here that are needed for multiple `test()` methods.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: tearDown()... This method is called after each test() method. Add cleanup instructions here. Such cleanup instructions will likely undo those in the setUp() method.
      - def single_compare(self, path, expected, args, debug=False)
        doc: Perform a test with a single comparison.
      - def test20000(self)
        doc: Test M0 command Generation.
      - def test20010(self)
        doc: Test M1 command Generation.
      - def test20020(self)
        doc: Test M2 command Generation.
      - def test20030(self)
        doc: Test M3 command Generation.
      - def test20040(self)
        doc: Test M4 command Generation.
      - def test20050(self)
        doc: Test M5 command Generation.
      - def test20060(self)
        doc: Test M6 command Generation.
      - def test20070(self)
        doc: Test M7 command Generation.
      - def test20080(self)
        doc: Test M8 command Generation.
      - def test20090(self)
        doc: Test M9 command Generation.
      - def test20300(self)
        doc: Test M30 command Generation.
      - def test20480(self)
        doc: Test M48 command Generation.
      - def test20490(self)
        doc: Test M49 command Generation.
      - def test20600(self)
        doc: Test M60 command Generation.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/CAMTests/TestPostOutput.py
classes:
  class TestFileNameGenerator(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: String substitution allows the following: %D ... directory of the active document %d ... name of the active document (with extension) %M ... user macro directory %j ... name of the active Job object The Following can be used if output is being split. If Output is not split these will be ignored. %S ... Sequence Number (default) Either: %T ... Tool Number %t ... Tool Controller label %W ... Work Coordinate System %O ... Operation Label |split on| use | Ignore | |-----------|-------|--------| |fix
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def test000(self)
      - def test010(self)
      - def test015(self)
      - def test020(self)
      - def test030(self)
      - def test040(self)
      - def test045(self)
        doc: Testing the sequence number substitution
      - def test046(self)
        doc: Testing the sequence number substitution
      - def test050(self)
      - def test060(self)
        doc: Test subpart naming
      - def test070(self)
        doc: Test %T substitution (tool number) with actual tool controller
      - def test071(self)
        doc: Test %t substitution (tool description) with actual tool controller
      - def test072(self)
        doc: Test %W substitution (work coordinate system/fixture)
      - def test073(self)
        doc: Test %O substitution (operation label)
      - def test075(self)
        doc: Test path and filename substitutions together
      - def test076(self)
        doc: Test invalid substitution characters are ignored
      - def test080_file_extension_override(self)
        doc: Test that a file_extension override replaces the default .nc extension. Expected: file_extension = "gcode" PostProcessorOutputFile = "" -> output file ends with .gcode instead of .nc
      - def test081_file_extension_overrides_job_extension(self)
        doc: Test that file_extension override always wins over the extension from PostProcessorOutputFile, since the postprocessor is the authoritative source for the output format. Expected: file_extension = "gcode" PostProcessorOutputFile = "output.tap" -> output file ends with .gcode (postprocessor wins)
      - def test082_no_machine_falls_back_to_nc(self)
        doc: Test that .nc is used when no machine is provided and no extension is specified by the user. Expected: No machine, PostProcessorOutputFile = "" -> output file ends with .nc
  class TestExport2Integration(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Integration tests for the export2() function.
    methods:
      - def setUpClass(cls)
      - def tearDownClass(cls)
      - def test010_export2_returns_gcode_sections(self)
        doc: Test that export2() returns a non-empty list of (name, gcode) tuples.
      - def test020_header_enabled_shows_machine_name(self)
        doc: Test that _build_header includes machine name when header and include_machine_name are True. Expected: Output contains a comment line with the machine name.
      - def test021_header_disabled_suppresses_all_header(self)
        doc: Test that _build_header produces no header when output_header is False. Expected: No machine name, no tool list, no date in output header area.
      - def test022_header_true_comments_false_suppresses_inline(self)
        doc: Test that header:true + comments:false shows header but suppresses inline comments. Expected: Header comments present, but inline (Test comment) is suppressed.
      - def test023_header_false_comments_true_shows_inline(self)
        doc: Test that header:false + comments:true suppresses header but shows inline comments. Expected: No header comments, but inline (Test inline comment) is present.
      - def test040_canned_cycle_termination(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_canned_cycles adds G80 termination after drill cycle sequences. Expected: BEFORE: G81 X10 Y20 Z-10 R1 F100 G81 X10 Y30 Z-10 R1 F100 AFTER: G81 X10 Y20 Z-10 R1 F100 G81 X10 Y30 Z-10 R1 F100 G80
      - def test050_split_arcs(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_split_arcs splits arc moves into linear segments. Expected when split_arcs=True: BEFORE: G2 X10 Y0 I5 J0 F100 AFTER: G1 X... Y... F100 (multiple linear segments)
      - def test060_spindle_wait_injects_dwell(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_spindle_wait injects G4 pause after M3/M4 spindle start. Expected when spindle_wait=2.5: BEFORE: M3 S1000 G0 X10 Y20 AFTER: M3 S1000 G4 P2.5 G0 X10 Y20
      - def test061_spindle_wait_zero_no_dwell(self)
        doc: Test that no G4 is injected when spindle_wait is 0. Expected: M3 S1000 G0 X10 Y20 (no G4 between)
      - def test070_coolant_delay_injects_dwell(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_coolant_delay injects G4 pause after coolant on commands. Expected when coolant_delay=1.5: BEFORE: M8 G1 X10 F100 AFTER: M8 G4 P1.5 G1 X10 F100
      - def test080_translate_rapid_moves(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_translate_rapids converts G0 to G1 when enabled. Expected when translate_rapid_moves=True: BEFORE: G0 X10 Y10 G1 X20 Y10 F100 G0 Z5 AFTER: G1 X10 Y10 G1 X20 Y10 F100 G1 Z5
      - def test090_xy_before_z_after_tool_change(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_xy_before_z decomposes first move after tool change. When xy_before_z_after_tool_change=True, a combined XYZ move after an M6 tool change is split into XY first, then Z, to prevent plunging before positioning. Expected: BEFORE: M6 T1 G0 X10 Y20 Z-5 AFTER: M6 T1 G0 X10 Y20 G0 Z-5
      - def test100_bcnc_comments_enabled(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_bcnc_commands injects bCNC block annotations when enabled. Expected when output_bcnc_comments=True: (Block-name: TestProfile) (Block-expand: 0) (Block-enable: 1) G0 X0 Y0 Z5 ... (Block-name: post_amble) (Block-expand: 0) (Block-enable: 1)
      - def test101_bcnc_comments_disabled(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_bcnc_commands removes bCNC annotations when disabled. Expected when output_bcnc_comments=False: No (Block-name:, (Block-expand:, or (Block-enable: lines.
      - def test102_bcnc_with_regular_comments_disabled(self)
        doc: Test that bCNC comments appear even when regular comments are disabled. bCNC block annotations are structural, not user comments, so they should be output regardless of the comments.enabled setting.
      - def test103_bcnc_block_structure(self)
        doc: Test that each bCNC block has the correct 3-line structure: (Block-name: <label>) (Block-expand: 0) (Block-enable: 1)
      - def test110_tool_length_offset_enabled(self)
        doc: Test that _expand_tool_length_offset adds G43 after M6 in a tool controller path. G43 (tool length offset) must be injected immediately after M6 in the tool controller postable's path. M6 belongs in the tool_controller postable - not in operation paths - so the test builds a postlist directly rather than using the full export2 pipeline. Given: A to
      - def test111_tool_length_offset_disabled(self)
        doc: Test that no G43 commands appear when tool length offset is disabled. When output_tool_length_offset=False, M6 in the operation path should NOT be followed by G43. Expected: BEFORE: M6 T1 G0 X10 Y20 Z5 AFTER: M6 T1 G0 X10 Y20 Z5 (no G43)
      - def test120_line_numbers_exclude_header(self)
        doc: Test that line numbers are applied to G-code body but not header comments. Expected: (Machine: TestMachine) <- no line number N100 G21 <- numbered N110 G0 X0 Y0 Z5 <- numbered
      - def test121_line_numbers_start_and_increment(self)
        doc: Test that line_number_start and line_increment are respected. Expected with start=50, increment=5: N50 ... N55 ... N60 ...
      - def test122_precision_from_config(self)
        doc: Test that axis_precision and feed_precision control decimal places. Expected with axis_precision=4, feed_precision=1: G0 X10.1235 Y20.9876 Z5.5000 G1 X100.1235 ... F6007.4
      - def test123_spindle_decimals(self)
        doc: Test that spindle_decimals controls decimal places for spindle speed. Expected with spindle_decimals=0: M3 S1235 (rounded, no decimals)
      - def test124_comment_symbol_formatting(self)
        doc: Test that comment_symbol controls comment format. Expected with comment_symbol='(': (Test comment) Expected with comment_symbol=';': ; Test comment
      - def test125_command_space_option(self)
        doc: Test that command_space controls spacing between command and parameters. Expected with command_space=" ": G0 X10.000 Expected with command_space="": G0X10.000
      - def test126_end_of_line_chars(self)
        doc: Test that end_of_line_chars controls line ending characters. Expected with "\r\n": CRLF line endings Expected with "\n": LF-only line endings
      - def test127_output_units(self)
        doc: Test that output_units inserts G20 (imperial) or G21 (metric). Expected: G21 for metric, G20 for imperial.
      - def test128_duplicate_parameters_suppressed(self)
        doc: Test that duplicate parameters are suppressed when duplicates.parameters=False. Expected: BEFORE: G0 X10 Y10 Z5 F60000 G1 X20 Y10 Z5 F60000 AFTER: G0 X10 Y10 Z5 F60000 G1 X20 (Y, Z, F suppressed - unchanged)
      - def test129_duplicate_commands_suppressed(self)
        doc: Test that duplicate G-code commands are suppressed when duplicates.commands=False. Expected: BEFORE: G1 X10 Y10 G1 X20 Y10 G1 X20 Y20 AFTER: G1 X10 Y10 X20 Y10 (G1 suppressed - same as previous) X20 Y20
      - def test128b_bare_command_suppressed_when_all_params_duplicate(self)
        doc: Test that a command is fully suppressed when all its parameters are duplicates, rather than emitting a bare command name. Expected: G0 X10 Y10 Z5 <- first move, all params output G0 X10 Y10 Z5 <- identical, ALL params suppressed -> entire line dropped
      - def test130_modal_state_reset_after_tool_change(self)
        doc: Test that modal state resets after tool change so parameters are not suppressed as duplicates. When duplicates.parameters=False, the second M3 S6000 and G4 P4.000 after a tool change must still include S and P parameters even though they are numerically identical to the first occurrence. Expected: M6 T2 M3 S6000 G4 P4.000 G0 X10 Y10 Z5 M5 M6 T3 M3 
      - def test140_gcode_blocks_insertion(self)
        doc: Test that all G-code blocks from machine config are properly inserted. Expected: safety, preamble, prejob, preoperation, postoperation, postjob, and postamble all appear in output.
      - def test141_rotary_blocks_insertion(self)
        doc: Test that pre/post rotary blocks are inserted around rotary axis moves. Expected: 2 rotary groups -> 2 pre-rotary + 2 post-rotary blocks.
      - def test142_fixture_change_blocks_insertion(self)
        doc: Test that pre/post fixture change blocks are inserted when fixtures change. Expected with fixtures=[G54, G55]: (prefixture) before each fixture, (postfixture) after.
      - def test150_tool_radius_compensation_property(self)
        doc: Test that postprocessors declare support for G41/G42 tool radius compensation. Expected: LinuxCNC schema has supports_tool_radius_compensation=True.
      - def test151_supported_commands_property(self)
        doc: Test that postprocessors declare their supported G-code command list. Expected: Generic post has a substantial list including G0, G1, G2, G3, M3, M6.
      - def test152_nested_parentheses_in_comments(self)
        doc: Test that nested parentheses in comments are sanitized. Path.Command truncates at first ) after (, so nested parens get mangled. The post processor must ensure output has balanced parentheses. Expected: INPUT: (Begin operation: Pocket (fast)) OUTPUT: (Begin operation: Pocket fast]) <- balanced, ] replaces nested )
      - def test153_drill_cycles_to_translate_property(self)
        doc: Test that drill_cycles_to_translate postprocessor property is stored correctly. Expected: Property value round-trips through Machine.from_dict.
      - def test154_schema_defaults_applied_for_missing_properties(self)
        doc: Test that schema defaults are applied when postprocessor.properties is sparse (only contains user-changed values). Real .fcm files only store properties the user explicitly changed. Properties like preamble, postamble, safetyblock may be absent. _apply_schema_defaults must fill them in from the schema so that export2 can read them. INPUT: - Machine
```

```text
      - def test020_classify_legacy_post(self)
        doc: Legacy posts without POST_TYPE are classified as 'legacy'.

      - def test050_machine_list_excludes_legacy(self)
```
