```text
    methods:
      - def build_image(self, obj, imageName, as_bytes=False, view='default')
  class GuiImageBuilder(ImageBuilder)
    doc: A class for generating images of 3D objects in a FreeCAD-compatible runtime GUI environment.
    methods:
      - def prepare_view(self, obj, view='default')
      - def record_visibility(self)
      - def destroy_view(self, idx)
      - def restore_visibility(self)
      - def build_image(self, obj, image_name, as_bytes=False, view='default')
        doc: Makes an image of the target object. Returns either the image as bytes or a filename. On failure, logs a warning and returns b"" (as_bytes) or "" (file path).
      - def capture_image(self, file_path)
      - def capture_image_to_bytes(self)
        doc: Capture the current view directly to bytes without writing to disk
  class NonGuiImageBuilder(ImageBuilder)
    methods:
      - def build_image(self, obj, image_name, as_bytes=False, view='default')
        doc: Generates a headless picture of a 3D object and saves it as a PNG and optionally a PostScript file. Args: - obj: The 3D object to generate an image for. - image_name: Base name for the output image file without extension. Returns: - A boolean indicating the success of the operation.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Main/Sanity/Sanity.py
classes:
  class CAMSanity
    methods:
      - def summarize(self)
        doc: Gather all the incremental parts of the analysis
      - def squawk(self, operator, note, date=datetime.now(), squawkType='NOTE')
      - def serialize(self, obj)
        doc: A function to serialize non-serializable objects.
      - def get_output_report(self)
      - def get_all_squawks(self, overrides=None)
        doc: Collect squawks from all validation sections without generating images or HTML. Calls each _xxxData() method directly using the current image_builder (a DummyImageBuilder when invoked via validate_job(), so no GUI or file I/O occurs). Also runs _validate_job_structure() for structural checks. Args: overrides: Optional dict of postprocessor property
      - def validate_job(job, overrides=None)
        doc: Lightweight job validation without generating images or HTML. Bypasses __init__ entirely to avoid calling summarize() or any image-generation code. Sets up only the attributes needed by the _xxxData() methods, uses DummyImageBuilder unconditionally, and calls get_all_squawks(). Args: job: FreeCAD-compatible runtime CAM job object overrides: Optiona
      - def validate_for_postprocessing(self)
        doc: Lightweight validation for post-processing without full report generation. Returns: tuple: (has_critical_issues, all_squawks, critical_squawks)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Adaptive.py
functions:
  - def convertTo2d(pathArray)
  - def sceneDrawPath(path, color=(0, 0, 1))
  - def sceneClean()
  - def renderProgressCallback(tpaths)
  - def initSceneGraph(z=10)
    doc: Initialize the scene graph for adaptive visualization. Args: z: The Z height at which to draw paths (default: 10) Returns True if scene graph was initialized, False otherwise.
  - def discretize(edge, flipDirection=False)
  - def GenerateGCode(op, obj, adaptiveResults)
  - def Execute(op, obj)
  - def get_cleared_area(op, obj, pathArray, zOverride=None)
  - def ExecuteModelAware(op, obj)
  - def vectorsOnStraightLine(v)
  - def projectFacesToXY(faces, minEdgeLength=1e-10)
    doc: projectFacesToXY(faces, minEdgeLength) Calculates the projection of the provided list of faces onto the XY plane. The returned value is a single shape that may contain multiple faces if there were disjoint projections. Each individual face will be clean, without triangulated geometry, etc., and will be at Z=0 on the XY plane minEdgeLength is provided to (eg) filter out the tips of cones that are internally represented as arbitrarily-small circular faces- using those for additional operations cau
  - def SetupProperties()
  - def Create(name, obj=None, parentJob=None)
    doc: Create(name) ... Creates and returns a Adaptive operation.
classes:
  class PathAdaptive(PathOp.ObjectOp)
    methods:
      - def opFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: opFeatures(obj) ... returns the OR'ed list of features used and supported by the operation. The default implementation returns "FeatureTool | FeatureDepths | FeatureHeights | FeatureStartPoint" Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def propertyEnumerations(self, dataType='data')
        doc: helixOpPropertyEnumerations(dataType="data")... return property enumeration lists of specified dataType. Args: dataType = 'data', 'raw', 'translated' Notes: 'data' is list of internal string literals used in code 'raw' is list of (translated_text, data_string) tuples 'translated' is list of translated string literals
      - def initOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initOperation(obj) ... implement to create additional properties. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
      - def opExecute(self, obj)
        doc: opExecute(obj) ... called whenever the receiver needs to be recalculated. See documentation of execute() for a list of base functionality provided. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Area.py
functions:
  - def SetupProperties()
classes:
  class ObjectOp(PathOp.ObjectOp)
    doc: Base class for all Path.Area based operations. Provides standard features including debugging properties AreaParams, PathParams and removalshape, all hidden. The main reason for existence is to implement the standard interface to Path.Area so subclasses only have to provide the shapes for the operations.
    methods:
      - def opFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: opFeatures(obj) ... returns the base features supported by all Path.Area based operations. The standard feature list is OR'ed with the return value of areaOpFeatures(). Do not overwrite, implement areaOpFeatures(obj) instead.
      - def areaOpFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpFeatures(obj) ... overwrite to add operation specific features. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def initOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initOperation(obj) ... sets up standard Path.Area properties and calls initAreaOp(). Do not overwrite, overwrite initAreaOp(obj) instead.
      - def initAreaOp(self, obj)
        doc: initAreaOp(obj) ... overwrite if the receiver class needs initialisation. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def areaOpShapeForDepths(self, obj, job)
        doc: areaOpShapeForDepths(obj) ... returns the shape used to make an initial calculation for the depths being used. The default implementation returns the job's Base.Shape
      - def areaOpOnChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: areaOpOnChanged(obj, porp) ... overwrite to process operation specific changes to properties. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opOnChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: opOnChanged(obj, prop) ... base implementation of the notification framework - do not overwrite. The base implementation takes a stab at determining Heights and Depths if the operations's Base changes. Do not overwrite, overwrite areaOpOnChanged(obj, prop) instead.
      - def opOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def areaOpOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpOnDocumentRestored(obj) ... overwrite to fully restore receiver
      - def opSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: opSetDefaultValues(obj) ... base implementation, do not overwrite. The base implementation sets the depths and heights based on the areaOpShapeForDepths() return value. Do not overwrite, overwrite areaOpSetDefaultValues(obj, job) instead.
      - def areaOpSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: areaOpSetDefaultValues(obj, job) ... overwrite to set initial values of operation specific properties. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opSetDefaultSide(self, obj)
        doc: setDefaltSide(obj) ... offer side while creating new operation
      - def getMiddlePointLongestEdge(self, shape)
        doc: getMiddlePointLongestEdge(shape) ... return middle point of longest edge from shape.
      - def opExecute(self, obj, getsim=False)
        doc: opExecute(obj, getsim=False) ... implementation of Path.Area ops. determines the parameters for _buildPathArea(). Do not overwrite, implement areaOpAreaParams(obj, isHole) ... op specific area param dictionary areaOpPathParams(obj, isHole) ... op specific path param dictionary areaOpShapes(obj) ... the shape for path area to process areaOpUseProjec
      - def areaOpAreaParams(self, obj, isHole)
        doc: areaOpAreaParams(obj, isHole) ... return operation specific area parameters in a dictionary. Note that the resulting parameters are stored in the property AreaParams. Must be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def areaOpPathParams(self, obj, isHole)
        doc: areaOpPathParams(obj, isHole) ... return operation specific path parameters in a dictionary. Note that the resulting parameters are stored in the property PathParams. Must be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def areaOpShapes(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpShapes(obj) ... return all shapes to be processed by Path.Area for this op. Must be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def areaOpUseProjection(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpUseProcjection(obj) ... return True if the operation can use procjection, defaults to False. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Base.py
functions:
  - def getCycleTimeEstimate(obj)
  - def SetupPropertiesLinking()
classes:
  class PathNoTCException(Exception)
    doc: PathNoTCException is raised when no TC was selected or matches the input criteria. This can happen intentionally by the user when they cancel the TC selection dialog.
  class ObjectOp(object)
    doc: Base class for proxy objects of all Path operations. Use this class as a base class for new operations. It provides properties and some functionality for the standard properties each operation supports. By OR'ing features from the feature list an operation can select which ones of the standard features it requires and/or supports. The currently supported features are: FeatureTool ... Use of a ToolController FeatureDepths ... Depths, for start, final FeatureHeights ... Heights, safe and clearance
    methods:
      - def addBaseProperty(self, obj)
      - def addOpValues(self, obj, values)
      - def addLinking(self, obj)
      - def opPropertyEnumerations(self, dataType='data')
        doc: opPropertyEnumerations(dataType="data")... return property enumeration lists of specified dataType. Args: dataType = 'data', 'raw', 'translated' Notes: 'data' is list of internal string literals used in code 'raw' is list of (translated_text, data_string) tuples 'translated' is list of translated string literals
      - def setEditorModes(self, obj, features)
        doc: Editor modes are not preserved during document store/restore, set editor modes for all properties
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: __getstat__(self) ... called when receiver is saved. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: __getstat__(self) ... called when receiver is restored. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: opFeatures(obj) ... returns the OR'ed list of features used and supported by the operation. The default implementation returns "FeatureTool | FeatureDepths | FeatureHeights | FeatureStartPoint" Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def initOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initOperation(obj) ... implement to create additional properties. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: opOnDocumentRestored(obj) ... implement if an op needs special handling like migrating the data model. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opOnChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: opOnChanged(obj, prop) ... overwrite to process property changes. This is a callback function that is invoked each time a property of the receiver is assigned a value. Note that the FC framework does not distinguish between assigning a different value and assigning the same value again. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: opSetDefaultValues(obj, job) ... overwrite to set initial default values. Called after the receiver has been fully created with all properties. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def opUpdateDepths(self, obj)
        doc: opUpdateDepths(obj) ... overwrite to implement special depths calculation. Can safely be overwritten by subclass.
      - def opExecute(self, obj)
        doc: opExecute(obj) ... called whenever the receiver needs to be recalculated. See documentation of execute() for a list of base functionality provided. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def baseShapes(self, obj)
        doc: baseShapes(obj) ... yield (base, subs) tuples for the operation's base geometry. When a 3+2 geometry rotation is active (``self._geom_transform_matrix`` is set), each *base* object is wrapped in a :class:`_TransformedShapeProxy` whose ``.Shape`` returns the pre-transformed (Z-up) copy. Sub-element names are unchanged - ``proxy.Shape.getElement("Fac
      - def opRejectAddBase(self, obj, base, sub)
        doc: opRejectAddBase(base, sub) ... if op returns True the addition of the feature is prevented. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: onChanged(obj, prop) ... base implementation of the FC notification framework. Do not overwrite, overwrite opOnChanged() instead.
      - def applyExpression(self, obj, prop, expr)
        doc: applyExpression(obj, prop, expr) ... set expression expr on obj.prop if expr is set
      - def setDefaultValues(self, obj)
        doc: setDefaultValues(obj) ... base implementation. Do not overwrite, overwrite opSetDefaultValues() instead.
      - def getJob(self, obj)
        doc: getJob(obj) ... return the job this operation is part of.
      - def updateDepths(self, obj, ignoreErrors=False)
        doc: updateDepths(obj) ... base implementation calculating depths depending on base geometry. Should not be overwritten.
      - def checkBase(self, obj)
        doc: checkBase(obj) ... check if Base is valid.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: execute(obj) ... base implementation - do not overwrite! Verifies that the operation is assigned to a job and that the job also has a valid Base. It also sets the following instance variables that can and should be safely be used by implementation of opExecute(): self.model ... List of base objects of the Job itself self.stock ... Stock object for 
      - def addBase(self, obj, base, sub)
      - def isToolSupported(self, obj, tool)
        doc: toolSupported(obj, tool) ... Returns true if the op supports the given tool. This function can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
  class Compass
    doc: A compass is a tool to help with direction so the Compass is a helper class to manage settings that affect tool and spindle direction. Settings managed: - Spindle Direction: Forward / Reverse / None - Cut Side: Inside / Outside (for perimeter operations) - Cut Mode: Climb / Conventional - Path Direction: CW / CCW (derived for perimeter operations) - Operation Type: Perimeter / Area (for facing/pocketing operations) This class allows the user to set and get any of these properties and the rest wi
    methods:
      - def spindle_dir(self)
      - def spindle_dir(self, value)
      - def cut_side(self)
      - def cut_side(self, value)
      - def cut_mode(self)
      - def cut_mode(self, value)
      - def operation_type(self)
      - def operation_type(self, value)
      - def path_dir(self)
      - def get_step_direction(self, approach_direction)
        doc: For area operations, determine the step direction for climb/conventional milling. Args: approach_direction: "X+", "X-", "Y+", "Y-" - the primary cutting direction Returns: True if steps should be in positive direction, False for negative direction
      - def get_cutting_direction(self, approach_direction, pass_index=0, pattern='zigzag')
        doc: For area operations, determine the cutting direction for each pass. Args: approach_direction: "X+", "X-", "Y+", "Y-" - the primary cutting direction pass_index: Index of the current pass (0-based) pattern: "zigzag", "unidirectional", "spiral" Returns: True if cutting should be in forward direction, False for reverse
      - def report(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/CircularHoleBase.py
classes:
  class ObjectOp(PathOp.ObjectOp)
    doc: Base class for proxy objects of all operations on circular holes.
    methods:
      - def opFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: opFeatures(obj) ... calls circularHoleFeatures(obj) and ORs in the standard features required for processing circular holes. Do not overwrite, implement circularHoleFeatures(obj) instead
      - def circularHoleFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: circularHoleFeatures(obj) ... overwrite to add operations specific features. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def initOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initOperation(obj) ... adds Disabled properties and calls initCircularHoleOperation(obj). Do not overwrite, implement initCircularHoleOperation(obj) instead.
      - def updateSortingVisibility(self, obj)
        doc: Show or hide StartPoint, EndPoint and UseEndPoint based on SortingMode.
      - def opOnChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: opOnChanged(obj, prop) ... react to SortingMode changes to update property visibility.
      - def initCircularHoleOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initCircularHoleOperation(obj) ... overwrite if the subclass needs initialisation. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def holeDiameter(self, base, sub)
        doc: holeDiameter(base, sub) ... returns the diameter of the specified hole.
      - def holePosition(self, base, sub)
        doc: holePosition(base, sub) ... returns a Vector for the position defined by the given features. Note that the value for Z is set to 0.
      - def isHoleEnabled(self, obj, base, sub)
        doc: isHoleEnabled(obj, base, sub) ... return true if hole is enabled.
      - def opExecute(self, obj)
        doc: opExecute(obj) ... processes all Base features and Locations and collects them in a list of positions and radii which is then passed to circularHoleExecute(obj, holes). Do not overwrite, implement circularHoleExecute(obj, holes) instead.
      - def circularHoleExecute(self, obj, holes)
        doc: circularHoleExecute(obj, holes) ... implement processing of holes. holes is a list of dictionaries with 'x', 'y' and 'r' specified for each hole. Note that for Vertexes, non-circular Edges and Locations r=0. Must be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def findAllHoles(self, obj, selection=[])
        doc: findAllHoles(obj) ... find all holes of all base or selected models and assign as features.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
```

```text
functions:
  - def endPoints(edgeOrWire)
    doc: endPoints(edgeOrWire) ... return the first and last point of the wire or the edge, assuming the argument is not a closed wire.

    doc: includesPoint(p, pts) ... answer True if the collection of pts includes the point p
  - def selectOffsetWire(feature, wires)
    doc: selectOffsetWire(feature, wires) ... returns the Wire in wires which is does not intersect with feature
  - def extendWire(feature, wire, length)
    doc: extendWire(wire, length) ... return a closed Wire which extends wire by length
  - def createExtension(obj, extObj, extFeature, extSub)

    doc: readObjExtensionFeature(obj)... Return three item string tuples (base name, feature, subfeature) extracted from obj.ExtensionFeature
  - def getExtensions(obj)
  - def setExtensions(obj, extensions)
  - def getStandardAngle(x, y)
    doc: getStandardAngle(x, y)... Return standard degree angle given x and y values of vector.
  - def arcAdjustmentAngle(arc1, arc2)
    doc: arcAdjustmentAngle(arc1, arc2)... Return adjustment angle to apply to arc2 in order to align it with arc1. Arcs must have same center point.
  - def initialize_properties(obj)
    doc: initialize_properties(obj)... Adds feature properties to object argument
  - def set_default_property_values(obj, job)
    doc: set_default_property_values(obj, job) ... set default values for feature properties
  - def SetupProperties()
    doc: SetupProperties()... Returns list of feature property names
  - def getExtendOutlineFace(base_shape, face, extension, remHoles=False, offset_tolerance=0.0001)
    doc: getExtendOutlineFace(obj, base_shape, face, extension, remHoles) ... Creates an extended face for the pocket, taking into consideration lateral collision with the greater base shape. Arguments are: parent base shape of face, target face, extension magnitude, remove holes boolean, offset tolerance = 1e-4 default The default value of 1e-4 for offset tolerance is the same default value at getOffsetArea() function definition. Return is an all access face extending the specified extension value from 
  - def getWaterlineFace(base_shape, face)
    doc: getWaterlineFace(base_shape, face) ... Creates a waterline extension face for the target face, taking into consideration the greater base shape. Arguments are: parent base shape and target face. Return is a waterline face at height of the target face.
classes:
  class Extension(object)
    methods:
      - def getSubLink(self)
      - def getExtensionFaces(self, extensionWire)
        doc: getExtensionFace(extensionWire)... A public helper method to retrieve the requested extension as a face, rather than a wire because some extensions require a face shape for definition that allows for two wires for boundary definition.
      - def getWire(self)
        doc: getWire()... Public method to retrieve the extension area, pertaining to the feature and sub element provided at class instantiation, as a closed wire. If no closed wire is possible, a `None` value is returned.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Gui/Base.py
functions:
  - def Create(res)
    doc: Create(res) ... generic implementation of a create function. res is an instance of CommandResources. It is not expected that the user invokes this function directly, but calls the Activated() function of the Command object that is created in each operations Gui implementation.
  - def SetupOperation(name, objFactory, opPageClass, pixmap, menuText, toolTip, setupProperties=None)
    doc: SetupOperation(name, objFactory, opPageClass, pixmap, menuText, toolTip, setupProperties=None) Creates an instance of CommandPathOp with the given parameters and registers the command with FreeCAD-compatible runtime. When activated it creates a model with proxy (by invoking objFactory), assigns a view provider to it (see ViewProvider in this module) and starts the editor specifically for this operation (driven by opPageClass). This is an internal function that is automatically called by the init
classes:
  class ViewProvider(object)
    doc: Generic view provider for path objects. Deducts the icon name from operation name, brings up the TaskPanel with pages corresponding to the operation's opFeatures() and forwards property change notifications to the page controllers.
    methods:
      - def attach(self, vobj)
      - def isSelected(self)
        doc: Check if this operation is currently selected.
      - def updateWorkplaneVisualization(self)
        doc: Update the workplane coordinate system visualization based on the operation Placement.
      - def deleteObjectsOnReject(self)
        doc: deleteObjectsOnReject() ... return true if all objects should be created if the user hits cancel. This is used during the initial edit session, if the user does not press OK, it is assumed they've changed their mind about creating the operation.
      - def setDeleteObjectsOnReject(self, state=False)
      - def setEdit(self, vobj=None, mode=0)
        doc: setEdit(vobj, mode=0) ... initiate editing of receivers model.
      - def setupTaskPanel(self, panel)
        doc: setupTaskPanel(panel) ... internal function to start the editor.
      - def clearTaskPanel(self)
        doc: clearTaskPanel() ... internal callback function when editing has finished.
      - def unsetEdit(self, arg1, arg2)
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: dumps() ... callback before receiver is saved to a file. Returns a dictionary with the receiver's resources as strings.
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: loads(state) ... callback on restoring a saved instance, pendant to dumps() state is the dictionary returned by dumps().
      - def getIcon(self)
        doc: getIcon() ... the icon used in the object tree
      - def getTaskPanelOpPage(self, obj)
        doc: getTaskPanelOpPage(obj) ... use the stored information to instantiate the receiver op's page controller.
      - def getSelectionFactory(self)
        doc: getSelectionFactory() ... return a factory function that can be used to create the selection observer.
      - def onChanged(self, vobj, prop)
        doc: onChanged(vobj, prop) ... callback when a view property changes.
      - def updateData(self, obj, prop)
        doc: updateData(obj, prop) ... callback whenever a property of the receiver's model is assigned. The callback is forwarded to the task panel - in case an editing session is ongoing.
      - def onDelete(self, vobj, arg2=None)
      - def setupContextMenu(self, vobj, menu)
  class TaskPanelPage(object)
    doc: Base class for all task panel pages.
    methods:
      - def show_error_message(self, title, message)
      - def setParent(self, parent)
        doc: setParent() ... used to transfer parent object link to child class. Do not overwrite.
      - def onDirtyChanged(self, callback)
        doc: onDirtyChanged(callback) ... set callback when dirty state changes.
      - def setDirty(self)
        doc: setDirty() ... mark receiver as dirty, causing the model to be recalculated if OK or Apply is pressed.
      - def setClean(self)
        doc: setClean() ... mark receiver as clean, indicating there is no need to recalculate the model even if the user presses OK or Apply.
      - def pageGetFields(self)
        doc: pageGetFields() ... internal callback. Do not overwrite, implement getFields(obj) instead.
      - def pageSetFields(self)
        doc: pageSetFields() ... internal callback. Do not overwrite, implement setFields(obj) instead.
      - def pageCleanup(self)
        doc: pageCleanup() ... internal callback. Do not overwrite, implement cleanupPage(obj) instead.
      - def pageRegisterSignalHandlers(self)
        doc: pageRegisterSignalHandlers() .. internal callback. Registers a callback for all signals returned by getSignalsForUpdate(obj). Do not overwrite, implement getSignalsForUpdate(obj) and/or registerSignalHandlers(obj) instead.
      - def pageUpdateData(self, obj, prop)
        doc: pageUpdateData(obj, prop) ... internal callback. Do not overwrite, implement updateData(obj) instead.
      - def setTitle(self, title)
        doc: setTitle(title) ... sets a title for the page.
      - def getTitle(self, obj)
        doc: getTitle(obj) ... return title to be used for the receiver page. The default implementation returns what was previously set with setTitle(title). Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def setIcon(self, icon)
        doc: setIcon(icon) ... sets the icon for the page.
      - def getIcon(self, obj)
        doc: getIcon(obj) ... return icon for page or None. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def initPage(self, obj)
        doc: initPage(obj) ... overwrite to customize UI for specific model. Note that this function is invoked after all page controllers have been created. Should be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def cleanupPage(self, obj)
        doc: cleanupPage(obj) ... overwrite to perform any cleanup tasks before page is destroyed. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def modifyStandardButtons(self, buttonBox)
        doc: modifyStandardButtons(buttonBox) ... overwrite if the task panel standard buttons need to be modified. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def getForm(self)
        doc: getForm() ... return UI form for this page. Must be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def getFields(self, obj)
        doc: getFields(obj) ... overwrite to transfer values from UI to obj's properties. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def setFields(self, obj)
        doc: setFields(obj) ... overwrite to transfer obj's property values to UI. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def getSignalsForUpdate(self, obj)
        doc: getSignalsForUpdate(obj) ... return signals which, when triggered, cause the receiver to update the model. See also registerSignalHandlers(obj) Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def registerSignalHandlers(self, obj)
        doc: registerSignalHandlers(obj) ... overwrite to register custom signal handlers. In case an update of a model is not the desired operation of a signal invocation (see getSignalsForUpdate(obj)) this function can be used to register signal handlers manually. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def updateData(self, obj, prop)
        doc: updateData(obj, prop) ... overwrite if the receiver needs to react to property changes that might not have been caused by the receiver itself. Sometimes a model will recalculate properties based on a change of another property. In order to keep the UI up to date with such changes this function can be used. Please note that the callback is synchrono
      - def updateSelection(self, obj, sel)
        doc: updateSelection(obj, sel) ... overwrite to customize UI depending on current selection. Can safely be overwritten by subclasses.
      - def selectInComboBox(self, name, combo)
        doc: selectInComboBox(name, combo) ... helper function to select a specific value in a combo box.
      - def populateCombobox(self, form, enumTups, comboBoxesPropertyMap)
        doc: populateCombobox(form, enumTups, comboBoxesPropertyMap) ... proxy for PathGuiUtil.populateCombobox()
      - def tcComboChanged(self, newIndex)
      - def updateToolControllerEditorVisibility(self)
      - def resetToolController(self, job, tc)
      - def copyToolController(self)
      - def tcEditorChanged(self)
      - def resetTCCombo(self)
      - def setupToolController(self, obj=None, combo=None)
        doc: setupToolController(obj, combo) ... helper function to setup obj's ToolController in the given combo box.
      - def updateToolController(self, obj, combo)
        doc: updateToolController(obj, combo) ... helper function to update obj's ToolController property if a different one has been selected in the combo box.
      - def setupCoolant(self, obj, combo)
        doc: setupCoolant(obj, combo) ... helper function to setup obj's Coolant option.
      - def updateCoolant(self, obj, combo)
        doc: updateCoolant(obj, combo) ... helper function to update obj's Coolant property if a different one has been selected in the combo box.
      - def updatePanelVisibility(self, panelTitle, obj)
        doc: updatePanelVisibility(panelTitle, obj) ... Function to call the `updateVisibility()` GUI method of the page whose panel title is as indicated.

  class TaskPanel(object)
    doc: Generic TaskPanel implementation handling the standard Path operation layout. This class only implements the framework and takes care of bringing all pages up and down in a controller fashion. It implements the standard editor behaviour for OK, Cancel and Apply and tracks if the model is still in sync with the UI. However, all display and processing of fields is handled by the page controllers which are managed in a list. All event callbacks and framework actions are forwarded to the page contro
    methods:
      - def isDirty(self)
        doc: isDirty() ... returns true if the model is not in sync with the UI anymore.
      - def setClean(self)
        doc: setClean() ... set the receiver and all its pages clean.
      - def accept(self, resetEdit=True)
        doc: accept() ... callback invoked when user presses the task panel OK button.
      - def reject(self, resetEdit=True)
        doc: reject() ... callback invoked when user presses the task panel Cancel button.
      - def preCleanup(self)
      - def cleanup(self, resetEdit)
        doc: cleanup() ... implements common cleanup tasks.
      - def pageDirtyChanged(self, page)
        doc: pageDirtyChanged(page) ... internal callback
      - def clicked(self, button)
        doc: clicked(button) ... callback invoked when the user presses any of the task panel buttons.
      - def modifyStandardButtons(self, buttonBox)
        doc: modifyStandarButtons(buttonBox) ... callback in case the task panel buttons need to be modified.
      - def panelGetFields(self)
        doc: panelGetFields() ... invoked to trigger a complete transfer of UI data to the model.
      - def panelSetFields(self)
        doc: panelSetFields() ... invoked to trigger a complete transfer of the model's properties to the UI.
      - def panelCleanup(self)
        doc: panelCleanup() ... invoked before the receiver is destroyed.
      - def open(self)
        doc: open() ... callback invoked when the task panel is opened.
      - def getStandardButtons(self)
        doc: getStandardButtons() ... returns the Buttons for the task panel.
      - def setupUi(self)
        doc: setupUi() ... internal function to initialise all pages.
      - def updateData(self, obj, prop)
        doc: updateDate(obj, prop) ... callback invoked whenever a model's property is assigned a value.
      - def needsFullSpace(self)
      - def updateSelection(self)
      - def addSelection(self, doc, obj, sub, pnt)
      - def removeSelection(self, doc, obj, sub)
      - def setSelection(self, doc)
      - def clearSelection(self, doc)
  class CommandSetStartPoint
    doc: Command to set the start point for an operation.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
      - def IsActive(self)
      - def setpoint(self, point, o)
      - def Activated(self)
  class CommandPathOp
    doc: Generic, data driven implementation of a Path operation creation command. Instances of this class are stored in all Path operation Gui modules and can be used to create said operations with view providers and all.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
      - def IsActive(self)
      - def Activated(self)
      - def setJob(self, job)
  class CommandResources
    doc: POD class to hold command specific resources.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Pocket.py
functions:
  - def SetupProperties()
  - def Create(name, obj=None, parentJob=None)
    doc: Create(name) ... Creates and returns a Pocket operation.
classes:
  class ObjectPocket(PathPocketBase.ObjectPocket)
    doc: Proxy object for Pocket operation.
    methods:
      - def pocketOpFeatures(self, obj)
      - def initPocketOp(self, obj)
        doc: initPocketOp(obj) ... setup receiver
      - def propertyEnumerations(self, dataType='data')
        doc: propertyEnumerations(dataType="data")... return property enumeration lists of specified dataType. Args: dataType = 'data', 'raw', 'translated' Notes: 'data' is list of internal string literals used in code 'raw' is list of (translated_text, data_string) tuples 'translated' is list of translated string literals
      - def opOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: opOnDocumentRestored(obj) ... adds the properties if they doesn't exist.
      - def pocketInvertExtraOffset(self)
      - def opUpdateDepths(self, obj)
        doc: opUpdateDepths(obj) ... Implement special depths calculation.
      - def areaOpShapes(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpShapes(obj) ... return shapes representing the solids to be removed.
      - def areaOpSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: areaOpSetDefaultValues(obj, job) ... set default values
      - def calculateAdaptivePocket(self, obj, base, subObjTups)
        doc: calculateAdaptivePocket(obj, base, subObjTups) Orient multiple faces around common facial center of mass. Identify edges that are connections for adjacent faces. Attempt to separate unconnected edges into top and bottom loops of the pocket. Trim the top and bottom of the pocket if available and requested. return: tuple with pocket shape information
      - def orderFacesAroundCenterOfMass(self, subObjTups)
        doc: orderFacesAroundCenterOfMass(subObjTups) Order list of faces by center of mass in angular order around average center of mass for all faces. Positive X-axis is zero degrees. return: subObjTups [ordered/sorted]
      - def findSharedEdges(self, subObjTups)
        doc: findSharedEdges(self, subObjTups) Find connected edges given a group of faces
      - def identifyUnconnectedEdges(self, subObjTups, touching)
        doc: identifyUnconnectedEdges(subObjTups, touching) Categorize unconnected edges into two groups, if possible: low and high
      - def hasCommonVertex(self, edge1, edge2, show=False)
        doc: findCommonVertexIndexes(edge1, edge2, show=False) Compare vertexes of two edges to identify a common vertex. Returns the vertex index of edge1 to which edge2 is connected
      - def groupConnectedEdges(self, holding)
        doc: groupConnectedEdges(self, holding) Take edges and determine which are connected. Group connected chains/loops into: low and high
      - def getMinMaxOfFaces(self, Faces)
        doc: getMinMaxOfFaces(Faces) return the zmin and zmax values for given set of faces or edges.
```

```text
    doc: Create(name) ... Creates and returns a Pocket operation.
classes:
  class ObjectPocket(PathPocketBase.ObjectPocket)
    doc: Proxy object for Pocket operation.
    methods:
      - def areaOpFeatures(self, obj)
      - def removeHoles(self, solid, face)
        doc: removeHoles(solid, face) ... Remove hole wires from a face, keeping outer wire and boss wires. Uses a cross-section algorithm: sections the solid slightly above the face level. Wires that appear in the section are bosses (material above). Wires that don't appear are holes (voids). Args: solid: The parent solid object face: The face to process Retur
      - def initPocketOp(self, obj)
        doc: initPocketOp(obj) ... setup receiver
      - def areaOpOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: opOnDocumentRestored(obj) ... adds the UseOutline property if it doesn't exist.
      - def pocketInvertExtraOffset(self)
      - def areaOpSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: areaOpSetDefaultValues(obj, job) ... set default values
      - def areaOpShapes(self, obj)
        doc: areaOpShapes(obj) ... return shapes representing the solids to be removed.
      - def classifySubEdge(self, bs, sub)
        doc: classifySubFace(bs, sub)... Given a base and a sub-feature name, returns True if the sub-feature is a horizontal edge.
      - def classifySubFace(self, bs, sub)
        doc: classifySubFace(bs, sub)... Given a base and a sub-feature name, returns True if the sub-feature is a horizontally or vertically oriented flat face.
```

```text
functions:
  - def getExtrudedShape(wire)

  - def pathGeomToLinesPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)
    doc: pathGeomToLinesPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)... Convert a compound set of sequential line segments to directionally-oriented collinear groupings.
  - def pathGeomToZigzagPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)
    doc: _pathGeomToZigzagPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)... Convert a compound set of sequential line segments to directionally-oriented collinear groupings with a ZigZag directional indicator included for each collinear group.
  - def pathGeomToCircularPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)
    doc: pathGeomToCircularPointSet(self, obj, compGeoShp)... Convert a compound set of arcs/circles to a set of directionally-oriented arc end points and the corresponding center point.
  - def pathGeomToSpiralPointSet(obj, compGeoShp)
    doc: _pathGeomToSpiralPointSet(obj, compGeoShp)... Convert a compound set of sequential line segments to directional, connected groupings.
  - def pathGeomToOffsetPointSet(obj, compGeoShp)
    doc: pathGeomToOffsetPointSet(obj, compGeoShp)... Convert a compound set of 3D profile segmented wires to 2D segments, applying linear optimization.
  - def makeExtendedBoundBox(wBB, bbBfr, zDep)
classes:
  class PathGeometryGenerator
    doc: Creates a path geometry shape from an assigned pattern for conversion to tool paths. PathGeometryGenerator(obj, shape, pattern) `obj` is the operation object, `shape` is the horizontal planar shape object, and `pattern` is the name of the geometric pattern to apply. First, call the getCenterOfPattern() method for the CenterOfMass for patterns allowing a custom center. Next, call the generatePathGeometry() method to request the path geometry shape.
    methods:
      - def setDebugObjectsGroup(self, tmpGrpObject)
        doc: setDebugObjectsGroup(tmpGrpObject)... Pass the temporary object group to show temporary construction objects
      - def getCenterOfPattern(self)
        doc: getCenterOfPattern()... Returns the Center Of Mass for the current class instance.
      - def generatePathGeometry(self)
        doc: generatePathGeometry()... Call this function to obtain the path geometry shape, generated by this class.
  class ProcessSelectedFaces
    doc: ProcessSelectedFaces(JOB, obj) class. This class processes the `obj.Base` object for selected geometery. Calling the preProcessModel(module) method returns two compound objects as a tuple: (FACES, VOIDS) or False.
    methods:
      - def PathSurface(self)
      - def PathWaterline(self)
      - def setShowDebugObjects(self, grpObj, val)
      - def preProcessModel(self, module)
      - def findUnifiedRegions(self, shapeAndIndexTuples, useAreaImplementation=True)
        doc: Wrapper around area and wire based region unification implementations.
  class FindUnifiedRegions
    doc: FindUnifiedRegions() This class requires a list of face shapes. It finds the unified horizontal unified regions, if they exist.
    methods:
      - def setTempGroup(self, grpObj)
        doc: setTempGroup(grpObj)... For debugging, pass temporary object group.
      - def getUnifiedRegions(self)
        doc: getUnifiedRegions()... Returns a list of unified regions from list of tuples (faceShape, faceIndex) received at instantiation of the class object.
      - def getInternalFeatures(self)
        doc: getInternalFeatures()... Returns internal features identified after calling getUnifiedRegions().
  class OCL_Tool
    doc: The OCL_Tool class is designed to translate a FreeCAD-compatible runtime standard ToolBit shape in the active Tool Controller, into an OCL tool type.
    methods:
      - def getOclTool(self)
        doc: getOclTool()... Call this method after class instantiation to return OCL tool object.
      - def useTiltCutter(self)
        doc: useTiltCutter()... Call this method after getOclTool() method to return status of cutter tilt availability - generally this is for a ball end mill.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Util.py
functions:
  - def debugEdge(label, e)
    doc: debugEdge(label, e) ... prints a python statement to create e Currently lines and arcs are supported.
  - def makeWires(inEdges)
    doc: makeWires ... function to make non-forking wires from a collection of edges
  - def debugWire(label, w)
    doc: debugWire(label, w) ... prints python statements for all edges of w to be added to the object tree in a group.
  - def getCoincideTolerance(edges)
    doc: getCoincideTolerance(edges) ... Returns actual tolerance of edges connection in wire Assumes the edges are in an order so they can be connected. Return None if any edge length less than defined tolerance Note: Default tolerance of methods Part.sortEdges() and Part.__sortEdges__() is Base.Precision.confusion() => 1e-7
  - def isWireClockwise(w)
    doc: isWireClockwise(w) ... returns True if the wire winds clockwise.
  - def orientWire(w, forward=True)
    doc: orientWire(w, forward=True) ... orients given wire in a specific direction. If forward = True (the default) the wire is oriented clockwise, looking down the negative Z axis. If forward = False the wire is oriented counter clockwise. If forward = None the orientation is determined by the order in which the edges appear in the wire.
  - def approximateWire(wire, tolerance=0.01)
    doc: approximateWire approximates any non-line/arc edges with lines or arcs. Edges that are lines or circular arcs are kept as-is. tolerance: Deflection tolerance for approximation. Must be positive if wire contains non-line/arc edges. Returns the wire with non-line/arc edges replaced by arcs and line segments.
  - def offsetWire(wire, base, offset, forward, Side=None, tolerance=0.01)
    doc: offsetWire ... offsets the wire away from base and orients the wire accordingly. The function tries to avoid most of the pitfalls of Part.makeOffset2D which is possible because all offsetting happens in the XY plane. tolerance: Deflection tolerance for discretization. Must be positive if wire contains non-line/arc edges.
  - def getClearedAreas(currentOp, bbox)
    doc: Returns the cleared area relevant to the operation - currentOp: the operation we are checking for. Only operations performed before this operation will be considered - bbox: the cleared region is only generated where it is close enough to impact the bbox region Operations whose Workplane differs from the current op's are skipped: each op's Path stores X/Y/Z in the rotated workplane frame used at generation time, and projecting cleared area between non-coplanar workplanes has no meaningful 2D int
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Op/Vcarve.py
functions:
  - def getReversedEdge(edge)
  - def generateVirtualBackTrackEdges(positionHistory, nextEdge, tolerance) -> list
    doc: Generate a list of "virtual edges" to backtrack using normal G1 moves instead lifting toolbit and repositioning using G0 to get to beginning of nextEdge. Those virtual edges are either already carved or are part of nextEdge anyway so it's safe to follow them without lifting toolbit. This approach makes carving a lot of faster.
  - def canSkipRepositioning(positionHistory, newPosition, tolerance)
    doc: Calculate if it makes sense to raise head to safe height and reposition before starting to cut another edge
  - def SetupProperties()
  - def Create(name, obj=None, parentJob=None)
    doc: Create(name) ... Creates and returns a Vcarve operation.
classes:
  class ObjectVcarve(PathEngraveBase.ObjectOp)
    doc: Proxy class for Vcarve operation.
    methods:
      - def opFeatures(self, obj)
        doc: opFeatures(obj) ... return all standard features and edges based geometries
      - def setupAdditionalProperties(self, obj)
      - def initOperation(self, obj)
        doc: initOperation(obj) ... create vcarve specific properties.
      - def opOnDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def buildMedialWires(self, obj, faces)
        doc: constructs a medial axis path using openvoronoi :returns: dictionary - each face object is a key containing list of wires
      - def buildCommandList(self, obj, faces)
        doc: Build command list to cut wires - based on voronoi wire list from buildMedialWires. :returns: list of Path.Command objects
      - def opExecute(self, obj)
        doc: opExecute(obj) ... process engraving operation
      - def opUpdateDepths(self, obj)
        doc: updateDepths(obj) ... engraving is always done at the top most z-value
      - def opSetDefaultValues(self, obj, job)
        doc: opSetDefaultValues(obj) ... set depths for vcarving
      - def isToolSupported(self, obj, tool)
        doc: isToolSupported(obj, tool) ... returns True if v-carve op can work with tool.
      - def debugVoronoiMedial(self, obj)
        doc: Debug function to display calculated voronoi medial wires
      - def debugVoronoiEdges(self, obj)
        doc: Debug function to display calculated voronoi edges
```

```text
  class DrillCycleExpander

    methods:

        doc: Expand a single drill cycle command into basic movements. Args: command: Path.Command object (e.g., Path.Command("G81", {"X": 10.0, "Y": 10.0, "Z": -5.0, "R": 2.0, "F": 100.0})) Returns: List of expanded Path.Command objects
      - def expand_commands(self, commands: List[Path.Command]) -> List[Path.Command]

      - def expand_path(self, path: Path.Path) -> Path.Path
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/GcodeProcessingUtils.py
functions:
  - def insert_line_numbers(gcode: List[str], start: int=10, increment: int=10) -> List[str]
    doc: Insert line numbers (N-codes) into G-code lines. Args: gcode: List of G-code strings start: Starting line number (default: 10) increment: Line number increment (default: 10) Returns: List of G-code strings with line numbers inserted
  - def suppress_redundant_axes_words(gcode: List[str]) -> List[str]
    doc: Suppress redundant axis and feed rate words by tracking current machine state. Removes axis words where the value matches the current machine position, and F words where the feed rate matches the current feed rate. Args: gcode: List of G-code strings Returns: List of G-code strings with redundant words suppressed
  - def filter_inefficient_moves(gcode: List[str]) -> List[str]
    doc: Filter out inefficient or redundant moves from G-code. Removes unnecessary rapid (G0) moves by collapsing chains that only move along single axes or within linear/rotary groups. Args: gcode: List of G-code strings Returns: List of G-code strings with inefficient moves filtered out
  - def deduplicate_repeated_commands(gcode: List[str]) -> List[str]
    doc: Deduplicate consecutive repeated commands from G-code. Removes the command word from consecutive commands of the same type, keeping only the parameters. This is modal G-code behavior. Example: G1 X10 Y20 G1 X30 Y40 -> X30 Y40 (G1 removed) G1 X50 Y60 -> X50 Y60 (G1 removed) G0 Z5 -> G0 Z5 (different command, kept) Args: gcode: List of G-code strings Returns: List of G-code strings with modal command words removed
classes:
  class NumberGenerator
    doc: Generate a sequence of line numbers with configurable formatting. Args: template: Format string for the line number (e.g., 'N{:04d}') start: Starting number for the sequence (default: 1) increment: Step size for the sequence (default: 1)
    methods:
      - def get(self) -> str
        doc: Get the next number in the sequence and format it according to the template.
      - def reset(self) -> None
        doc: Reset the sequence to the starting number.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/PostList.py
functions:
  - def needsTcOp(oldTc: Any, newTc: Any) -> bool
  - def create_fixture_setup(processor: Any, order: int, fixture: str) -> Postable
  - def build_postlist_by_fixture(processor: Any) -> list
    doc: Build postlist ordered by fixture (work coordinate system). Wraps operations early to prevent accessing live document objects, which can mark operations dirty during postprocessing. Args: processor: The postprocessor object with job and operations Returns: List of tuples: [(fixture_name, [postable_items])]
  - def build_postlist_by_tool(processor: Any) -> list
    doc: Build postlist ordered by tool. Groups operations by tool controller to minimize tool changes. Wraps operations early to prevent accessing live document objects. Args: processor: The postprocessor object with job and operations Returns: List of tuples: [(tool_name, [postable_items])]
  - def build_postlist_by_operation(processor: Any) -> list
    doc: Build postlist ordered by operation. Creates separate sections for each operation with all fixtures. Wraps operations early to prevent accessing live document objects. Args: processor: The postprocessor object with job and operations Returns: List of tuples: [(operation_name, [postable_items])]
  - def buildPostList(processor: Any) -> List[Tuple[str, List]]
    doc: Build ordered list of postables for postprocessing. Determines ordering strategy based on job.OrderOutputBy and delegates to appropriate builder function. All operations are wrapped early to prevent accessing live document objects. Args: processor: The postprocessor object with job and operations Returns: List of tuples: [(section_name, [postable_items])]
  - def apply_early_tool_prep(postlist: List[Tuple[str, List]]) -> List[Tuple[str, List]]
    doc: Apply early tool preparation optimization to the postlist. This function modifies tool change commands to enable early tool preparation: - Always outputs tool changes as "Tn M6" (tool number followed by change command) - Additionally emits standalone "Tn" prep commands immediately after the previous M6 to allow the machine to prepare the next tool while the current tool is working Example output: T4 M6 <- change to tool 4 T5 <- prep tool 5 early (while T4 is working) <gcode> <- operations with T
classes:
  class Postable
    doc: Uniform wrapper for every item that passes through the post-processing pipeline. Replaces the three ad-hoc mock classes (_FixtureSetupObject, _CommandObject, _RotationSetupObject) and raw document objects. All downstream code can rely on this single interface instead of duck-typing via hasattr. The wrapper copies essential data (path, label) to prevent postprocessing from accidentally marking the original FreeCAD-compatible runtime document objects dirty. Attributes: item_type: One of "operation
    methods:
      - def Path(self)
      - def Path(self, value)
      - def Label(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/Processor.py
functions:
  - def needsTcOp(oldTc, newTc)
classes:
  class PostProcessorFactory
    doc: Factory class for creating post processors.
    methods:
      - def get_post_processor(job, postname)
  class PostProcessor
    doc: Base Class. All non-legacy postprocessors should inherit from this class.
    methods:
      - def get_common_property_schema(cls) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
        doc: Return schema for common properties that all postprocessors should expose. These are properties that apply to all postprocessors and define fundamental capabilities or behaviors. Subclasses can override this to change defaults but should generally include all common properties. Returns: List of common property schema dictionaries.
      - def get_property_schema(cls) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
        doc: Return schema describing configurable properties for this postprocessor. This method should be overridden by subclasses to add postprocessor-specific properties. The common properties from get_common_property_schema() are automatically included. Each property is a dictionary with the following keys: - name: str - Internal property name (used as key
      - def get_full_property_schema(cls) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
        doc: Return the complete property schema including both common and postprocessor-specific properties. This method combines common properties with postprocessor-specific properties. The machine editor should call this method to get all properties. Returns: List of all property schema dictionaries (common + specific).
      - def exists(cls, processor)
      - def tooltip(self)
        doc: Get the tooltip text for the post processor.
      - def tooltipArgs(self) -> FormatHelp
      - def units(self)
        doc: Get the units used by the post processor.
      - def get_file_extension(self)
        doc: Return the effective file extension for output files. Legacy PP's use this method Returns: Extension string without leading dot (e.g. 'sbp', 'gcode', 'nc').
      - def build_configuration_bundle(self, overrides=None)
        doc: Build the complete postprocessor configuration as a flat dict. Pure computation with no side effects - suitable for testing. Merges in priority order: 1. Machine postprocessor_properties (from .fcm file) 2. Schema defaults (for keys not already present) 3. Overrides (from job or caller) 4. other job configuration elements (author, comment, selected
      - def apply_configuration_bundle(self, overrides=None)
        doc: Build the bundle and apply it everywhere. 1. Calls build_configuration_bundle() to get the final dict. 2. Writes the result back to machine.postprocessor_properties. 3. Merges machine output config into self.values (header, comments, etc.). 4. Syncs every bundle key into self.values as UPPERCASE so that all consumers (G-code generation, sanity chec
      - def use_machine_state(self)
        doc: Initialize machine_state for a looping over postables. resets to None on finish
      - def dump_sections(self, msg, sections)
        doc: Print the sections for development/debugging
      - def export2(self) -> Union[None, GCodeSections]
        doc: Process jobs through all postprocessing stages to produce final G-code. Assumes Stage 0 (Configuration) is complete. Stages: 0. Pre-processing Dialog - Collect user input before processing 1. Ordering - Build ordered list of postables 2. Command Expansion - Canned cycles, arc splitting 3. Command Conversion - Convert Path.Commands to G-code strings
      - def export(self) -> Union[None, GCodeSections]
        doc: Process the parser arguments, then postprocess the 'postables'.
      - def init_arguments(self, values: Values, argument_defaults: Defaults, arguments_visible: Visible) -> Parser
        doc: Initialize the shared argument definitions.
      - def init_argument_defaults(self, argument_defaults: Defaults) -> None
        doc: Initialize which arguments (in a pair) are shown as the default argument.
      - def init_arguments_visible(self, arguments_visible: Visible) -> None
        doc: Initialize which argument pairs are visible in TOOLTIP_ARGS.
      - def init_values(self, values: Values) -> None
        doc: Initialize values that are used throughout the postprocessor.
      - def process_arguments(self) -> Tuple[bool, ParserArgs]
        doc: Process any arguments to the postprocessor.
      - def process_postables(self) -> GCodeSections
        doc: Postprocess the 'postables' in the job to g code sections.
      - def reinitialize(self) -> None
        doc: Initialize or reinitialize the 'core' data structures for the postprocessor.
      - def remote_post(self, gcode_sections)
        doc: Hook for remote posting functionality. This method is called after all G-code has been generated and written. Base implementation does nothing, but subclasses can override to implement remote posting, file uploads, or other post-processing actions. Args: gcode_sections: List of (section_name, gcode) tuples containing all generated G-code
      - def pre_processing_dialog(self)
        doc: Hook for pre-processing dialog functionality. This method is called before any post-processing begins, allowing post processors to collect user input through dialogs or other interactive means. Base implementation does nothing, but subclasses can override to implement configuration dialogs, validation checks, or user input collection. When the unif
      - def get_sanity_checks(self, job)
        doc: Hook for postprocessor-specific sanity checks. This method allows postprocessors to define custom validation rules specific to their machine capabilities, configuration requirements, or operational constraints. These checks are integrated into the CAM_QuickValidation system and displayed alongside generic checks. Args: job: FreeCAD-compatible runti
      - def convert_command_to_gcode(self, command: Path.Command) -> str
        doc: Converts a single-line command to gcode. Return one-string use " " to join multiple-lines Return None or "" for nothing. This method dispatches to specialized hook methods based on command type. Derived postprocessors can override individual hook methods to customize specific command handling without reimplementing the entire function. Hook methods
      - def format_parameter(self, param_name, value)
  class WrapperPost(PostProcessor)
    doc: Wrapper class for old post processors that are scripts.
    methods:
      - def load_script(self)
        doc: Dynamically load the script as a module.
      - def export(self)
        doc: Dynamically reload the module for the export to ensure up-to-date usage.
      - def tooltip(self)
      - def tooltipArgs(self)
      - def units(self)
```

```text
  - def output_start_bcnc(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, obj) -> None
    doc: Output the starting BCNC header.
  - def output_tool_list(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, objectslist) -> None
    doc: Output a list of the tools used in the objects.
  - def output_tool_return(values: Values, gcode: Gcode) -> None
    doc: Output the tool return block.
  - def output_units(values: Values, gcode: Gcode) -> None
    doc: Verify if PREAMBLE or SAFETYBLOCK have changed UNITS.
  - def export_common(values: Values, objectslist, filename: str) -> str
    doc: Do the common parts of postprocessing the objects in objectslist to filename.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/UtilsParse.py
functions:
  - def check_for_an_adaptive_op(values: Values, command: str, command_line: CommandLine, adaptive_op_variables: Tuple[bool, float, float]) -> str
    doc: Check to see if the current command is an adaptive op.
  - def check_for_drill_translate(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, command_line: CommandLine, params: PathParameters, motion_location: PathParameters, drill_retract_mode: str) -> bool
    doc: Check for drill commands to translate.
  - def check_for_machine_specific_commands(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str) -> None
    doc: Check for comments containing machine-specific commands.
  - def check_for_spindle_wait(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, command_line: CommandLine) -> None
    doc: Check for commands that might need a wait command after them.
  - def check_for_suppressed_commands(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, command_line: CommandLine) -> bool
    doc: Check for commands that will be suppressed.
  - def check_for_tlo(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, params: PathParameters) -> None
    doc: Output a tool length command if USE_TLO is True.
  - def check_for_tool_change(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, command_line: CommandLine) -> bool
    doc: Check for a tool change.
  - def create_comment(values: Values, comment_string: str) -> str
    doc: Create a comment from a string using the correct comment symbol.
  - def default_axis_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process an axis parameter.
  - def default_D_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process the D parameter.
  - def default_F_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process the F parameter.
  - def default_int_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process a parameter that is treated like an integer.
  - def default_length_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process a parameter that is treated like a length.
  - def default_P_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process the P parameter.
  - def default_Q_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process the Q parameter.
  - def default_rotary_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process a rotarty parameter (such as A, B, and C).
  - def default_S_parameter(values: Values, command: str, param: str, param_value: PathParameter, parameters: PathParameters, current_location: PathParameters) -> str
    doc: Process the S parameter.
  - def determine_adaptive_op(values: Values, pathobj) -> Tuple[bool, float, float]
    doc: Determine if the pathobj contains an Adaptive operation.
  - def drill_translate(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, params: PathParameters, motion_location: PathParameters, drill_retract_mode: str) -> None
    doc: Translate drill cycles. Currently only cycles in XY are provided (G17). XZ (G18) and YZ (G19) are not dealt with. In other words only Z drilling can be translated. This is a compatibility wrapper around DrillCycleExpander.
  - def format_command_line(values: Values, command_line: CommandLine) -> str
    doc: Construct the command line for the final output.
  - def format_for_axis(values: Values, number) -> str
    doc: Format a number using the precision for an axis value.
  - def format_for_feed(values: Values, number) -> str
    doc: Format a number using the precision for a feed rate.
  - def format_for_spindle(values: Values, number) -> str
    doc: Format a number using the precision for a spindle speed.
  - def init_parameter_functions(parameter_functions: Dict[str, ParameterFunction]) -> None
    doc: Initialize a list of parameter functions. These functions are called in the UtilsParse.parse_a_path function to return the appropriate parameter value.
  - def linenumber(values: Values, space: Union[str, None]=None) -> str
    doc: Output the next line number if appropriate.
  - def output_G73_G83_drill_moves(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, params: PathParameters, drill_z: float, retract_z: float, F_feedrate: str, G0_retract_z: str) -> None
    doc: Output the movement G code for G73 and G83.
  - def output_G81_G82_drill_moves(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, command: str, params: PathParameters, drill_z: float, F_feedrate: str, G0_retract_z: str) -> None
    doc: Output the movement G code for G81 and G82.
  - def parse_a_group(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, pathobj) -> None
    doc: Parse a Group (compound, project, or simple path).
  - def parse_a_path(values: Values, gcode: Gcode, pathobj) -> None
    doc: Parse a simple Path.
  - def set_adaptive_op_speed(values: Values, command: str, command_line: CommandLine, params: PathParameters, adaptive_op_variables: Tuple[bool, float, float]) -> None
    doc: Set the appropriate feed speed for an adaptive op.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/scripts/linuxcnc_post.py
classes:
  class Linuxcnc(PostProcessor)
    doc: The LinuxCNC post processor class. LinuxCNC supports various trajectory control methods (path blending) as described at https://linuxcnc.org/docs/2.4/html/common_User_Concepts.html#r1_1_2 This post processor implements the following trajectory control methods: - Exact Path (G61) - Exact Stop (G61.1) - Blend (G64)
    methods:
      - def get_common_property_schema(cls)
        doc: Override common properties with LinuxCNC-specific defaults.
      - def get_property_schema(cls)
        doc: Return schema for LinuxCNC-specific configurable properties.
      - def init_values(self, values: Values) -> None
        doc: Initialize values that are used throughout the postprocessor.
      - def get_sanity_checks(self, job)
        doc: LinuxCNC specific sanity checks.
      - def tooltip(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/scripts/marlin_post.py
classes:
  class Marlin(PostProcessor)
    doc: Marlin 2.x post processor. Marlin firmware interprets G4 P as milliseconds and G4 S as seconds, which differs from standard G-code where G4 P is seconds. This post processor converts all G4 dwell commands to use the S parameter so that dwell times are in seconds as expected by the CAM pipeline. All other behaviour is handled by the base PostProcessor class and machine configuration.
    methods:
      - def get_common_property_schema(cls)
        doc: Override common properties with Marlin-specific defaults.
      - def get_property_schema(cls)
        doc: Return schema for Marlin-specific configurable properties.
      - def init_values(self, values: Values) -> None
        doc: Initialize values that are used throughout the postprocessor.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/scripts/smoothie_post.py
classes:
  class Smoothie(PostProcessor)
    doc: The SmoothieBoard post processor class. This postprocessor outputs G-code suitable for SmoothieBoard controllers. It supports direct network upload to the SmoothieBoard via TCP/IP.
    methods:
      - def init_values(self, values: Values) -> None
        doc: Initialize values that are used throughout the postprocessor.
      - def init_arguments(self, values, argument_defaults, arguments_visible)
        doc: Initialize command-line arguments, including SmoothieBoard-specific options.
      - def process_arguments(self)
        doc: Process arguments and update values, including SmoothieBoard-specific settings.
      - def export(self)
        doc: Override export to handle network upload to SmoothieBoard.
      - def remote_post(self, gcode_sections)
        doc: Override remote_post to handle SmoothieBoard network upload.
      - def tooltip(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Post/scripts/snapmaker_legacy_post.py
functions:
  - def convert_option_to_attr(option_name)
classes:
  class CoordinatesAction(argparse.Action)
    doc: argparse Action to handle coordinates (x,y,z)
  class ExtremaAction(argparse.Action)
    doc: argparse Action to handle integer extrema (min,max)
  class Snapmaker(Path.Post.Processor.PostProcessor)
    doc: FreeCAD-compatible runtime postprocessor targeting Snapmaker machines with CNC capabilities
    methods:
      - def initialize(self)
        doc: initialize values and arguments
      - def snapmaker_init_values(self)
        doc: Initialize values that are used throughout the postprocessor.
      - def snapmaker_init_argument_defaults(self) -> None
        doc: Initialize which arguments (in a pair) are shown as the default argument.
      - def snapmaker_init_arguments_visible(self) -> None
        doc: Initialize which argument pairs are visible in TOOLTIP_ARGS.
      - def snapmaker_init_parser(self, values, argument_defaults, arguments_visible) -> argparse.ArgumentParser
        doc: Initialize the postprocessor arguments parser
      - def snapmaker_process_arguments(self, filename: str='-') -> Tuple[bool, str | argparse.Namespace]
        doc: Process any arguments to the postprocessor.
      - def snapmaker_process_postables(self, filename: str='-') -> List[Tuple[str, str]]
        doc: process job sections to gcode
      - def get_thumbnail(self) -> str
        doc: generate a thumbnail of the job from the given objects
      - def output_header(self, gcode: List[str])
        doc: custom method derived from Path.Post.UtilsExport.output_header
      - def convert_spindle(self, gcode: List[str]) -> List[str]
        doc: convert spindle speed values from RPM to percent (%) (M3/M4 commands)
      - def check_boundaries(self, gcode: List[str]) -> bool
        doc: Check boundaries and return whether it succeeded
      - def export_common(self, objects: List, filename: str | pathlib.Path) -> str
        doc: custom method derived from Path.Post.UtilsExport.export_common
      - def export(self, filename: str | pathlib.Path='-')
        doc: process gcode and export
      - def tooltip(self) -> str
      - def tooltipArgs(self) -> str
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/CAM/Path/Preferences.py
functions:
  - def preferences()

  - def getLastToolLibrarySortKey() -> Optional[str]
  - def setLastToolLibrarySortKey(name: str)
  - def allAvailablePostProcessors()
  - def allEnabledPostProcessors(include=None)
  - def classifyPostProcessor(name)
    doc: Classify a postprocessor as 'machine', 'legacy', or 'unknown'. Checks for a POST_TYPE module-level constant in the post's .py file. Returns 'machine' for new-style posts, 'legacy' for old-style, 'unknown' if the file cannot be found or loaded.
  - def allAvailableLegacyPostProcessors()
    doc: Return only legacy postprocessors.
  - def allAvailableMachinePostProcessors()
    doc: Return only new-style machine postprocessors.
  - def allEnabledLegacyPostProcessors(include=None)
    doc: Return enabled legacy postprocessors (for Job context).
  - def allEnabledMachinePostProcessors(include=None)
    doc: Return enabled machine postprocessors (for Machine editor context).
  - def defaultPostProcessor()
  - def defaultPostProcessorArgs()
  - def defaultGeometryTolerance()
  - def defaultLibAreaCurveAccuracy()
  - def defaultFilePath()
  - def filePath()
  - def macroFilePath()
  - def searchPaths()
  - def searchPathsPost()
  - def addAddonPostPath(path: str) -> None
    doc: Register an additional directory to search for post-processors. Called by addon Init.py at FreeCAD-compatible runtime startup. Each call adds one directory. Duplicate registrations are silently ignored. Invalidates the post-type cache so newly registered posts are classified correctly.
  - def addAddonAssetPath(addon_dir: str) -> None
    doc: Register all assets provided by an addon directory. Convenience function for addon Init.py files. Discovers the standard subdirectory layout of a Machines-style addon and registers each type: - ``<addon_dir>/posts/`` -> post-processor search path - ``<addon_dir>/machines/`` -> machine definition templates Duplicate registrations are silently ignored. Args: addon_dir: Root directory of the installed addon.
  - def defaultJobTemplate()
  - def setJobDefaults(jobTemplate, geometryTolerance, curveAccuracy)
  - def postProcessorBlacklist()
  - def setPostProcessorDefaults(processor, args, blacklist)
  - def setOutputFileDefaults(fileName, policy)
  - def defaultOutputFile()
  - def defaultOutputPolicy()
  - def showEditorOnPostProcess()
    doc: Get user preference for showing editor before writing G-code. Returns: bool: True to show editor, False to skip it (default: True)
  - def setShowEditorOnPostProcess(show: bool)
    doc: Set user preference for showing editor before writing G-code. Args: show: True to show editor, False to skip it
  - def defaultStockTemplate()
  - def setDefaultStockTemplate(template)
  - def defaultTaskPanelLayout()
  - def setDefaultTaskPanelLayout(style)
  - def advancedOCLFeaturesEnabled()
  - def experimentalFeaturesEnabled()
  - def suppressAllSpeedsWarning()
  - def suppressRapidSpeedsWarning(user=True)
  - def suppressSelectionModeWarning()
  - def suppressOpenCamLibWarning()
  - def setPreferencesAdvanced(ocl, warnSpeeds, warnRapids, warnModes, warnOCL)
```
