```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/WorkingPlane.py
functions:
  - def get_working_plane(update=True)
  - def getPlacementFromPoints(points)
    doc: Return a placement from a list of 3 or 4 points. The 4th point is no longer used. Calls draftgeoutils.geometry.placement_from_points(). See there.
  - def getPlacementFromFace(face, rotated=False)
    doc: Return a placement from a face. Calls draftgeoutils.geometry.placement_from_face(). See there.
classes:
  class PlaneBase
    doc: PlaneBase is the base class for the Plane class and the PlaneGui class. Parameters ---------- u: Base.Vector or WorkingPlane.PlaneBase, optional Defaults to Vector(1, 0, 0). If a WP is provided: A copy of the WP is created, all other parameters are then ignored. If a vector is provided: Unit vector for the `u` attribute (+X axis). v: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 1, 0). Unit vector for the `v` attribute (+Y axis). w: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 0, 1). Unit vector fo
    methods:
      - def copy(self)
        doc: Return a new WP that is a copy of the present object.
      - def match(self, source, target=None)
        doc: Match the main properties of two working planes. Parameters ---------- source: WP object WP to copy properties from. target: WP object, optional Defaults to `None`. WP to copy properties to. If `None` the present object is used.
      - def get_parameters(self)
        doc: Return a data dictionary with the main properties of the WP.
      - def set_parameters(self, data)
        doc: Set the main properties of the WP according to a data dictionary.
      - def align_to_3_points(self, p1, p2, p3, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to 3 points with an optional offset. The points must define a plane. Parameters ---------- p1: Base.Vector New WP `position`. p2: Base.Vector Point on the +X axis. (p2 - p1) defines the WP `u` axis. p3: Base.Vector Defines the plane. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `True`/`False` `T
      - def align_to_edges_vertexes(self, shapes, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to the endpoints of edges and/or the points of vertexes with an optional offset. The points must define a plane. The first 2 points define the WP `position` and `u` axis. Parameters ---------- shapes: iterable One or more edges and/or vertexes. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `True`
      - def align_to_edge_or_wire(self, shape, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to an edge or wire with an optional offset. The shape must define a plane. If the shape is an edge with a `Center` then that defines the WP `position`. The vector between the center and its start point then defines the WP `u` axis. In other cases the start point of the first edge defines the WP `position` and the 1st derivative at that
      - def align_to_straight_edge(self, edge, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a straight edge. Used when a single straight edge is selected and does not define a plane. The edge direction becomes the WP `u` axis. All three axes are recomputed using XZY priority so the result is always a valid orthonormal frame. Parameters ---------- edge: Part.Edge A straight edge. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Of
      - def align_to_vertex(self, vertex, offset=0)
        doc: Move the WP origin to a vertex, keeping the current orientation. Parameters ---------- vertex: Part.Vertex A vertex. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `True` Always successful.
      - def align_to_two_vertices(self, v1, v2, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to two vertices. The direction from `v1` to `v2` defines the WP `u` axis. The `v` and `axis` vectors are recomputed using XZY priority. The WP origin is set to `v1`. Parameters ---------- v1: Part.Vertex First vertex (WP origin). v2: Part.Vertex Second vertex (defines u axis direction). offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset 
      - def align_to_face(self, face, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a face with an optional offset. The face must be planar. The center of gravity of the face defines the WP `position` and the normal of the face the WP `axis`. The WP `u` and `v` vectors are determined by the draftgeoutils.geometry.uv_vectors_from_face function. See there. Parameters ---------- face: Part.Face Face. offset: float, op
      - def align_to_face_and_edge(self, face, edge, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a face and an edge. The face must be planar. The normal of the face defines the WP `axis`, the first vertex of the edge its `position`, and its `u` vector is determined by the second vertex of the edge. Parameters ---------- face: Part.Face Face. edge: Part.Edge Edge, need not be an edge of the face. offset: float, optional Defaults
      - def align_to_placement(self, place, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a placement with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- place: Base.Placement Placement. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `True`
      - def align_to_point_and_axis(self, point, axis, offset=0, upvec=Vector(1, 0, 0))
        doc: Align the WP to a point and an axis with an optional offset and an optional up-vector. If the axis and up-vector are parallel the FreeCAD-compatible runtime.Rotation algorithm will replace the up-vector: Vector(0, 0, 1) is tried first, then Vector(0, 1, 0), and finally Vector(1, 0, 0). Parameters ---------- point: Base.Vector New WP `position`. axi
      - def align_to_point_and_axis_svg(self, point, axis, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a point and an axis with an optional offset. It aligns `u` and `v` based on the magnitude of the components of `axis`. Parameters ---------- point: Base.Vector The new `position` of the plane, adjusted by the `offset`. axis: Base.Vector A vector whose unit vector will be used as the new `axis` of the plane. The magnitudes of the `x`
      - def align_to_rotation(self, rot)
        doc: Align the WP to a rotation. Parameter --------- rot: Base.Rotation Rotation. Returns ------- `True`
      - def get_global_coords(self, point, as_vector=False)
        doc: Translate a point or vector from the local (WP) coordinate system to the global coordinate system. Parameters ---------- point: Base.Vector Point. as_vector: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` treat point as a vector. Returns ------- Base.Vector
      - def get_local_coords(self, point, as_vector=False)
        doc: Translate a point or vector from the global coordinate system to the local (WP) coordinate system. Parameters ---------- point: Base.Vector Point. as_vector: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` treat point as a vector. Returns ------- Base.Vector
      - def get_closest_axis(self, vec)
        doc: Return a string indicating the positive or negative WP axis closest to a vector. Parameters ---------- vec: Base.Vector Vector. Returns ------- str `"x"`, `"y"` or `"z"`.
      - def get_placement(self)
        doc: Return a placement calculated from the WP.
      - def is_global(self)
        doc: Return `True` if the WP matches the global coordinate system exactly.
      - def is_ortho(self)
        doc: Return `True` if all WP axes are parallel to a global axis.
      - def project_point(self, point, direction=None, force_projection=True)
        doc: Project a point onto the WP and return the global coordinates of the projected point. Parameters ---------- point: Base.Vector Point to project. direction: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to `None` in which case the WP `axis` is used. Direction of projection. force_projection: bool, optional Defaults to `True`. See draftgeoutils.geometry.project_poi
      - def set_to_top(self, offset=0)
        doc: Set the WP to the top position with an optional offset.
      - def set_to_front(self, offset=0)
        doc: Set the WP to the front position with an optional offset.
      - def set_to_side(self, offset=0)
        doc: Set the WP to the right side position with an optional offset.
  class Plane(PlaneBase)
    doc: The old Plane class. Parameters ---------- u: Base.Vector or WorkingPlane.Plane, optional Defaults to Vector(1, 0, 0). If a WP is provided: A copy of the WP is created, all other parameters are then ignored. If a vector is provided: Unit vector for the `u` attribute (+X axis). v: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 1, 0). Unit vector for the `v` attribute (+Y axis). w: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 0, 1). Unit vector for the `axis` attribute (+Z axis). pos: Base.Vector, opt
    methods:
      - def copy(self)
        doc: See PlaneBase.copy.
      - def offsetToPoint(self, point, direction=None)
        doc: Return the signed distance from a point to the plane. The direction argument is ignored. The returned value is the negative of the local Z coordinate of the point.
      - def projectPoint(self, point, direction=None, force_projection=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.project_point.
      - def alignToPointAndAxis(self, point, axis, offset=0, upvec=None)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_point_and_axis.
      - def alignToPointAndAxis_SVG(self, point, axis, offset=0)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_point_and_axis_svg.
      - def alignToCurve(self, shape, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to a curve. NOT IMPLEMENTED. Parameters ---------- shape: Part.Shape Edge or Wire. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `False`
      - def alignToEdges(self, shapes, offset=0)
        doc: Align the WP to edges with an optional offset. The eges must define a plane. The endpoints of the first edge defines the WP `position` and `u` axis. Parameters ---------- shapes: iterable Two edges. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. Returns ------- `True`/`False` `True` if successful.
      - def alignToFace(self, shape, offset=0, parent=None)
        doc: Align the WP to a face with an optional offset. The face must be planar. The center of gravity of the face defines the WP `position` and the normal of the face the WP `axis`. The WP `u` and `v` vectors are determined by the draftgeoutils.geometry.uv_vectors_from_face function. See there. Parameters ---------- shape: Part.Face Face. offset: float, o
      - def alignTo3Points(self, p1, p2, p3, offset=0)
        doc: Align the plane to a temporary face created from three points. Parameters ---------- p1: Base.Vector First point. p2: Base.Vector Second point. p3: Base.Vector Third point. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis` Returns ------- `True`/`False` `True` if successful.
      - def alignToSelection(self, offset=0)
        doc: Align the plane to a selection with an optional offset. The selection must define a plane. Parameter --------- offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis` Returns ------- `True`/`False` `True` if successful.
      - def setup(self, direction=None, point=None, upvec=None, force=False)
        doc: Set up the working plane if it exists but is undefined. If `direction` and `point` are present, it calls `align_to_point_and_axis(point, direction, 0, upvec)`. Otherwise, it gets the camera orientation to define a working plane that is perpendicular to the current view, centered at the origin, and with `v` pointing up on the screen. This method onl
      - def reset(self)
        doc: Reset the WP. Sets the `weak` attribute to `True`.
      - def setTop(self)
        doc: Set the WP to the top position and updates the GUI.
      - def setFront(self)
        doc: Set the WP to the front position and updates the GUI.
      - def setSide(self)
        doc: Set the WP to the left side position and updates the GUI. Note that set_to_side from the parent class sets the WP to the right side position. Which matches the Side option from Draft_SelectPlane.
      - def getRotation(self)
        doc: Return a placement describing the WP orientation only.
      - def getPlacement(self, rotated=False)
        doc: Return a placement calculated from the WP. The rotated argument is ignored.
      - def getNormal(self)
        doc: Return the normal vector (axis) of the WP.
      - def setFromPlacement(self, place, rebase=False)
        doc: Align the WP to a placement. Parameters ---------- place: Base.Placement Placement. rebase: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `False` the `position` of the WP is not changed.
      - def inverse(self)
        doc: Invert the direction of the plane. It inverts the `u` and `axis` vectors.
      - def save(self)
        doc: Store the plane attributes. Store `u`, `v`, `axis`, `position` and `weak` in a list in `stored`.
      - def restore(self)
        doc: Restore the plane attributes that were saved. Restores the attributes `u`, `v`, `axis`, `position` and `weak` from `stored`, and set `stored` to `None`.
      - def getLocalCoords(self, point)
        doc: Translate a point from the global coordinate system to the local (WP) coordinate system.
      - def getGlobalCoords(self, point)
        doc: Translate a point from the local (WP) coordinate system to the global coordinate system.
      - def getLocalRot(self, vec)
        doc: Translate a vector from the global coordinate system to the local (WP) coordinate system.
      - def getGlobalRot(self, vec)
        doc: Translate a vector from the local (WP) coordinate system to the global coordinate system.
      - def getClosestAxis(self, vec)
        doc: Return the closest WP axis to a vector. Parameters ---------- vec: Base.Vector Vector. Returns ------- str `'x'`, `'y'` or `'z'`.
      - def isGlobal(self)
        doc: Return `True` if the WP matches the global coordinate system.
      - def isOrtho(self)
        doc: Return `True` if the WP axes are orthogonal to the global axes.
      - def getDeviation(self)
        doc: Return the angle between the u axis and the horizontal plane. It defines a projection of `u` on the horizontal plane (without a Z component), and then measures the angle between this projection and `u`. It also considers the cross product of the projection and `u` to determine the sign of the angle. Returns ------- float Angle between the `u` vecto
      - def getParameters(self)
        doc: Return a dictionary with the data which define the plane: `u`, `v`, `axis`, `position`, `weak`. Returns ------- dict dictionary of the form: {"u":x, "v":v, "axis":axis, "position":position, "weak":weak}
      - def setFromParameters(self, data)
        doc: Set the plane according to data. Parameters ---------- data: dict dictionary of the form: {"u":x, "v":v, "axis":axis, "position":position, "weak":weak}
  class PlaneGui(PlaneBase)
    doc: The PlaneGui class. The class handles several GUI related aspects of the WP including a history. Parameters ---------- u: Base.Vector or WorkingPlane.Plane, optional Defaults to Vector(1, 0, 0). If a WP is provided: A copy of the WP is created, all other parameters are then ignored. If a vector is provided: Unit vector for the `u` attribute (+X axis). v: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 1, 0). Unit vector for the `v` attribute (+Y axis). w: Base.Vector, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 
    methods:
      - def copy(self)
        doc: Return a new plane that is a copy of the present object.
      - def align_to_selection(self, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: Align the WP to a selection with an optional offset. For a single face the WP is aligned to that face. For a single curved edge or wire the WP is aligned to the plane of that shape. For a single straight edge the WP u-axis is aligned along the edge while the WP axis (normal) is preserved. For a single vertex the WP origin is moved to that point whi
      - def align_to_3_points(self, p1, p2, p3, offset=0, _hist_add=True)

        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_edges_vertexes.

        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_edge_or_wire.
      - def align_to_straight_edge(self, edge, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_straight_edge.
      - def align_to_vertex(self, vertex, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_vertex.
      - def align_to_two_vertices(self, v1, v2, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_two_vertices.
      - def align_to_face(self, face, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_face.
      - def align_to_face_and_edge(self, face, edge, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_face.
      - def align_to_placement(self, place, offset=0, _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_placement.
      - def align_to_point_and_axis(self, point, axis, offset=0, upvec=Vector(1, 0, 0), _hist_add=True)
        doc: See PlaneBase.align_to_point_and_axis.
      - def align_to_obj_placement(self, obj, offset=0, place=None, _hist_add=True)
        doc: Align the WP to an object placement with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- obj: App::DocumentObject Object to derive the Placement (if place is `None`), the icon and the label from. offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. place: Base.Placement, optional Defaults to `None`. If `None` the Placement from obj is us
      - def align_to_wp_proxy(self, obj, offset=0, place=None, _hist_add=True)
        doc: Align the WP to a WPProxy with an optional offset. See align_to_obj_placement. Also handles several WPProxy related features.
      - def auto_align(self)
        doc: Align the WP to the current view if self.auto is True.
      - def set_to_default(self)
        doc: Set the WP to the default from the preferences.
      - def set_to_auto(self)
        doc: Set the WP to auto.
      - def set_to_top(self, offset=0, center_on_view=False)
        doc: Set the WP to the top position with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. center_on_view: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` the WP `position` is moved along the (offset) WP to the center of the view
      - def set_to_front(self, offset=0, center_on_view=False)
        doc: Set the WP to the front position with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. center_on_view: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` the WP `position` is moved along the (offset) WP to the center of the view
      - def set_to_side(self, offset=0, center_on_view=False)
        doc: Set the WP to the right side position with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. center_on_view: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` the WP `position` is moved along the (offset) WP to the center of the view
      - def set_to_view(self, offset=0, center_on_view=False)
        doc: Align the WP to the view with an optional offset. Parameters ---------- offset: float, optional Defaults to zero. Offset along the WP `axis`. center_on_view: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `True` the WP `position` is moved along the (offset) WP to the center of the view
      - def set_to_position(self, pos)
        doc: Set the `position` of the WP.
      - def center_on_view(self)
        doc: Move the WP `position` along the WP to the center of the view.
      - def align_view(self)
        doc: Align the view to the WP.
```

```text
functions:
  - def array(objectslist, arg1, arg2, arg3, arg4=None, arg5=None, arg6=None)
    doc: This function creates an array of independent objects. Use make_array() to create a parametric array object. Creates an array of the given objects (that can be an object or a list of objects). In case of rectangular array, xnum of iterations in the x direction at xvector distance between iterations, and same for y and z directions with yvector and ynum and zvector and znum. In case of polar array, center is a vector, totalangle is the angle to cover (in degrees) and totalnum is the number of obj
  - def rectArray(objectslist, xvector, yvector, xnum, ynum)

  - def polarArray(objectslist, center, angle, num)
```

```text
functions:

    doc: Return a cut object made from the difference of the 2 given objects. Parameters ---------- object1: Part::Feature Any object with a `Part::TopoShape`. object2: Part::Feature Any object with a `Part::TopoShape`. Returns ------- Part::Cut The resulting cut object. None If there is a problem and the new object can't be created.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftfunctions/downgrade.py
functions:
  - def downgrade(objects, delete=False, force=None)
    doc: Downgrade the given objects. This is a counterpart to `upgrade`. Parameters ---------- objects: Part::Feature or list A single object to downgrade or a list containing various such objects. delete: bool, optional It defaults to `False`. If it is `True`, the old objects are deleted, and only the resulting object is kept. force: str, optional It defaults to `None`. Its value can be used to force a certain method of downgrading. It can be any of: `'explode'`, `'shapify'`, `'subtr'`, `'splitFaces'`,
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftfunctions/fuse.py
functions:

    doc: fuse(oject1, object2) Returns an object made from the union of the 2 given objects. If the objects are coplanar, a special Draft Wire is used, otherwise we use a standard Part fuse.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftfunctions/heal.py
functions:
  - def heal(objlist=None, delete=True, reparent=True)
    doc: heal([objlist],[delete],[reparent]) Recreate Draft objects that are damaged, for example if created from an earlier version. If ran without arguments, all the objects in the document will be healed if they are damaged. Parameters ---------- objlist : list delete : Base.Vector or list of Base.Vector If delete is True, the damaged objects are deleted (default). reparent : bool If reparent is True (default), new objects go at the very same place in the tree than their original.
```

It is also used as the `Placement.Base` of the resulting object. p2: Base::Vector3 Point 2 of the mirror plane. Returns -----

```text
    doc: Get the SVG representation of a dimension line overshoot.
  - def getOvershoot(point, shootsize, color, linewidth, angle=0)
    doc: Get the SVG representation of a dimension line overshoot. DEPRECATED.
  - def format_point(coords, action='L')
    doc: Return a string with a formatted point.
  - def get_svg(obj, scale=1, linewidth=0.35, fontsize=12, fillstyle='shape color', direction=None, linestyle=None, color=None, linespacing=None, techdraw=False, rotation=0, fillspaces=False, override=True)
    doc: Return a string containing an SVG representation of the object. Paramaeters ----------- scale: float, optional It defaults to 1. It allows scaling line widths down, so they are resolution-independent. linewidth: float, optional It defaults to 0.35. fontsize: float, optional It defaults to 12, which is interpreted as `pt` unit (points). It is used if the given object contains any text. fillstyle: str, optional It defaults to 'shape color'. direction: Base::Vector3, optional It defaults to `None`.
  - def get_print_color(obj)
    doc: Return the print color of the parent layer, if available.
  - def getSVG(obj, scale=1, linewidth=0.35, fontsize=12, fillstyle='shape color', direction=None, linestyle=None, color=None, linespacing=None, techdraw=False, rotation=0, fillSpaces=False, override=True)
    doc: Return SVG string of the object. DEPRECATED. Use 'get_svg'.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftfunctions/svgshapes.py
functions:
  - def get_proj(vec, plane=None)
    doc: Get a projection of the vector in the plane's u and v directions. TODO: check if the same function for SVG and DXF projection can be used so that this function is not just duplicated code. This function may also be present elsewhere, like `WorkingPlane` or `DraftGeomUtils`, so we should avoid code duplication. Parameters ---------- vec: Base::Vector3 An arbitrary vector that will be projected on the U and V directions. plane: WorkingPlane.PlaneBase Working plane.
  - def getProj(vec, plane=None)
    doc: Get a projection of a vector. DEPRECATED.
  - def get_discretized(edge, plane)
    doc: Get a discretized edge on a plane.
  - def getDiscretized(edge, plane)
    doc: Get a discretized edge on a plane. DEPRECATED.
  - def get_circle(plane, fill, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, edge)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from a circular edge.
  - def getCircle(plane, fill, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, edge)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from a circular edge.
  - def get_ellipse(plane, fill, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, edge)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from an elliptical edge.
  - def getEllipse(plane, fill, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, edge)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from an elliptical edge. DEPRECATED.
  - def get_path(obj, plane, fill, pathdata, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, fill_opacity=None, edges=[], wires=[], pathname=None)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from an object's edges or wires. TODO: the `edges` and `wires` must not default to empty list `[]` but to `None`. Verify that the code doesn't break with this change. `edges` and `wires` are mutually exclusive. If no `wires` are provided, sort the `edges`, and use them. If `wires` are provided, sort the edges in these `wires`, and use them.
  - def getPath(obj, plane, fill, pathdata, stroke, linewidth, lstyle, fill_opacity, edges=[], wires=[], pathname=None)
    doc: Get the SVG representation from a path. DEPRECATED.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftfunctions/upgrade.py
functions:
  - def upgrade(objects, delete=False, force=None)
    doc: Upgrade the given objects. This is a counterpart to `downgrade`. Parameters ---------- objects: Part::Feature or list A single object to upgrade or a list containing various such objects. delete: bool, optional It defaults to `False`. If it is `True`, the old objects are deleted, and only the resulting object is kept. force: str, optional It defaults to `None`. Its value can be used to force a certain method of upgrading. It can be any of: `'makeCompound'`, `'closeGroupWires'`, `'makeSolid'`, `'
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/arcs.py
functions:
  - def isClockwise(edge, ref=None)
    doc: Return True if a circle-based edge has a clockwise direction. Parameters ---------- edge : The edge to be analyzed. ref : Vector, optional The normal around which the direction of the edge is to be determined. Defaults to the Z normal vector. Returns ------- bool Returns True if the edge is clockwise oriented around the ref Vector or not.
  - def isWideAngle(edge)
    doc: Return True if the given edge is an arc with angle > 180 degrees.
  - def arcFrom2Pts(firstPt, lastPt, center, axis=None)
    doc: Build an arc with center and 2 points, can be oriented with axis.
  - def arcFromSpline(edge)
    doc: Turn given edge into a circular arc from three points. Takes its first point, midpoint and endpoint. It works best with bspline segments such as those from imported svg files. Use this only if you are sure your edge is really an arc. It returns None if there is a problem, including passing straight edges.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/circle_inversion.py
functions:
  - def pointInversion(circle, point)
    doc: Return the circle inversion of a point. It will return `None` if the given point is equal to the center of the circle.
  - def polarInversion(circle, edge)
    doc: Return the inversion pole of a line. The edge is the polar. The nearest point on the line is inversed. http://mathworld.wolfram.com/InversionPole.html
  - def circleInversion(circle, circle2)
    doc: Circle inversion of a circle, inverting the center point. Returns the new circle created from the inverted center of circle2.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/circles.py
functions:
  - def findClosestCircle(point, circles)
    doc: Return the circle with closest center to a given point.
  - def getCircleFromSpline(edge)
    doc: Return a circle-based edge from a bspline-based edge. Return None if the edge is not a BSplineCurve.
  - def circlefrom1Line2Points(edge, p1, p2)
    doc: Return a list of circles created from an edge and two points. It calculates up to 2 possible centers.
  - def circlefrom2Lines1Point(edge1, edge2, point)
    doc: Return a list of circles from two edges and one point.
  - def circleFrom2LinesRadius(edge1, edge2, radius)
    doc: Return a list of circles from two edges and one radius. It calculates 4 centers.
  - def circleFrom3LineTangents(edge1, edge2, edge3)
    doc: Return a list of circles from three edges. It calculates up to 6 possible centers.
  - def circleFromPointLineRadius(point, edge, radius)
    doc: Return a list of circles from one point, one edge, and one radius. It calculates up to 2 possible centers.
  - def circleFrom2PointsRadius(p1, p2, radius)
    doc: Return a list of circles from two points, and one radius. The two points must not be equal. It calculates up to 2 possible centers.
  - def findHomotheticCenterOfCircles(circle1, circle2)
    doc: Calculate the homothetic centers from two circles. Return None if the objects are not circles, or if they are concentric. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Homothetic_center http://mathworld.wolfram.com/HomotheticCenter.html
  - def findRadicalAxis(circle1, circle2)
    doc: Calculate the radical axis of two circles. On the radical axis (also called power line) of two circles any tangents drawn from a point on the axis to both circles have the same length. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radical_axis http://mathworld.wolfram.com/RadicalLine.html See Also -------- findRadicalCenter
  - def findRadicalCenter(circle1, circle2, circle3)
    doc: Calculate the radical center of three circles. It is also called the power center. It is the intersection point of the three radical axes of the pairs of circles. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_center_(geometry) http://mathworld.wolfram.com/RadicalCenter.html See Also -------- findRadicalAxis
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/circles_apollonius.py
functions:
  - def outerSoddyCircle(circle1, circle2, circle3)
    doc: Compute the outer soddy circle for three tightly packed circles. Original Java code Copyright (rc) 2008 Werner Randelshofer Converted to python by Martin Buerbaum 2009 http://www.randelshofer.ch/treeviz/ Either Creative Commons Attribution 3.0, the MIT license, or the GNU Lesser General License LGPL.
  - def innerSoddyCircle(circle1, circle2, circle3)
    doc: Compute the inner soddy circle for three tightly packed circles. Original Java code Copyright (rc) 2008 Werner Randelshofer Converted to python by Martin Buerbaum 2009 http://www.randelshofer.ch/treeviz/
  - def circleFrom3CircleTangents(circle1, circle2, circle3)
    doc: Return the circle that is tangent to three other circles. This problem is called the 'Problem of Appollonius'. A special case is that of 'Soddy circles'. ```
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/circles_incomplete.py
functions:
  - def circlefromCircleLinePoint(circle, line, point)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circlefrom2Circles1Point(circle1, circle2, point)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom2tan1pt(tan1, tan2, point)
    doc: Circle from two tangents and one point. The tangents should be edges, and they may be either straight line edges or circular edges, so four combinations are possible.
  - def circleFromCircleLineRadius(circle, line, radius)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom2CirclesRadius(circle1, circle2, radius)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom2tan1rad(tan1, tan2, rad)
    doc: Circle from two tangents and one radius. The tangents should be edges, and they may be either straight line edges or circular edges, so four combinations are possible.
  - def circlefrom1Circle2Points(circle, point1, point2)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom1tan2pt(tan1, p1, p2)
    doc: Circle from one tangent and two points. The tangents should be edges, and they may be either straight line edges or circular edges, so two combinations are possible.
  - def circleFromPointCircleRadius(point, circle, radius)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom1tan1pt1rad(tan1, p1, rad)
    doc: Circle from one tangent, one point, and radius. The tangents should be edges, and they may be either straight line edges or circular edges, so two combinations are possible.
  - def circleFrom1Circle2Lines(circle, line1, line2)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom2Circle1Lines(circle1, circle2, line)
    doc: Do nothing. Placeholder function. Needs to be implemented.
  - def circleFrom3tan(tan1, tan2, tan3)
    doc: Circle from three tangents. The tangents should be edges, and they may be either straight line edges or circular edges, so eight combinations are possible.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/cuboids.py
functions:
  - def isCubic(shape)
    doc: Return True if the shape is a parallelepiped (cuboid). A parallelepiped of cube-like shape has 8 vertices, 6 faces, 12 edges, and all angles are 90 degrees between its edges.
  - def getCubicDimensions(shape)
    doc: Return a list containing the placement, and dimensions of the shape. The dimensions are length, width and height of a the parallelepiped, rounded to the value indicated by `precision`. With the length larger than or equal to width. The placement point is the center of the lowest face. If it is not a parallelepiped (cuboid), return None.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/edges.py
functions:
  - def findEdge(anEdge, aList)
    doc: Return True if edge is found in list of edges.
  - def orientEdge(edge, normal=None, make_arc=False)
    doc: Re-orient the edge such that it is in the XY plane. Re-orients `edge` such that it is in the XY plane. If `normal` is passed, this is used as the basis for the rotation, otherwise the placement of `edge` is used.
  - def isSameLine(e1, e2)
    doc: Return True if the 2 edges are lines and have the same points.
  - def is_line(bspline)
    doc: Return True if the given BSpline curve is a straight line.
  - def invert(shape)
    doc: Return an inverted copy of the edge or wire contained in the shape.
  - def findMidpoint(edge)
    doc: Return the midpoint of an edge.
  - def getTangent(edge, from_point=None)
    doc: Return the tangent to an edge, including BSpline and circular arcs. If from_point is given, it is used to calculate the tangent, only useful for a circular arc.
  - def get_referenced_edges(property_value)
    doc: Return the Edges referenced by the value of a App:PropertyLink, App::PropertyLinkList, App::PropertyLinkSub or App::PropertyLinkSubList property.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/faces.py
functions:
  - def concatenate(shape)
    doc: Turn several faces into one.
  - def getBoundary(shape)
    doc: Return the boundary edges of a group of faces.
  - def is_coplanar(faces, tol=-1)
    doc: Return True if all faces in the given list are coplanar. Parameters ---------- faces: list List of faces to check coplanarity. tol: float, optional It defaults to `-1`, the tolerance of confusion, equal to 1e-7. Is the maximum deviation to be considered coplanar. Returns ------- out: bool True if all face are coplanar. False in other case.
  - def bind(w1, w2, per_segment=False)
    doc: Bind 2 wires by their endpoints and returns a face / compound of faces. If per_segment is True and the wires have the same number of edges, the wires are processed per segment: a separate face is created for each pair of edges (one from w1 and one from w2), and the faces are then fused. This avoids problems with walls based on wires that selfintersect, or that have a loop that ends in a T-connection (f.e. a wire shaped like a number 6).
  - def cleanFaces(shape)
    doc: Remove inner edges from coplanar faces.
  - def removeSplitter(shape)
    doc: Return a face from removing the splitter in a list of faces. This is an alternative, shared edge-based version of Part.removeSplitter. Returns a face, or `None` if the operation failed.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/fillets.py
functions:
  - def fillet(lEdges, r, chamfer=False)
    doc: Return a list of sorted edges describing a round corner.
  - def filletWire(aWire, r, chamfer=False)
    doc: Fillet each angle of a wire with r as radius. If chamfer is true, a `chamfer` is made instead, and `r` is the size of the chamfer.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/general.py
functions:
  - def precision()
    doc: Return the Draft precision setting.
  - def vec(edge, use_orientation=False)
    doc: Return a vector from an edge or a Part.LineSegment. If use_orientation is True, it takes into account the edges orientation. If edge is not straight, you'll get strange results!
  - def edg(p1, p2)
    doc: Return an edge from 2 vectors.
  - def getVerts(shape)
    doc: Return a list containing vectors of each vertex of the shape.
  - def v1(edge)
    doc: Return the first point of an edge.
  - def isNull(something)
    doc: Return True if the given shape, vector, or placement is Null. If the vector is (0, 0, 0), it will return True.
  - def isPtOnEdge(pt, edge)
    doc: Test if a point lies on an edge.
  - def hasCurves(shape)
    doc: Check if the given shape has curves.
  - def isAligned(edge, axis='x')
    doc: Check if the given edge or line is aligned to the given axis. The axis can be 'x', 'y' or 'z'.
  - def getQuad(face)
    doc: Return a list of 3 vectors if the face is a quad, otherwise None. Returns ------- basepoint, Xdir, Ydir If the face is a quad. None If the face is not a quad.
  - def areColinear(e1, e2)
    doc: Return True if both edges are colinear.
  - def hasOnlyWires(shape)
    doc: Return True if all edges are inside a wire.
  - def geomType(edge)
    doc: Return the type of geometry this edge is based on. Parameters ---------- edge: the edge whose `Curve` attribute is to be checked. Returns ------- str Return the type of the edge's Curve attribute or "Unknown", if the parameter is missing.
  - def isValidPath(shape)
    doc: Return True if the shape can be used as an extrusion path.
  - def findClosest(base_point, point_list)
    doc: Find closest point in a list of points to the base point. Returns ------- int An index from the list of points is returned. None If point_list is empty.
  - def getBoundaryAngles(angle, alist)
    doc: Return the 2 closest angles that encompass the given angle.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/geo_arrays.py
functions:
  - def print_places(places, title='Places')
    doc: Print a vector with a title.
  - def get_init_values(path, count=6)
    doc: Set values needed to create the array.
  - def get_n_params(edge, number, step, norm)
    doc: Get the parameters needed in each iteration.
  - def get_twisted_placements(path, count=15, rot_factor=0.25)
    doc: Get the placements of the twisted array elements.
  - def get_twisted_array_shape(base, path, count=15, rot_factor=0.25)
    doc: Get the twisted array shape as a compound.
  - def get_twisted_bridge_shape(base, path, count=15, rot_factor=0.25, width=100, thickness=10)
    doc: Get the twisted bridge array shape as a compound.
  - def create_frames(obj, places)
    doc: Create the frames from the placements.
  - def make_tunnel(path, profiles)
    doc: Create the tunnel shape.
  - def make_walkway(path, width=100, thickness=10)
    doc: Construct the walkway of the twisted bridge array.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/geometry.py
functions:
  - def findPerpendicular(point, edgeslist, force=None)
    doc: Find the perpendicular distance between a point and a list of edges. If force is specified, only the edge[force] will be considered, and it will be considered infinite. Returns ------- [vector_from_point_to_closest_edge, edge_index] The vector and the index in the list. None If no perpendicular vector could be found.
  - def findDistance(point, edge, strict=False)
    doc: Return a vector from the point to its closest point on the edge. If `strict` is `True`, the vector will be returned only if its endpoint lies on the `edge`. Edge can also be a list of 2 points.
  - def get_spline_normal(edge, tol=-1)
    doc: Find the normal of a BSpline edge.
  - def get_shape_normal(shape)
    doc: Find the normal of a shape or list of points or colinear edges, if possible.
  - def get_normal(shape, tol=-1)
    doc: Find the normal of a shape or list of points, if possible.
  - def getRotation(v1, v2=App.Vector(0, 0, 1))
    doc: Get the rotation Quaternion between 2 vectors.
  - def is_planar(shape, tol=-1)
    doc: Return True if the given shape or list of points is planar.
  - def is_straight_line(shape, tol=-1)
    doc: Return True if shape is a straight line. function used in other methods because Part.Shape.findPlane assign a plane and normal to straight wires creating privileged directions and to deal with straight wires with overlapped edges.
  - def are_coplanar(shape_a, shape_b, tol=-1)
    doc: Return True if exist a plane containing both shapes.
  - def get_spline_surface_normal(shape, tol=-1)
    doc: Check if shape formed by BSpline surfaces is planar and get normal. If shape is not planar return None.
  - def find_plane(shape, tol=-1)
    doc: Find the plane containing the shape if possible. Use this function as a workaround due Part.Shape.findPlane fail to find plane on BSpline surfaces.
  - def calculatePlacement(shape)
    doc: Return a placement located in the center of gravity of the shape. If the given shape is planar, return a placement located at the center of gravity of the shape, and oriented towards the shape's normal. Otherwise, it returns a null placement.
  - def mirror(point, edge)
    doc: Find mirror point relative to an edge.
  - def mirror_matrix(mtx, pos, nor)
    doc: Return a mirrored copy of a matrix. Parameters ---------- mtx: Base::Matrix Matrix. pos: Base::Vector3 Point on mirror plane. nor: Base::Vector3 Normal of mirror plane. Returns ------- Base::Matrix
  - def uv_vectors_from_face(face, vec_z=App.Vector(0, 0, 1), tol=-1)
    doc: Return the u and v vectors of a planar face. It is up to the calling function to ensure the face is planar. If the u vector matches +/-vec_z, or the v vector matches -vec_z, the vectors are rotated to ensure the v vector matches +vec_z. Parameters ---------- face: Part.Face Face. vec_z: Base::Vector3, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 0, 1). Z axis vector used for reference. Is replaced by Vector(0, 0, 1) if it matches the +/-normal of the face. tol: float, optional Defaults to -1. Internal toleran
  - def placement_from_face(face, vec_z=App.Vector(0, 0, 1), rotated=False, tol=-1)
    doc: Return a placement from the center of gravity, and the u and v vectors of a planar face. It is up to the calling function to ensure the face is planar. Parameters ---------- face: Part.Face Face. vec_z: Base::Vector3, optional Defaults to Vector(0, 0, 1). Z axis vector used for reference. Is replaced by Vector(0, 0, 1) if it matches the +/-normal of the face. rotated: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If `False` the v vector of the face defines the Y axis of the placement. If `True` the -v vec
  - def placement_from_points(pt_pos, pt_x, pt_y, as_vectors=False, tol=-1)
    doc: Return a placement from 3 points defining an origin, an X axis and a Y axis. If the vectors calculated from the arguments are too short or parallel, the returned placement will have a default rotation. Parameters ---------- pt_pos: Base::Vector3 Origin (Base of Placement). pt_x: Base::Vector3 Point on positive X axis. Or X axis vector if as_vectors is `True`. pt_y: Base::Vector3 Point on positive Y axis. Or Y axis vector if as_vectors is `True`. as_vectors: bool, optional Defaults to `False`. If
  - def distance_to_plane(point, base, normal)
    doc: Return the signed distance from a plane to a point. The distance is positive if the point lies on the +normal side of the plane. Parameters ---------- point: Base::Vector3 Point to project. base: Base::Vector3 Point on plane. normal: Base::Vector3 Normal of plane. Returns ------- float
  - def project_point_on_plane(point, base, normal, direction=None, force_projection=False, tol=-1)

If `True` forces the projection if the deviation between the direction and the normal is less than tol from the orthogonality. The direction of projection is then modi
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/intersections.py
functions:
  - def findIntersection(edge1, edge2, infinite1=False, infinite2=False, ex1=False, ex2=False, dts=True, findAll=False)
    doc: Return a list containing the intersection points of 2 edges. You can also feed 4 points instead of `edge1` and `edge2`. If `dts` is used, `Shape.section()` is used. Parameters ---------- edge1 Part.Edge, Circle, Line -> the first edge. Base::Vector3 -> the starting point of the first line. In which case `infinite1` must also be a point. edge2 Part.Edge, Circle, Line -> the second edge. Base::Vector3 -> the ending point of the second line. In which case `infinite2` must also be a point. the secon
  - def wiresIntersect(wire1, wire2)
    doc: Return True if some of the edges of the wires are intersecting. Otherwise return `False`.
  - def connect(edges, closed=False, wireNedge=False)
    doc: Connect the edges in the given list by their intersections.
  - def angleBisection(edge1, edge2)
    doc: Return an edge that bisects the angle between the 2 straight edges.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/linear_algebra.py
functions:
  - def linearFromPoints(p1, p2)
    doc: Calculate linear equation from points. Calculate the slope and offset parameters of the linear equation of a line defined by two points. Linear equation: y = m * x + b m = dy / dx m ... Slope b ... Offset (point where the line intersects the y axis) dx/dy ... Delta x and y. Using both as a vector results in a non-offset direction vector.
  - def determinant(mat, n)
    doc: Return the determinant of an N-matrix. It recursively expands the minors.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/offsets.py
functions:
  - def pocket2d(shape, offset)
    doc: Return a list of wires obtained from offsetting wires from the shape. Return a list of wires obtained from offsetting the wires from the given shape by the given offset, and intersection if needed.
  - def offset(edge, vector, trim=False)
    doc: Return a copy of the edge at a certain vector offset. If the edge is an arc, the vector will be added at its first point and a complete circle will be returned. None if there is a problem. Parameters ---------- edge: Part.Shape the edge to offset vector: Base::Vector3 the vector by which the edge is to be offset trim: bool, optional If `edge` is an arc and `trim` is `True`, the resulting arc will be trimmed to the proper angle Returns ------- Part.Shape The offset shape
  - def offsetWire(wire, dvec, bind=False, occ=False, widthList=None, offsetMode=None, alignList=[], normal=None, basewireOffset=0, wireNedge=False)
    doc: Offset the wire along the given vector. Parameters ---------- wire as a sorted list of edges (the list is used directly), or as a wire or a face (Draft Wire with MakeFace True or False supported). The vector will be applied at the first vertex of the wire. If bind is True (and the shape is open), the original wire and the offsetted one are bound by 2 edges, forming a face. If widthList is provided (values only, not lengths - i.e. no unit), each value will be used to offset each corresponding edg
```

```text
    doc: Sort edges. Deprecated. Use Part.__sortEdges__ instead.
  - def sortEdgesOld(lEdges, aVertex=None)
    doc: Sort edges. Deprecated. Use Part.__sortEdges__ instead.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftgeoutils/wires.py
functions:
  - def findWires(edgeslist)
    doc: Find wires in a list of edges.
  - def findWiresOld2(edgeslist)
    doc: Find connected wires in the given list of edges.
  - def findWiresOld(edges)
    doc: Return a list of lists containing edges that can be connected. Find connected edges in the list.
  - def flattenWire(wire, origin=None, normal=None)
    doc: Force a wire to be flat on a plane defined by an origin and a normal. If origin or normal are None they are derived from the wire.
  - def superWire(edgeslist, closed=False)
    doc: Force a wire between edges that don't have coincident endpoints. Forces a wire between edges that don't necessarily have coincident endpoints. If closed=True, the wire will always be closed.
  - def isReallyClosed(wire)
  - def curvetowire(obj, steps)
    doc: Discretize the object and return a list of edges.
  - def curvetosegment(curve, seglen)
    doc: Discretize the curve and return a list of edges.
  - def rebaseWire(wire, vidx=0)
    doc: Return a copy of the wire with the first vertex indicated by the index. Return a new wire which is a copy of the current wire, but where the first vertex is the vertex indicated by the given index vidx, starting from 1. 0 will return an exact copy of the wire.
  - def removeInterVertices(wire)
    doc: Remove middle vertices from a straight wire and return a new wire. Remove unneeded vertices, those that are in the middle of a straight line, from a wire, return a new wire.
  - def cleanProjection(shape, tessellate=True, seglength=0.05)
    doc: Return a compound of edges, optionally tessellate ellipses, splines and bezcurves. The function was formerly used to workaround bugs in the projection algorithm. These bugs have since been fixed. Now the function is only used when tessellation of ellipses, splines and bezcurves is required (DXF output and Draft_Shape2DView).
  - def tessellateProjection(shape, seglen)
    doc: Return projection with BSplines and Ellipses broken into line segments. Useful for exporting projected views to DXF files.
  - def get_placement_perpendicular_to_wire(wire)
    doc: Return the placement whose base is the wire's first vertex and it's z axis aligned to the wire's tangent.
  - def get_extended_wire(wire, offset_start, offset_end)
    doc: Return a wire trimmed (negative offset) or extended (positive offset) at its first vertex, last vertex or both ends. get_extended_wire(wire, -100.0, 0.0) -> returns a copy of the wire with its first 100 mm removed get_extended_wire(wire, 0.0, 100.0) -> returns a copy of the wire extended by 100 mm after it's last vertex
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_annotationstyleeditor.py
classes:
  class AnnotationStyleEditor(gui_base.GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: Annotation style editor for text and dimensions. It inherits `GuiCommandSimplest` to set up the document, `IsActive`, and other behavior. See this class for more information. Attributes ---------- doc: App::Document The active document when the command is used, so that the styles are saved to this document. styles: dict A dictionary with key-value pairs that define the new style. renamed: dict A dictionary that holds the name of the style that is renamed by the editor. form: PySide.QtWidgets.QDi
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. The document attribute is set here by the parent class.
      - def read_meta(self)
        doc: Read the document Meta attribute and return a dict.
      - def save_meta(self, styles)
        doc: Save a dict to the document Meta attribute and update objects.
      - def on_style_changed(self, index)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the styles combobox changes.
      - def on_delete(self)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the delete button is pressed.
      - def on_rename(self)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the rename button is pressed.
      - def on_import(self)
        doc: Import styles from a json file.
      - def on_export(self)
        doc: Export styles to a json file.
      - def fill_editor(self, style=None)
        doc: Fill the editor fields with the contents of a style.
      - def update_style(self)
        doc: Update the current style with the values from the editor.
      - def get_editor_values(self)
      - def get_annotations(self)
        doc: Get all objects that support annotation styles.
      - def get_style_users(self, style)
        doc: Get all objects using a certain style.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_base.py
classes:
  class GuiCommandSimplest
    doc: Simplest base class for GuiCommands. This class only sets up the command name and the document object to use for the command. When it is executed, it logs the command name to the log file, and prints the command name to the console. It implements the `IsActive` method, which must return `True` when the command should be available. It should return `True` when there is an active document, otherwise the command (button or menu) should be disabled. This class is meant to be inherited by other GuiCo
    methods:
      - def IsActive(self)
        doc: Return True when this command should be available.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. Log the command name to the log file and console. Also update the `doc` attribute.
  class GuiCommandNeedsSelection(GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: Base class for GuiCommands that need a selection to be available. It re-implements the `IsActive` method to return `True` when there is both an active document and an active selection. It inherits `GuiCommandSimplest` to set up the document and other behavior. See this class for more information.
    methods:
      - def IsActive(self)
        doc: Return True when this command should be available.
  class GuiCommandBase
    doc: Generic class that is the basis of all Gui commands. This class should eventually replace `DraftTools.DraftTool`, once all functionality in that class is merged here. Attributes ---------- commit_list : list of 2-element tuples Each tuple is made of a string, and a list of strings. :: commit_list = [(string1, list1), (string2, list2), ...] The string is a simple header, for example, a command name, that indicates what is being executed. Each string in the list of strings represents a Python inst
    methods:
      - def IsActive(self)
        doc: Return True when this command should be available.
      - def Activated(self)
      - def update_hints(self)
      - def get_hints(self)
      - def finish(self)
        doc: Terminate the active command by committing the list of commands. It also perform some other tasks like terminating the plane tracker and the snapper.
      - def commit(self, name, func)
        doc: Store actions to be committed to the document. Parameters ---------- name : str A string that indicates what is being committed. func : list of strings Each element of the list should be a Python command that will be executed.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_base_original.py
classes:
  class DraftTool
    doc: The base class of all Draft Tools. This is the original class that was defined in `DraftTools.py` before any re-organization of the code. This class is subclassed by `Creator` and `Modifier` to set up a few additional properties of these two types. This class connects with the `draftToolBar` and `Snapper` classes that are installed in the `FreeCAD-compatible GUI runtime` namespace in order to set up some properties of the running tools such as the task panel, the snapping func
    methods:
      - def IsActive(self)
        doc: Return True when this command should be available.
      - def Activated(self, name='None', is_subtool=False)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. If an active Gui Command exists, it will call the `finish` method of it to terminate it. If no active Gui Command exists, it will proceed with configuration of the tool. The child class that subclasses this class then should continue with its own Activated method. Parameters ---------- name: str, optional It defa
      - def update_hints(self)
      - def get_hints(self)
      - def end_callbacks(self, call)
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Finish the current command. These are general cleaning tasks that are performed when terminating all commands. These include setting the node list to empty, setting to `None` the active command, turning off the graphical interface (task panel), finishing the plane tracker, restoring the working plane, turning off the snapper. If a callback is insta
      - def commit(self, name, func)
        doc: Store actions in the commit list to be run later. Parameters ---------- name: str An arbitrary string that indicates the name of the operation to run. func: list of str Each element of the list is a string that will be run by `Gui.doCommand`. See the complete information in the `draftutils.todo.ToDo` class.
      - def getStrings(self, addrot=None)
        doc: Return useful strings that will be used to build commands. Returns ------- str, str, str, str A tuple of four strings that represent useful information. * the current working plane rotation quaternion as a string * the support object if available as a string * the list of nodes inside the `node` attribute as a string * the string `'True'` or `'Fals
  class Creator(DraftTool)
    doc: A generic Creator tool, used by creation tools such as line or arc. It inherits `DraftTool`, which sets up the majority of the behavior of this class.
    methods:
      - def Activated(self, name='None')
        doc: Execute when the command is called. Parameters ---------- name: str, optional It defaults to `'None'`. It is the `featureName` of the object, to know what is being run.
  class Modifier(DraftTool)
    doc: A generic Modifier tool, used by modification tools such as move. After initializing the parent class, it sets the `copymode` attribute to `False`. It inherits `DraftTool`, which sets up the majority of the behavior of this class. Modifier tools share a two-phase input flow: first the user picks the object(s) to operate on, then the user supplies one or more reference points (base point, angle, distance, mirror line, etc.). The ``selection_done`` flag distinguishes the two phases so the base cla
    methods:
      - def Activated(self, name='None', is_subtool=False)
      - def get_hints(self)
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
  class CubicBezCurve(gui_lines.Line)
    doc: Gui command for the 3rd degree B?zier Curve tool. The EnableSelection parameter has an impact on SoMouseButtonEvents. See: https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/6452
    methods:

      - def undolast(self)
        doc: Undo last line segment.

        doc: Create shape for display during creation process.

        doc: Create shape for display during creation process.

        doc: Terminate the operation and close the curve if asked. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`. closed: bool, optional Close the curve if `True`.
      - def get_hints(self)
```

```text
classes:
  class Circle(gui_arcs.Arc)
    doc: Gui command for the Circle tool. It inherits the entire `Arc` class. The only difference is that the `closedCircle` attribute is already set to `True`, and the `featureName` attribute is `'Circle'`. This will result in an arc that describes a complete circumference so the starting angle and end angle will be the same. Internally, both circular arcs and circles are `'Circle'` objects. Discussion ---------- Both arcs and circles are `'Circle'` objects, but when it comes to the Gui Commands, the re
    methods:
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_circulararray.py
classes:
  class CircularArray(gui_base.GuiCommandBase)
    doc: Gui command for the CircularArray tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. We add callbacks that connect the 3D view with the widgets of the task panel.
      - def move(self, event_cb)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the pointer moves in the 3D view. It should automatically update the coordinates in the widgets of the task panel.
      - def click(self, event_cb=None)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the pointer clicks on the 3D view. It should act as if the Enter key was pressed, or the OK button was pressed in the task panel.
      - def completed(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is terminated. We should remove the callbacks that were added to the 3D view and then close the task panel.
```

```text
      - def create_radial_dimension_obj(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_edit.py
classes:
  class Edit(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: The Draft_Edit FreeCAD-compatible runtime command definition. A tool to graphically edit FreeCAD-compatible runtime objects. Current implementation use many parts of pivy graphics code by user "looo". The tool collect editpoints from objects and display Trackers on them to allow editing their Shape and their parameters. Callbacks --------- selection_callback registered when tool is launched, identify selected objects. editing_callbacks self._keyPressedCB -> self.keyPressed self._mouseMovedCB -> 
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Activated is run when user launch Edit command. If something is selected -> call self.proceed() If nothing is selected -> self.register_selection_callback()
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: this method set the editTrackers
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Execute callback by the toolbar to activate the update function. This function gets called by the toolbar or by the mouse click and activate the update function.
      - def slotDeletedObject(self, obj)
        doc: Document observer callback: exit edit mode if the edited object is deleted.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate Edit Tool.
      - def reset_edit(self)
      - def get_hints(self)
        doc: Return status bar input hints for the current tool state.
      - def register_selection_callback(self)
        doc: Register callback for selection when command is launched.
      - def unregister_selection_callback(self)
        doc: remove selection callback if it exists
      - def register_editing_callbacks(self)
        doc: register editing callbacks (former action function)
      - def unregister_editing_callbacks(self)
        doc: remove callbacks used during editing if they exist
      - def keyPressed(self, event_callback)
        doc: Execute as callback for keyboard event.
      - def mousePressed(self, event_callback)
        doc: mouse button event handler, calls: startEditing, endEditing, addPoint, delPoint
      - def mouseMoved(self, event_callback)
        doc: Execute as callback for mouse movement. Update tracker position and update preview ghost.
      - def startEditing(self, obj, node_idx)
        doc: Start editing selected EditNode.
      - def updateTrackerAndGhost(self, event)
        doc: Update tracker position when editing and update ghost.
      - def endEditing(self, obj, nodeIndex, v=None)
        doc: Terminate editing and start object updating process.
      - def setTrackers(self, obj, points=None)
        doc: Set Edit Trackers for editpoints collected from self.obj.
      - def resetTrackers(self, obj)
        doc: Reset Edit Trackers and set them again.
      - def resetTrackersBezier(self, obj)
      - def removeTrackers(self, obj=None)
        doc: Remove Edit Trackers. Attributes ---------- obj: FreeCAD-compatible runtime object Removes trackers only for given object, if obj is None, removes all trackers
      - def hideTrackers(self, obj=None)
        doc: Hide Edit Trackers. Attributes ---------- obj: FreeCAD-compatible runtime object Hides trackers only for given object, if obj is None, hides all trackers
      - def showTrackers(self, obj=None)
        doc: Show Edit Trackers. Attributes ---------- obj: FreeCAD-compatible runtime object Shows trackers only for given object, if obj is None, shows all trackers
      - def initGhost(self, obj)
      - def updateGhost(self, obj, node_idx, v)
      - def finalizeGhost(self)
      - def display_tracker_menu(self, event)
      - def evaluate_menu_action(self, action)
      - def getEditPoints(self, obj)
        doc: Return a list of App.Vectors according to the given object edit nodes.
      - def update(self, obj, nodeIndex, v)
        doc: Apply the App.Vector to the modified point and update obj.
      - def update_object(self, obj, nodeIndex, v)
        doc: Update the object according to the given modified editpoint.
      - def has_obj_gui_tools(self, obj)
        doc: Check if the object has the GuiTools to provide information to edit it.
      - def get_obj_gui_tools(self, obj)
        doc: Retrieve the obj_gui_tools to support Draft Edit.
      - def getObjsFromSelection(self)
        doc: Evaluate selection and return a valid object to edit. #to be used for app link support for selobj in Gui.Selection.getSelectionEx('', 0): for sub in selobj.SubElementNames: obj = selobj.Object obj_matrix = selobj.Object.getSubObject(sub, retType=4)
      - def format_objects_for_editing(self, objs)
        doc: Change objects style during editing mode.
      - def deformat_objects_after_editing(self, objs)
        doc: Restore objects style during editing mode.
      - def get_specific_object_info(self, obj, pos)
        doc: Return info of a specific object at a given position.
      - def get_selected_obj_at_position(self, pos)
        doc: Return object at given position. If object is one of the edited objects (self.edited_objects).
      - def globalize_vectors(self, obj, pointList)
        doc: Return the given point list in the global coordinate system.
      - def globalize_vector(self, obj, point)
        doc: Return the given point in the global coordinate system.
      - def localize_vectors(self, obj, pointList)
        doc: Return the given point list in the given object coordinate system.
      - def localize_vector(self, obj, point)
        doc: Return the given point in the given object coordinate system.
      - def getEditNode(self, pos)
        doc: Get edit node from given screen position.
      - def sendRay(self, mouse_pos)
        doc: Send a ray through the scene and return the nearest entity.
      - def searchEditNode(self, picked_point)
        doc: Search edit node inside picked point list and return node number.
      - def getEditNodeIndex(self, point)
        doc: Get edit node index from given screen position.
  class GuiToolsRepository
    doc: This object provide a repository to collect all the specific objects editing tools.
    methods:
      - def get(self, obj_type)
      - def add(self, type, gui_tools)
      - def keys(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_edit_arch_objects.py
classes:
  class ArchWallGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
        doc: Return the list of editpoints for the given Arch Wall object. 0 : height of the wall 1-2 : endpoints if wall does not have a base
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
        doc: if node_idx == 0: edit_arch.updateWall(obj, node_idx, v) elif node_idx > 0: if obj.Base: if utils.get_type(obj.Base) in ["Wire", "Circle", "Rectangle", "Polygon", "Sketch"]: self.update(obj.Base, node_idx - 1, v)
  class ArchWindowGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
  class ArchStructureGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
      - def get_object_style(self, obj)
      - def set_object_editing_style(self, obj)
      - def restore_object_style(self, obj, modes)
  class ArchSpaceGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
  class ArchPanelCutGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
  class ArchPanelSheetGuiTools(GuiTools)
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_edit_base_object.py
classes:
  class GuiTools
    doc: Base class for object editing tools
    methods:
      - def get_edit_points(self, obj)
        doc: Return to Draft_Edit a list of vectors for the given object. Remember to use object local coordinates system. Parameters: obj: the object
      - def update_object_from_edit_points(self, obj, node_idx, v, alt_edit_mode=0)
        doc: Update the object from modified Draft_Edit point. No need to recompute at the end. Parameters: obj: the object node_idx: number of the edited node v: target vector of the node in object local coordinates system alt_edit_mode: alternative edit mode to perform different operations (usually can be selected from the Draft_Edit context menu) default = 0
      - def get_edit_point_context_menu(self, edit_command, obj, node_idx)
        doc: Get the context menu associated to edit points (user is over an editpoint) Return a list of tuples containing menu labels and associated functions: return [ ("action label", lambda: self.handle_action_label(edit_command, obj, node_idx)), ] Parameters: edit_command: running Draft_Edit command obj: the edited object node_idx: number of the edited nod
      - def get_edit_obj_context_menu(self, edit_command, obj, position)
        doc: Get the context menu associated to edited object (user is over the object) Return a list of tuples containing menu labels and associated functions: return [ ("action label", lambda: self.handle_action_label(edit_command, obj, position)), ] Parameters: edit_command: running Draft_Edit command obj: the edited object position: position of the cursor o
      - def get_object_style(self, obj)
      - def set_object_editing_style(self, obj)
      - def restore_object_style(self, obj, modes)
      - def init_preview_object(self, obj)
      - def update_preview_object(self, edit_command, obj, node_idx, v)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_ellipses.py
classes:
  class Ellipse(gui_base_original.Creator)
    doc: Gui command for the Ellipse tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`.
      - def createObject(self)
        doc: Create the actual object in the current document.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def appendPoint(self, point)
        doc: Append the point to the list of nodes.
      - def get_hints(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_field_locks.py
classes:
  class InputFieldLockGroup
    doc: Track manual-input locks for a group of ``Gui::InputField`` widgets. Parameters ---------- on_change: callable, optional Called as ``on_change(key, locked)`` after a field's lock state changes, for owners that need to react (e.g. update a snapper).
    methods:
      - def add_field(self, key, field)
        doc: Attach the lock icon and the lock/unlock triggers to a field.
      - def queue_locked_value_refresh(self, key)
      - def lock_valid_input(self, key)
      - def is_locked(self, key)
      - def locked_value(self, key)
      - def any_locked(self)
      - def set_locked(self, key, locked)
      - def unlock(self, key)
      - def unlock_keys(self, keys)
      - def unlock_all(self)
      - def dispose(self)
        doc: Release all locks and ignore callbacks from discarded fields.
```

```text
classes:
  class ToggleGrid(gui_base.GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: The Draft ToggleGrid command definition. If the grid tracker is invisible (hidden), it makes it visible (shown); and if it is visible, it hides it. It inherits `GuiCommandSimplest` to set up the document and other behavior. See this class for more information.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def IsActive(self)
        doc: Return True when this command should be available.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_groups.py
functions:
  - def moveToGroup(group)
    doc: Place the selected objects in the chosen group.
classes:
  class AddToGroup(gui_base.GuiCommandNeedsSelection)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_AddToGroup tool. It adds selected objects to a group, or removes them from any group. It inherits `GuiCommandNeedsSelection` to only be available when there is a document and a selection. See this class for more information.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def proceed(self, option)
        doc: Place the selected objects in the chosen group or ungroup them. Parameters ---------- option: str The passed string.
  class SelectGroup(gui_base.GuiCommandNeedsSelection)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_SelectGroup tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
  class SetAutoGroup(gui_base.GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_AutoGroup tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. It calls the `setAutogroup` method of the `DraftToolBar` class installed inside the global `Gui` namespace.
      - def proceed(self, option)
        doc: Set the defined autogroup, or create a new layer. Parameters ---------- option: str The passed string.
  class AddToConstruction(gui_base.GuiCommandNeedsSelection)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_AddConstruction tool. It adds the selected objects to the construction group defined in the `DraftToolBar` class which is initialized in the `Gui` namespace when the workbench loads. It adds a construction group if it doesn't exist. Added objects are also given the visual properties of the construction group.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
  class AddNamedGroup(gui_base.GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_AddNamedGroup tool. It adds a new named group.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_lines.py
classes:
  class Line(gui_base_original.Creator)
    doc: Gui command for the Line tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self, name=QT_TRANSLATE_NOOP('draft', 'Line'), icon='Draft_Line', task_title=None)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def finish(self, cont=False, closed=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation and close the polyline if asked. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`. closed: bool, optional Close the line if `True`.
      - def removeTemporaryObject(self)
        doc: Remove temporary object created.
      - def undolast(self)
        doc: Undoes last line segment.
      - def drawUpdate(self, point)
        doc: Draws new line segment.
      - def wipe(self)
        doc: Remove all previous segments and starts from last point.
      - def orientWP(self)
        doc: Orient the working plane.
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def get_hints(self)
  class Wire(Line)
    doc: Gui command for the Wire or Polyline tool. It inherits the `Line` class, and calls essentially the same code, only this time the `mode` is set to `"wire"`, so we are allowed to place more than two points.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_lineslope.py
classes:
  class LineSlope(gui_base.GuiCommandNeedsSelection)
    doc: Gui Command for the Line slope tool. For a line in the XY plane, it changes the height of one of its points to create a sloped line. To Do ----- Make it work also with lines lying on the YZ and XZ planes, or in an arbitrary plane, for which the normal is known.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def accept(self)
        doc: Execute when clicking the OK button or pressing Enter key. It changes the slope of the line that lies on the XY plane. TODO: make it work also with lines lying on the YZ and XZ planes.
```
