```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_move.py
classes:
  class Move(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the Move tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def get_object_selection(self)
        doc: Get the object selection.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Continue with the command after a selection has been made.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def handle_mouse_move_event(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the mouse when moving.
      - def handle_mouse_click_event(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the mouse when the first button is clicked.
      - def set_ghosts(self)
        doc: Set the ghost to display.
      - def get_subelement_ghosts(self, selection, copy)
        doc: Get ghost for the subelements (vertices, edges).
      - def move(self, copy)
        doc: Perform the move of the subelement(s) or the entire object(s).
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_orthoarray.py
classes:
  class OrthoArray(gui_base.GuiCommandBase)
    doc: Gui command for the OrthoArray tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. We add callbacks that connect the 3D view with the widgets of the task panel.
      - def click(self, event_cb=None)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the pointer clicks on the 3D view. It should act as if the Enter key was pressed, or the OK button was pressed in the task panel.
      - def completed(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is terminated. We should remove the callbacks that were added to the 3D view and then close the task panel.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_rotate.py
classes:
  class Rotate(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the Rotate tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def get_object_selection(self)
        doc: Get the object selection.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Continue with the command after a selection has been made.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def handle_mouse_move_event(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the mouse when moving.
      - def handle_mouse_click_event(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the mouse when the first button is clicked.
      - def set_center(self)
        doc: Set the center of the rotation.
      - def set_start_point(self)
        doc: Set the starting point of the rotation.
      - def set_rotation_angle(self, arg)
        doc: Set the rotation angle.
      - def set_ghosts(self)
        doc: Set the ghost to display.
      - def get_subelement_ghosts(self, selection, copy)
        doc: Get ghost for the subelements (vertices, edges).
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`.
      - def rotate(self, copy)
        doc: Perform the rotation of the subelement(s) or the entire object(s).
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def numericRadius(self, rad)
        doc: Validate the radius entry field in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when a valid radius has been entered in the input field.
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_scale.py
classes:
  class Scale(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the Scale tool. This tool scales the selected objects from a base point.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def get_object_selection(self)
        doc: Get object selection and proceed if successful.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Proceed with execution of the command after selection.
      - def set_ghosts(self)
        doc: Set the ghost to display.
      - def get_subelement_ghosts(self, selection, copy)
        doc: Get ghost for the subelements (vertices, edges).
      - def scale_ghosts(self, x, y, z, rel=False)
        doc: Scale the preview of the object.
      - def pick_ref(self)
        doc: Pick a point of reference.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def handle_mouse_move_event(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the mouse event of movement.
      - def handle_mouse_click_event(self)
        doc: Handle the mouse click event.
      - def scale(self)
        doc: Perform the scale of the object. Scales the subelements, or with a clone, or just general scaling.
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation.
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_snapper.py
classes:
  class Snapper
    doc: Classes to manage snapping in Draft and Arch. The Snapper objects contains all the functionality used by draft and arch module to manage object snapping. It is responsible for finding snap points and displaying snap markers. Usually You only need to invoke it's snap() function, all the rest is taken care of. 3 functions are useful for the scriptwriter: snap(), constrain() or getPoint() which is an all-in-one combo. The individual snapToXXX() functions return a snap definition in the form [real_p
    methods:
      - def init_active_snaps(self)
        doc: set self.active_snaps according to user prefs
      - def set_snap_style(self)
      - def cstr(self, lastpoint, constrain, point)
        doc: Return constraints if needed.
      - def snap(self, screenpos, lastpoint=None, active=True, constrain=False, noTracker=False)
        doc: Return a snapped point from the given (x, y) screen position. snap(screenpos,lastpoint=None,active=True,constrain=False, noTracker=False): returns a snapped point from the given (x,y) screenpos (the position of the mouse cursor), active is to activate active point snapping or not (passive), lastpoint is an optional other point used to draw an imagi
      - def cycleSnapObject(self)
        doc: Increase the index of the snap object by one.
      - def snapToObject(self, lastpoint, active, constrain, eline, point)
        doc: Snap to an object.
      - def toWP(self, point)
        doc: Project the given point on the working plane, if needed.
      - def getApparentPoint(self, x, y)
        doc: Return a 3D point, projected on the current working plane.
      - def snapToDim(self, obj)
      - def snapToExtensions(self, point, last, constrain, eline)
        doc: Return a point snapped to extension or parallel line. The parallel line of the last object, if any.
      - def snapToCrossExtensions(self, point)
        doc: Snap to the intersection of the last 2 extension lines.
      - def snapToPolar(self, point, last)
        doc: Snap to polar lines from the given point.
      - def snapToGrid(self, point)
        doc: Return a grid snap point if available.
      - def snapToEndpoints(self, shape, point=None)
        doc: Return a list of endpoints snap locations.
      - def snapToMidpoint(self, shape)
        doc: Return a list of midpoints snap locations.
      - def snapToBSplineKnots(self, edge)
        doc: Return a list of knot snap locations for a BSpline.
      - def snapToNear(self, shape, point)
        doc: Return a list with a near snap location for an edge.
      - def snapToNearFace(self, shape, point)
        doc: Return a list with a near snap location for a face.
      - def snapToNearUnprojected(self, point)
        doc: Return a list with a near snap location that is not projected on the object.
      - def snapToPerpendicular(self, shape, last)
        doc: Return a list of perpendicular snap locations for an edge.
      - def snapToPerpendicularFace(self, shape, last)
        doc: Return a list of perpendicular snap locations for a face.
      - def snapToOrtho(self, shape, last, constrain)
        doc: Return a list of ortho snap locations.
      - def snapToExtOrtho(self, last, constrain, eline)
        doc: Return an ortho X extension snap location.
      - def snapToHold(self, point)
        doc: Return a snap location that is orthogonal to hold points. Or if possible at crossings.
      - def snapToExtPerpendicular(self, last)
        doc: Return a perpendicular X extension snap location.
      - def snapToElines(self, e1, e2)
        doc: Return a snap at the infinite intersection of the given edges.
      - def snapToAngles(self, shape)
        doc: Return a list of angle snap locations.
      - def snapToCenter(self, shape)
        doc: Return a list of center snap locations.
      - def snapToCenterFace(self, shape)
        doc: Return a face center snap location.
      - def snapToIntersection(self, shape)
        doc: Return a list of intersection snap locations.
      - def snapToPolygon(self, obj)
        doc: Return a list of polygon center snap locations.
      - def snapToSpecials(self, obj, lastpoint=None, eline=None)
        doc: Return special snap locations, if any.
      - def getScreenDist(self, dist, cursor)
        doc: Return a distance in 3D space from a screen pixels distance.
      - def getPerpendicular(self, edge, pt)
        doc: Return a point on an edge, perpendicular to the given point.
      - def setArchDims(self, p1, p2)
        doc: Show arc dimensions between 2 points.
      - def get_quarter_widget(self, mw)
      - def device_pixel_ratio(self)
      - def get_cursor_with_tail(self, base_icon_name, tail_icon_name=None)
      - def setCursor(self, mode=None)
        doc: Set the cursor to the given mode or unset it.
      - def restack(self)
        doc: Lower the grid tracker so it doesn't obscure other objects.
      - def setPointConstraintProvider(self, provider)
        doc: Set the active task panel that constrains snapped points.
      - def clearPointConstraintProvider(self, provider=None)
        doc: Clear the active provider if it matches ``provider``.
      - def off(self)
        doc: Finish snapping.
      - def setSelectMode(self, mode)
        doc: Set the snapper into select mode (hides snapping temporarily).
      - def setAngle(self, delta=None)
        doc: Keep the current angle.
      - def constrain(self, point, basepoint=None, axis=None)
        doc: Return a constrained point. constrain(point,basepoint=None,axis=None: Returns a constrained point. Axis can be "x","y" or "z" or a custom vector. If None, the closest working plane axis will be picked. Basepoint is the base point used to figure out from where the point must be constrained. If no basepoint is given, the current point is used as base
      - def unconstrain(self)
        doc: Unset the basepoint and the constrain line.
      - def getPoint(self, last=None, callback=None, movecallback=None, extradlg=None, title=None, mode='point', hints=None)
        doc: Get a 3D point from the screen. getPoint([last],[callback],[movecallback],[extradlg],[title],[mode],[hints]): gets a 3D point from the screen. You can provide an existing point, in that case additional snap options and a tracker are available. You can also pass a function as callback, which will get called with the resulting point as argument, when
      - def get_snap_toolbar(self)
        doc: Get the snap toolbar.
      - def toggleGrid(self)
        doc: Toggle FreeCAD-compatible runtime Draft Grid.
      - def showradius(self)
        doc: Show the snap radius indicator.
      - def isEnabled(self, snap)
        doc: Returns true if the given snap is on
      - def toggle_snap(self, snap, set_to=None)
        doc: Sets the given snap on/off according to the given parameter
      - def save_snap_state(self)
        doc: Save snap state to user preferences to be restored in next session.
      - def show_hide_grids(self, show=True)
        doc: Show the grid in all 3D views where it was previously visible, or hide the grid in all 3D view. Used when switching to different workbenches. Hiding the grid can be prevented by setting the GridHideInOtherWorkbenches preference to `False`.
      - def show(self)
        doc: Show the grid in all 3D views where it was previously visible.
      - def hide(self)
        doc: Hide the grid in all 3D views.
      - def setGrid(self)
        doc: Set the grid, if visible.
      - def setTrackers(self, update_grid=True)
        doc: Set the trackers.
      - def addHoldPoint(self)
        doc: Add hold snap point to list of hold points.
      - def recenter_workingplane(self)
        doc: Recenters the working plane on the current snap position
```

```text
  class Draft_Snap_Special(Draft_Snap_Base)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_Snap_Special tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
  class Draft_Snap_Dimensions(Draft_Snap_Base)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_Snap_Dimensions tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
  class Draft_Snap_WorkingPlane(Draft_Snap_Base)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_Snap_WorkingPlane tool.

  class ShowSnapBar(Draft_Snap_Base)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_ShowSnapBar tool.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_splines.py
classes:
  class BSpline(gui_lines.Line)
    doc: Gui command for the BSpline tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. Activate the specific BSpline tracker.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view by the `Activated` method of the parent class. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def undolast(self)
        doc: Undo last line segment.
      - def drawUpdate(self, point)
        doc: Draw and update to the spline.
      - def finish(self, cont=False, closed=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation and close the spline if asked. Parameters ---------- cont: bool or None, optional Restart (continue) the command if `True`, or if `None` and `ui.continueMode` is `True`. closed: bool, optional Close the spline if `True`.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_stretch.py
classes:
  class Stretch(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the Stretch tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Proceed with execution of the command after proper selection.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def addPoint(self, point)
        doc: Add point to defined selection rectangle.
      - def numericInput(self, numx, numy, numz)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation of the command. and clean up.
      - def doStretch(self)
```

```text
classes:
  class SubelementHighlight(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the SubelementHighlight tool.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Continue with the command.
      - def finish(self)
        doc: Terminate the operation. Re-initialize by running __init__ again at the end.
      - def action(self, event)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- event: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def get_selection(self)
        doc: Get the selection.
      - def get_editable_objects_from_selection(self)
        doc: Get editable Draft objects for the selection.
      - def highlight_editable_objects(self)
        doc: Highlight editable Draft objects from the selection.
      - def restore_editable_objects_graphics(self)
        doc: Restore the editable objects' appearance.
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_togglemodes.py
classes:
  class BaseMode(gui_base.GuiCommandSimplest)
    doc: Base class for mode context GuiCommands. This is inherited by the other GuiCommand classes to run a set of similar actions when changing modes. It inherits `GuiCommandSimplest` to set up the document and other behavior. See this class for more information.
    methods:
      - def Activated(self, mode='None')
        doc: Execute when the command is called. Parameters ---------- action: str Indicates the type of mode to switch to. It can be `'construction'` or `'continue'`.
  class ToggleConstructionMode(BaseMode)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_ToggleConstructionMode tool. When construction mode is active, the following objects created will be included in the construction group, and will be drawn with the specified color and properties.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. It calls the `toggle()` method of the construction button in the `DraftToolbar` class.
  class ToggleDisplayMode(gui_base.GuiCommandNeedsSelection)
    doc: GuiCommand for the Draft_ToggleDisplayMode tool. Switches the display mode of selected objects from flatlines to wireframe and back. It inherits `GuiCommandNeedsSelection` to only be available when there is a document and a selection. See this class for more information.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called. It tests the view provider of the selected objects and changes their `DisplayMode` from `'Wireframe'` to `'Flat Lines'`, and the other way around, if possible.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_tool_utils.py
functions:
  - def get_mod_constrain_key()
  - def get_mod_snap_key()
  - def get_mod_alt_key()
  - def format_unit(exp, unit='mm')
    doc: Return a formatting string to set a number to the correct unit.
  - def select_object(arg)
    doc: Handle the selection of objects depending on buttons pressed. This is a scene event handler, to be called from the Draft tools when they need to select an object. :: self.call = self.view.addEventCallback("SoEvent", select_object) Parameters ---------- arg: Coin event The Coin event received from the 3D view. If it is of type Keyboard and the `ESCAPE` key, it runs the `finish` method of the active command. If Ctrl key is pressed, multiple selection is enabled until the button is released. Then i
  - def has_mod(args, mod)
    doc: Check if args has a specific modifier. Parameters ---------- args: Coin event The Coin event received from the 3D view. mod: str A string indicating the modifier, either `'shift'`, `'ctrl'`, or `'alt'`. Returns ------- bool It returns `args["ShiftDown"]`, `args["CtrlDown"]`, or `args["AltDown"]`, depending on the passed value of `mod`.
  - def set_mod(args, mod, state)
    doc: Set a specific modifier state in args. Parameters ---------- args: Coin event The Coin event received from the 3D view. mod: str A string indicating the modifier, either `'shift'`, `'ctrl'`, or `'alt'`. state: bool The boolean value of `state` is assigned to `args["ShiftDown"]`, `args["CtrlDown"]`, or `args["AltDown"]` depending on `mod`.
  - def get_point(target, args, noTracker=False)
    doc: Return a constrained 3D point and its original point. It is used by the Draft tools. Parameters ---------- target: object (class) The target object with a `node` attribute. If this is present, return the last node, otherwise return `None`. In the Draft tools, `target` is essentially the same class of the Gui command, that is, `self`. Therefore, this method probably makes more sense as a class method. args: Coin event The Coin event received from the 3D view. noTracker: bool, optional It defaults
  - def set_working_plane_to_object_under_cursor(mouseEvent)
    doc: Align the working plane to the face under the cursor. The working plane is only aligned if it is `'auto'`. Parameters ---------- mouseEvent: Coin event Coin mouse event. Returns ------- App::DocumentObject or None The parent object the face belongs to, if alignment occurred, or None.
  - def set_working_plane_to_selected_object()
    doc: Align the working plane to a preselected face. The working plane is only aligned if it is `'auto'`. Returns ------- App::DocumentObject or None The parent object the face belongs to, if alignment occurred, or None.
  - def get_support(mouseEvent=None)
    doc: "Align the working plane to a preselected face or the face under the cursor. The working plane is only aligned if it is `'auto'`. Parameters ---------- mouseEvent: Coin event, optional Defaults to `None`. Coin mouse event. Returns ------- App::DocumentObject or None The parent object the face belongs to, if alignment occurred, or None.
  - def redraw_3d_view()
    doc: Force a redraw of 3D view or do nothing if it fails.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftguitools/gui_trackers.py
classes:
  class Tracker
    doc: A generic Draft Tracker, to be used by other specific trackers.
    methods:
      - def finalize(self)
        doc: Finish the command by removing the switch. Also called by ghostTracker.remove.
      - def get_scene_graph(self)
        doc: Returns the current scenegraph or None if this is not a 3D view
      - def on(self)
        doc: Set the visibility to True.
      - def off(self)
        doc: Set the visibility to False.
      - def lowerTracker(self)
        doc: Lower the tracker to the bottom of the scenegraph. So it doesn't obscure the other objects.
      - def raiseTracker(self)
        doc: Raise the tracker to the top of the scenegraph. So it obscures the other objects.
      - def setColor(self, color=None)
        doc: Set the color.
  class snapTracker(Tracker)
    doc: Define Snap Mark tracker, used by tools that support snapping.
    methods:
      - def setMarker(self, style)
        doc: Set the marker index.
      - def setColor(self, color=None)
        doc: Set the color.
      - def setCoords(self, point)
        doc: Set the coordinates to the point.
      - def addCoords(self, point)
        doc: Add the point to the current point.
      - def clear(self)
        doc: Delete the values of the point.
  class lineTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A Line tracker, used by the tools that need to draw temporary lines
    methods:
      - def p1(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the first point of the line.
      - def p2(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the second point of the line.
      - def getLength(self)
        doc: Return the length of the line.
  class polygonTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A Polygon tracker, used by the polygon tool.
    methods:
      - def setNumVertices(self, num)
      - def setOrigin(self, point, radius=1.0)
        doc: Set the origin of the polygon and initialize the shape.
      - def update(self, point)
        doc: Update polygon size based on current mouse point.
  class rectangleTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A Rectangle tracker, used by the rectangle tool.
    methods:
      - def setorigin(self, point)
        doc: Set the base point of the rectangle.
      - def update(self, point)
        doc: Set the opposite (diagonal) point of the rectangle.
      - def setPlane(self, u, v=None)
        doc: Set given (u,v) vectors as working plane. You can give only `u` and `v` will be deduced automatically given the current working plane.
      - def p1(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the base point of the rectangle.
      - def p2(self)
        doc: Get the second point (on u axis) of the rectangle.
      - def p3(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the opposite (diagonal) point of the rectangle.
      - def p4(self)
        doc: Get the fourth point (on v axis) of the rectangle.
      - def getSize(self)
        doc: Return (length, width) of the rectangle.
      - def getNormal(self)
        doc: Return the normal of the rectangle.
      - def isInside(self, point)
        doc: Return True if the given point is inside the rectangle.
  class dimTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A Dimension tracker, used by the dimension tool.
    methods:
      - def update(self, pts)
        doc: Update the points and calculate.
      - def calc(self)
        doc: Calculate the new points from p1 and p2.
  class bsplineTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A bspline tracker.
    methods:
      - def update(self, points)
        doc: Update the points and recompute.
      - def recompute(self)
        doc: Recompute the tracker.
  class bezcurveTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A bezcurve tracker.
    methods:
      - def update(self, points, degree=None)
        doc: Update the points and recompute.
      - def recompute(self)
        doc: Recompute the tracker.
  class arcTracker(Tracker)
    doc: An arc tracker.
    methods:
      - def getDeviation(self)
        doc: Return a deviation vector that represents the base of the circle.
      - def setCenter(self, cen)
        doc: Set the center point.
      - def setRadius(self, rad)
        doc: Set the radius.
      - def getRadius(self)
        doc: Return the current radius.
      - def setStartAngle(self, ang)
        doc: Set the start angle.
      - def setEndAngle(self, ang)
        doc: Set the end angle.
      - def getAngle(self, pt)
        doc: Return the angle of a given vector in radians.
      - def getAngles(self)
        doc: Return the start and end angles in degrees.
      - def setStartPoint(self, pt)
        doc: Set the start angle from a point.
      - def setEndPoint(self, pt)
        doc: Set the end angle from a point.
      - def setApertureAngle(self, ang)
        doc: Set the end angle by giving the aperture angle.
      - def setBy3Points(self, p1, p2, p3)
        doc: Set the arc by three points.
      - def recompute(self)
        doc: Recompute the tracker.
  class ghostTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A Ghost tracker, that allows one to copy whole object representations. You can pass it an object or a list of objects, or a shape.
    methods:
      - def setColor(self, color=None)
        doc: Set the color.
      - def remove(self)
        doc: Remove the ghost when switching to and from subelement mode.
      - def move(self, delta)
        doc: Move the ghost to a given position. Relative from its start position.
      - def rotate(self, axis, angle)
        doc: Rotate the ghost of a given angle.
      - def center(self, point)
        doc: Set the rotation/scale center of the ghost.
      - def scale(self, delta)
        doc: Scale the ghost by the given factor.
      - def getNode(self, obj, parent_place=None)
        doc: Return a coin node representing the given object.
      - def getNodeFull(self, obj, parent_place=None)
        doc: Get a coin node which is a copy of the current representation.
      - def getNodeLight(self, shape)
        doc: Extract a lighter version directly from a shape.
      - def getMatrix(self)
        doc: Get matrix of the active view.
      - def setMatrix(self, matrix)
        doc: Set the transformation matrix. The 4th column of the matrix (the position) is ignored.
      - def flip_normals(self, flip)
  class editTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A node edit tracker.
    methods:
      - def set(self, pos)
        doc: Set the point to the position.
      - def get(self)
        doc: Get a vector from the point.
      - def get_doc_name(self)
        doc: Get the document name.
      - def get_obj_name(self)
        doc: Get the object name.
      - def get_subelement_name(self)
        doc: Get the subelement name.
      - def get_subelement_index(self)
        doc: Get the subelement index.
      - def move(self, delta)
        doc: Get the point and add a delta, and set the new point.
      - def setColor(self, color=None)
        doc: Set the color.
  class PlaneTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A working plane tracker.
    methods:
      - def set(self, pos=None)
        doc: Set the translation to the position.
  class wireTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A wire tracker.
    methods:
      - def update(self, wire, forceclosed=False)
        doc: Update the tracker.
      - def updateFromPointlist(self, points, forceclosed=False)
        doc: Update the tracker from points.
  class gridTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A grid tracker.
    methods:
      - def update(self)
        doc: Redraw the grid.
      - def getGridColors(self)
        doc: Returns grid colors stored in the preferences
      - def get_human_figure(self, loc=None)
        doc: Return a list of points defining a human figure, optionally translated to a given location. Based on "HumanFigure.brep" from the BIM Workbench.
      - def displayHumanFigure(self, wp)
        doc: Display the human figure at the grid corner. The silhouette is displayed only if: - preference BaseApp/Preferences/Mod/Draft/gridBorder is True; - preference BaseApp/Preferences/Mod/Draft/gridShowHuman is True; - the working plane normal is vertical.
      - def setAxesColor(self, wp)
        doc: set axes color
      - def setSize(self, size)
        doc: Set size of the lines and update.
      - def setSpacing(self, space)
        doc: Set spacing and update.
      - def setMainlines(self, ml)
        doc: Set mainlines and update.
      - def reset(self)
        doc: Reset the grid according to preferences settings.
      - def set(self)
        doc: Move and rotate the grid according to the current working plane.
      - def on(self)
        doc: Set the visibility to True and update the checked state of the grid button.
      - def off(self)
        doc: Set the visibility to False and update the checked state of the grid button.
      - def getClosestNode(self, point)
        doc: Return the closest node from the given point.
  class boxTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A box tracker, can be based on a line object.
    methods:
      - def update(self, line=None, normal=None)
        doc: Update the tracker.
      - def setRotation(self, rot)
        doc: Set the rotation.
      - def pos(self, p)
        doc: Set the translation.
      - def width(self, w=None)
        doc: Set the width.
      - def length(self, l=None)
        doc: Set the length.
      - def height(self, h=None)
        doc: Set the height.
  class radiusTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A tracker that displays a transparent sphere to indicate a radius.
    methods:
      - def update(self, arg1, arg2=None)
        doc: Update the tracker.
  class archDimTracker(Tracker)
    doc: A wrapper around a Sketcher dim.
    methods:
      - def setColor(self, color=None)
        doc: Set the color.
      - def setSize(self)
        doc: Set the text size.
      - def setString(self, text=None)
        doc: Set the dim string to the given value or auto value.
      - def setMode(self, mode=1)
        doc: Set the mode. 0 = without lines (a simple mark) 1 = aligned (default) 2 = horizontal 3 = vertical.
      - def p1(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the first point of the dim.
      - def p2(self, point=None)
        doc: Set or get the second point of the dim.
```

```text
classes:
  class Trimex(gui_base_original.Modifier)
    doc: Gui Command for the Trimex tool. This tool trims or extends lines, wires and arcs, or extrudes single faces. SHIFT constrains to the last point or extrudes in direction to the face normal.
    methods:
      - def GetResources(self)
        doc: Set icon, menu and tooltip.
      - def Activated(self)
        doc: Execute when the command is called.
      - def proceed(self)
        doc: Proceed with execution of the command after proper selection.
      - def action(self, arg)
        doc: Handle the 3D scene events. This is installed as an EventCallback in the Inventor view. Parameters ---------- arg: dict Dictionary with strings that indicates the type of event received from the 3D view.
      - def extrude(self, shift=False, real=False)
        doc: Redraw the ghost in extrude mode.
      - def redraw(self, point, snapped=None, shift=False, alt=False, real=None)
        doc: Redraw the ghost normally.
      - def trimObject(self)
        doc: Trim the actual object.
      - def trimObjects(self, objectslist)
        doc: Attempt to trim two objects together.
      - def finish(self, cont=False)
        doc: Terminate the operation of the Trimex tool.
      - def numericRadius(self, dist)
        doc: Validate the entry fields in the user interface. This function is called by the toolbar or taskpanel interface when valid x, y, and z have been entered in the input fields.
      - def get_action_hints(self)
```

```text
functions:
  - def make_arc_3points(points, placement=None, face=False, support=None, primitive=False)
    doc: Draw a circular arc defined by three points on the circumference. Parameters ---------- points: list of Base::Vector3 A list that must be three points. placement: Base::Placement, optional It is adjusted to match the geometry of the created edge. The Z axis of the adjusted placement will be parallel to the (negative) edge axis. Its Base will match the edge center. face: bool, optional It defaults to `False`. If it is `True` it will create a face in the closed arc. Otherwise only the circumferenc
```

Rectangular array ----------------- make_array(object, xvector, yvector, xnum, ynum) make_array(object, xvector, yvector, zvector, xnum, ynum, znum) xnum of iterations in the x direction at xvector distance between iterations, same for y direction with yvector and ynum, same for z direction with zvector and znum. Polar array ----------- make_array(object, center, totalangle, totalnum) for polar array, or center is a vector, totalangle is the angle to cov

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftmake/make_circle.py
functions:
  - def make_circle(radius, placement=None, face=None, startangle=None, endangle=None, support=None)
    doc: make_circle(radius, [placement], [face], [startangle], [endangle]) or make_circle(edge, [placement], [face]): Creates a circle object with given parameters. If startangle and endangle are provided and not equal, the object will be an arc instead of a full circle. Parameters ---------- radius: the radius of the circle or the shape of a circular edge If it is an edge, startangle and endangle are ignored. edge.Curve must be a Part.Circle. placement: optional If radius is an edge, placement is adjus
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftmake/make_clone.py
functions:
  - def make_clone(obj, delta=None, forcedraft=False)
    doc: clone(obj,[delta,forcedraft]) Makes a clone of the given object(s). The clone is an exact, linked copy of the given object. If the original object changes, the final object changes too. Parameters ---------- obj : delta : Base.Vector Delta Vector to move the clone from the original position. forcedraft : bool If forcedraft is True, the resulting object is a Draft clone even if the input object is an Arch object.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftmake/make_copy.py
functions:
  - def make_copy(obj, force=None, reparent=False, simple_copy=False)
    doc: make_copy(object, [force], [reparent], [simple_copy]) Make an exact copy of an object and return it. Parameters ---------- obj : Object to copy. force : Obsolete, not used anymore. reparent : Group the new object in the same group of the old one. simple_copy : Create a simple copy of the object (a new non parametric Part::Feature with the same Shape as the given object).
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftmake/make_dimension.py
functions:
  - def make_dimension(p1, p2, p3=None, p4=None)
    doc: Create one of three types of dimension objects. In all dimensions the p3 parameter defines a point through which the dimension line will go through. The current line width and color will be used. Linear dimension ---------------- - (p1, p2, p3): a simple linear dimension from p1 to p2 - (object, i1, i2, p3): creates a linked dimension to the provided object (edge), measuring the distance between its vertices indexed i1 and i2 Circular dimension ------------------ - (arc, i1, mode, p3): creates a
  - def makeDimension(p1, p2, p3=None, p4=None)
    doc: Create a dimension. DEPRECATED. Use 'make_dimension'.
  - def make_linear_dimension(p1, p2, dim_line=None)
    doc: Create a free linear dimension from two main points. Parameters ---------- p1: Base::Vector3 First point of the measurement. p2: Base::Vector3 Second point of the measurement. dim_line: Base::Vector3, optional It defaults to `None`. This is a point through which the extension of the dimension line will pass. This point controls how close or how far the dimension line is positioned from the measured segment that goes from `p1` to `p2`. If it is `None`, this point will be calculated from the inter
  - def make_linear_dimension_obj(edge_object, i1=1, i2=2, dim_line=None)
    doc: Create a linear dimension from an object. Parameters ---------- edge_object: Part::Feature The object which has an edge which will be measured. It must have a `Part::TopoShape`, and at least one element in `Shape.Vertexes`, to be able to measure a distance. i1: int, optional It defaults to `1`. It is the index of the first vertex in `edge_object` from which the measurement will be taken. The minimum value should be `1`, which will be interpreted as `'Vertex1'`. If the value is below `1`, it will
  - def make_radial_dimension_obj(edge_object, index=1, mode='radius', dim_line=None)
    doc: Create a radial or diameter dimension from an arc object. Parameters ---------- edge_object: Part::Feature The object which has a circular edge which will be measured. It must have a `Part::TopoShape`, and at least one element must be a circular edge in `Shape.Edges` to be able to measure its radius. index: int, optional It defaults to `1`. It is the index of the edge in `edge_object` which is going to be measured. The minimum value should be `1`, which will be interpreted as `'Edge1'`. If the v
  - def make_angular_dimension(center=App.Vector(0, 0, 0), angles=None, dim_line=App.Vector(10, 10, 0), normal=None)
    doc: Create an angular dimension from the given center and angles. Parameters ---------- center: Base::Vector3, optional It defaults to the origin `Vector(0, 0, 0)`. Center of the dimension line, which is a circular arc. angles: list of two floats, optional It defaults to `[0, 90]`. It is a list of two angles, given in degrees, that determine the aperture of the dimension line, that is, of the circular arc. It is drawn counter-clockwise. :: angles = [0 90] angles = [330 60] # the arc crosses the X ax
  - def makeAngularDimension(center, angles, p3, normal=None)
    doc: Create an angle dimension. DEPRECATED. Use 'make_angular_dimension'.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/array.py
functions:
  - def rect_placements(base_placement, xvector, yvector, zvector, xnum, ynum, znum)
    doc: Determine the placements where the rectangular copies will be.
  - def polar_placements(base_placement, center, angle, number, axis, axisvector)
    doc: Determine the placements where the polar copies will be.
  - def circ_placements(base_placement, r_distance, tan_distance, axis, center, circle_number, symmetry)
    doc: Determine the placements where the circular copies will be.
classes:
  class Array(DraftLink)
    doc: The Draft Array object. To Do ----- The `Array` class currently handles three types of arrays, orthogonal, polar, and circular. In the future, probably they should be split in separate classes so that they are easier to manage.
    methods:
      - def attach(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the properties when the object is attached.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_general_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set general properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_ortho_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set orthogonal properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_polar_circular_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set general polar and circular properties if they don't exist.
      - def set_polar_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set polar properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_circular_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set circular properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_link_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set link properties only if they don't exist.
      - def linkSetup(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the object as a link object.
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Execute when a property is changed.
      - def show_and_hide(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Show and hide the properties depending on the touched property.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/base.py
classes:
  class DraftObject(object)
    doc: The base class for Draft objects. Parameters ---------- obj : a base C++ object The base object instantiated during creation, which commonly may be of types `Part::Part2DObjectPython`, `Part::FeaturePython`, or `App::FeaturePython`. >>> obj = App.ActiveDocument.addObject('Part::Part2DObjectPython') >>> DraftObject(obj) This class instance is stored in the `Proxy` attribute of the base object. :: obj.Proxy = self tp : str, optional It defaults to `'Unknown'`. It indicates the type of this scripte
    methods:
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: Return a tuple of all serializable objects or None. When saving the document this object gets stored using Python's `json` module. Override this method to define the serializable objects to return. By default it returns the `Type` attribute. Returns ------- str Returns the value of `Type`
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: Set some internal properties for all restored objects. When a document is restored this method is used to set some properties for the objects stored with `json`. Override this method to define the properties to change for the restored serialized objects. By default the `Type` was serialized, so `state` holds this value, which is re-assigned to the 
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Run this method when the object is created or recomputed. Override this method to produce effects when the object is newly created, and whenever the document is recomputed. By default it does nothing. Parameters ---------- obj : the scripted object. This commonly may be of types `Part::Part2DObjectPython`, `Part::FeaturePython`, or `App::FeaturePyt
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Run this method when a property is changed. Override this method to handle the behavior of the object depending on changes that occur to its properties. By default it does nothing. Parameters ---------- obj : the scripted object. This commonly may be of types `Part::Part2DObjectPython`, `Part::FeaturePython`, or `App::FeaturePython`. prop : str Nam
      - def props_changed_store(self, prop)
        doc: Store the name of the property that will be changed in the self.props_changed list. The function should be called at the start of onChanged or onBeforeChange. The list is used to detect Placement-only changes. In such cases the Shape of an object does not need to be updated. Not updating the Shape avoids Uno/Redo issues for the Windows version and 
      - def props_changed_clear(self)
        doc: Remove the self.props_changed attribute if it exists. The function should be called just before execute returns.
      - def props_changed_placement_only(self, obj=None)
        doc: Return `True` if the self.props_changed list, after removing `_LinkTouched`, `Shape`, `Density`, `Volume` and `Mass` items, only contains `Placement` items. Parameters ---------- obj : the scripted object. Need only be supplied if the Shape of obj is, or can be, derived from other objects.
```

```text
classes:
  class BezCurve(DraftObject)
    doc: The BezCurve object
    methods:
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)

      - def resetcontinuity(self, fp)

        doc: Make two poles have the same tangent at knot.

        doc: calculate the coordinates of the opposite pole of a tangent knot
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/bspline.py
classes:
  class BSpline(DraftObject)
    doc: The BSpline object
    methods:
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def assureProperties(self, obj)
      - def parameterization(self, pts, a, closed)
        doc: Computes a knot Sequence for a set of points. fac (0-1) : parameterization factor fac = 0 -> Uniform / fac=0.5 -> Centripetal / fac=1.0 -> Chord-Length
      - def onChanged(self, fp, prop)
      - def execute(self, obj)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/dimension.py
functions:
  - def measure_one_obj_edge(obj, subelement, dim_point, diameter=False)
    doc: Measure one object with one subelement, a straight or circular edge. Parameters ---------- obj: Part::Feature The object that is measured. subelement: str The subelement that is measured, for example, `'Edge1'`. dim_line: Base::Vector3 A point through which the dimension goes through.
  - def measure_one_obj_vertices(obj, subelements)
    doc: Measure two vertices in the same object.
  - def measure_two_objects(link_sub_1, link_sub_2)
    doc: Measure two vertices from two different objects. Parameters ---------- link_sub_1: tuple A tuple containing one object and a list of subelement strings, which may be empty. Only the first subelement is considered, which must be a vertex. :: link_sub_1 = (obj1, ['VertexN', ...]) link_sub_2: tuple Same.
  - def measure_two_obj_angles(link_sub_1, link_sub_2)
    doc: Measure two edges from two different objects to measure the angle. This function is a prototype because it does not determine all possible starting and ending angles that could be used to draw the dimension line, which is a circular arc. Parameters ---------- link_sub_1: tuple A tuple containing one object and a list of subelement strings, which may be empty. Only the first subelement is considered, which must be an edge. :: link_sub_1 = (obj1, ['EdgeN', ...]) link_sub_2: tuple Same.
classes:
  class DimensionBase(DraftAnnotation)
    doc: The base objects for dimension objects. This class inherits `DraftAnnotation` to define the basic properties of all annotation type objects, like a scaling multiplier. This class is not used directly, but inherited by all dimension objects.
    methods:
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set basic properties only if they don't exist.
      - def update_properties_0v21(self, obj, vobj)
        doc: Update view properties.
  class LinearDimension(DimensionBase)
    doc: The linear dimension object. This inherits `DimensionBase` to provide the basic functionality of a dimension. This linear dimension includes measurements between two vertices, but also a radial dimension of a circular edge or arc.
    methods:
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set basic properties only if they don't exist.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: Execute code when the document is restored.
      - def loads(self, state)
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Execute when a property is changed. It just sets some properties to be read-only or hidden, as they aren't used.
      - def transform(self, obj, pla)
        doc: Transform the object by applying a placement.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed. Set start point and end point according to the linked geometry and the number of subelements. If it has one subelement, we assume a straight edge or a circular edge. If it has two subelements, we assume a straight edge (two vertices).
  class AngularDimension(DimensionBase)
    doc: The angular dimension object. This inherits `DimensionBase` to provide the basic functionality of a dimension.
    methods:
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set basic properties only if they don't exist.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: Execute code when the document is restored.
      - def loads(self, state)
      - def transform(self, obj, pla)
        doc: Transform the object by applying a placement.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed. Nothing is actually done here, except update the viewprovider, as the lines and text are created in the viewprovider.
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Execute when a property is changed. It just sets some properties to be read-only or hidden, as they aren't used.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/draft_annotation.py
classes:
  class DraftAnnotation(object)
    doc: The Draft Annotation Base object. This class is not used directly, but inherited by all Draft annotation objects. LinearDimension through DimensionBase AngularDimension through DimensionBase Label Text
    methods:
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: Execute code when the document is restored. Check if new properties are present after the object is restored in order to migrate older objects.
      - def add_missing_properties_0v19(self, obj, vobj)
        doc: Provide missing annotation properties.
      - def update_properties_1v1(self, obj, vobj)

      - def dumps(self)
      - def loads(self, state)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/draftlink.py
classes:
  class DraftLink(DraftObject)
    doc: New class to use the App::Link objects in arrays. Introduced by realthunder. This is subclassed by `draftobjects.array.Array` and by `draftobjects.patharray.PathArray`.
    methods:
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: Return a tuple of all serializable objects or None.
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: Set some internal properties for all restored objects.
      - def attach(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the properties when the object is attached.
      - def canLinkProperties(self, _obj)
        doc: Link properties. TODO: add more explanation. Overrides a C++ method?
      - def linkSetup(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the link properties on attachment.
      - def getViewProviderName(self, _obj)
        doc: Override the view provider name.
      - def migrate_attributes(self, obj)
        doc: Migrate old attribute names to new names if they exist. This is done to comply with Python guidelines or fix small issues in older code.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: Execute code when the document in restored.
      - def buildShape(self, obj, pl, pls)
        doc: Build the shape of the link object.
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Execute when a property changes.
      - def getPlacementOf(self, fp, subname, targetObj=None)
        doc: Return the placement of the sub-object relative to the link object.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/layer.py
functions:

    doc: Get the layer the object belongs to.
classes:
  class Layer
    doc: The Layer object. This class is normally used to extend a base `App::FeaturePython` object.
    methods:
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set properties only if they don't exist.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
        doc: Execute code when the document is restored.
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: Return a tuple of objects to save or None.
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: Set the internal properties from the restored state.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed. Do nothing.
      - def onBeforeChange(self, obj, prop)
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
      - def addObject(self, obj, child)
      - def removeObject(self, obj, child)
  class LayerContainer
    doc: The container object for layers. This class is normally used to extend a base `App::DocumentObjectGroupPython` object.
    methods:
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed. Update the value of `Group` by sorting the contained layers by `Label`.
      - def dumps(self)
        doc: Return a tuple of objects to save or None.
      - def loads(self, state)
        doc: Set the internal properties from the restored state.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/draftobjects/patharray.py
functions:
  - def placements_on_path(shapeRotation, pathwire, count, xlate, align, mode='Original', forceNormal=False, normalOverride=None, startOffset=0.0, endOffset=0.0, reversePath=False, spacingMode='Fixed count', spacingUnit=20.0, useSpacingPattern=False, spacingPattern=[1, 1, 1, 1])
    doc: Calculate the placements of a shape along a given path. Copies will be distributed according to spacing mode - evenly or in fixed offsets.
  - def calculate_placement(globalRotation, edge, offset, RefPt, xlate, align, normal=App.Vector(0.0, 0.0, 1.0), mode='Original', overrideNormal=False, reversePath=False)
    doc: Orient shape in the local coordinate system at parameter offset. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euler_angles (previous version) http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quaternions
  - def get_parameter_from_v0(edge, offset)
    doc: Return parameter at distance offset from edge.Vertexes[0]. sb method in Part.TopoShapeEdge???
classes:
  class PathArray(DraftLink)
    doc: The Draft Path Array object. The object distributes copies of an object along a path like a polyline, spline, or bezier curve. Attributes ---------- Align: bool It defaults to `False`. It sets whether the object will be specially aligned to the path. AlignMode: str It defaults to `'Original'`. Indicates the type of alignment that will be calculated when `Align` is `True`. `'Original'` mode is the historic `'Align'` for old (v0.18) documents. It is not really the Fernat alignment. It uses the nor
    methods:
      - def attach(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the properties when the object is attached. Note: we don't exactly know why the properties are added in the `attach` method. They should probably be added in the `__init__` method. Maybe this is related to the link behavior of this class. For PathLinkArray, DraftLink.attach creates the link to the Base. Realthunder: before the big merge, the
      - def set_properties(self, obj)
        doc: Set properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_general_properties(self, obj, properties)
        doc: Set general properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_align_properties(self, obj, properties)
        doc: Set general properties only if they don't exist.
      - def set_spacing_properties(self, obj, properties)
      - def linkSetup(self, obj)
        doc: Set up the object as a link object.
      - def execute(self, obj)
        doc: Execute when the object is created or recomputed.
      - def get_wires(self, path_object, subelements)
        doc: Get wires from the path object.
      - def get_wire_from_subelements(self, subelements)
        doc: Make a wire from the path object subelements.
      - def onChanged(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Execute when a property is changed.
      - def show_and_hide(self, obj, prop)
        doc: Show and hide the properties depending on the touched property. Note that when the array is created, some properties will change more than once in a seemingly random order.
      - def onDocumentRestored(self, obj)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/drafttaskpanels/task_circulararray.py
classes:
  class TaskPanelCircularArray
    doc: TaskPanel code for the CircularArray command. The names of the widgets are defined in the `.ui` file. This `.ui` file `must` be loaded into an attribute called `self.form` so that it is loaded into the task panel correctly. In this class all widgets are automatically created as `self.form.<widget_name>`. The `.ui` file may use special FreeCAD-compatible runtime widgets such as `Gui::InputField` (based on `QLineEdit`) and `Gui::QuantitySpinBox` (based on `QAbstractSpinBox`). See the Doxygen docum
    methods:
      - def set_widget_callbacks(self)
        doc: Set up the callbacks (slots) for the widget signals.
      - def accept(self)
        doc: Execute when clicking the OK button or Enter key.
      - def validate_input(self, selection, r_distance, tan_distance, number, symmetry, axis, center)
        doc: Check that the input is valid. Some values may not need to be checked because the interface may not allow one to input wrong data.
      - def create_object(self)
        doc: Create the new object. At this stage we already tested that the input is correct so the necessary attributes are already set. Then we proceed with the internal function to create the new object.
      - def get_distances(self)
        doc: Get the distance parameters from the widgets.
      - def get_number_symmetry(self)
        doc: Get the number and symmetry parameters from the widgets.
      - def get_center(self)
        doc: Get the value of the center from the widgets.
      - def constrain_point(self, point, last=None)
        doc: Apply locked center coordinates to a snapped point.
      - def get_axis(self)
        doc: Get the axis that will be used for the array. NOT IMPLEMENTED. It should consider a second selection of an edge or wire to use as an axis.
      - def reset_point(self)
        doc: Reset the center point to the original distance.
      - def print_fuse_state(self, fuse)
        doc: Print the fuse state translated.
      - def set_fuse(self)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the fuse checkbox changes.
      - def print_link_state(self, use_link)
        doc: Print the link state translated.
      - def set_link(self)
        doc: Execute as a callback when the link checkbox changes.
      - def print_messages(self)
        doc: Print messages about the operation.
      - def display_point(self, point=None, plane=None, mask=None)
        doc: Display the coordinates in the x, y, and z widgets. This function should be used in a Coin callback so that the coordinate values are automatically updated when the mouse pointer moves. This was copied from `DraftGui.py` but needs to be improved for this particular command. point: Base::Vector3 is a vector that arrives by the callback. plane: Worki
      - def set_focus(self, key=None)
        doc: Set the focus on the widget that receives the key signal.
      - def reject(self)
        doc: Execute when clicking the Cancel button or pressing Escape.
      - def finish(self)
        doc: Finish the command, after accept or reject. It finally calls the parent class to execute the delayed functions, and perform cleanup.
```

This was copied from `DraftGui.py` but needs to be improved for this particular command. point : is a vector that arrives by the callback. plane : is a `WorkingPlan

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/drafttaskpanels/task_scale.py
classes:
  class ScaleTaskPanel
    doc: The task panel for the Draft Scale tool.
    methods:
      - def setValue(self, val=None)
        doc: Set the value of the scale factors.
      - def setLock(self, state)
        doc: Set the uniform scaling.
      - def setRelative(self, state)
        doc: Set the relative scaling.
      - def setCopy(self, state)
        doc: Set the copy option.
      - def setSubelementMode(self, state)
        doc: Set the subelement option.
      - def setClone(self, state)
        doc: Set the clone option.
      - def pickRef(self)
        doc: Pick a reference point from the calling class.
      - def accept(self)
        doc: Execute when clicking the OK button.
      - def reject(self)
        doc: Execute when clicking the Cancel button.
  class ScaleTaskPanelEdit(ScaleTaskPanel)
    doc: The task panel to edit the scale of Draft Clones.
    methods:
      - def setValue(self, val=None)
        doc: Set the value of the scale factors.
      - def scale_ghost(self, x, y, z)
        doc: Scale the preview of the object.
      - def set_ghost(self)
        doc: Set the ghost to display.

        doc: Execute when clicking the OK button.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/drafttests/test_dimension_gui.py
classes:
  class DraftGuiDimension(test_base.DraftTestCaseDoc)
    doc: GUI regressions for Draft dimensions.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
        doc: Set up a temporary Draft document and keep a stable name for teardown.
      - def tearDown(self)
        doc: Close the temporary document even if save/reopen invalidated the Python wrapper.
      - def getAngularOvershootState(self, dimension)
        doc: Return the angular overshoot scenegraph state used by the regression checks.
      - def assertRotationAlmostEqual(self, actual, expected)
        doc: Compare Coin quaternion values with a tolerance.
      - def normalizeAngleDegrees(self, angle)
        doc: Normalize an SVG rotation angle so wraparound is easy to compare.
      - def angleDeltaDegrees(self, actual, expected)
        doc: Return the shortest distance between two rotation angles.
      - def makeAngularDimension(self)
        doc: Create a stable angular dimension setup shared by the GUI regressions.
      - def getAngularSvg(self, dimension)
        doc: Return the Draft SVG body used by TechDraw DraftView for this dimension.
      - def extractOvershootLines(self, svg_text)
        doc: Extract overshoot line transforms from Draft SVG output.
      - def getExpectedAngularSvgOvershoots(self, dimension)
        doc: Compute the expected TechDraw overshoot lines from the angular geometry.
      - def assertOvershootLinesMatch(self, actual_lines, expected_lines)
        doc: Match SVG overshoot lines by anchor, angle, and length.
      - def assertAngularSvgOvershoots(self, dimension, expected_lines=None, svg_text=None)
        doc: Assert the Draft SVG path exposes the angular overshoot lines.
      - def createTechDrawDraftView(self, doc, source)
        doc: Create a TechDraw DraftView for the provided angular dimension.
      - def assertAngularOvershoots(self, dimension, expected_state=None)
        doc: Assert that the angular dimension view provider drew both overshoot marks.
      - def test_angular_dimension_draws_overshoots_after_restore(self)
        doc: Angular dimensions keep both overshoot marks after saving and reopening a document.
      - def test_angular_dimension_svg_overshoots_after_restore_and_in_techdraw(self)
        doc: Angular dimension overshoots survive restore in SVG and propagate into TechDraw DraftView.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/drafttests/test_manual_input_gui.py
classes:
  class DraftGuiManualInput(test_base.DraftTestCaseDoc)
    doc: GUI regressions for manual point-input field locking in DraftGui.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def test_unlock_polar_fields_clears_length_and_angle(self)
        doc: _unlock_polar_fields must drop length and angle locks.
      - def test_unlock_cartesian_fields_clears_x_y_z(self)
        doc: _unlock_cartesian_fields must drop X, Y, and Z locks.
      - def test_editing_x_unlocks_length_and_angle(self)
        doc: Editing X must drop length and angle locks (polar group).
      - def test_editing_length_unlocks_cartesian_fields(self)
        doc: Editing length must drop X, Y, and Z locks (cartesian group).
      - def test_constrain_point_overrides_locked_cartesian_component(self)
        doc: A locked X component must replace the candidate point's X.
      - def test_constrain_point_preserves_locked_length(self)
        doc: A locked length must rescale the candidate vector to that length.
      - def test_angle_lock_controls_direction_without_locking_length(self)
      - def test_angle_lock_preserves_established_off_plane_direction(self)
      - def test_length_lock_controls_magnitude_without_locking_direction(self)
      - def test_angle_input_replaces_coordinate_locks_in_preview_and_result(self)
      - def test_length_input_replaces_coordinate_locks_without_locking_angle(self)
      - def test_length_lock_clears_existing_axis_constraint(self)
      - def test_length_lock_preserves_existing_angle_lock(self)
      - def test_angle_and_length_locks_allow_opposite_direction(self)
      - def test_snap_marker_uses_resolved_angle_point(self)
      - def test_enter_locks_valid_input_and_unit_only_input_stays_unlocked(self)
      - def test_typing_locks_the_current_value_after_inputfield_parsing(self)
      - def test_point_constraint_provider_follows_task_panel_lifetime(self)
      - def test_array_panels_constrain_locked_center_coordinates(self)
      - def test_deleting_numeric_part_unlocks_field(self)
      - def test_lock_icon_and_double_click_unlock_field(self)
      - def test_arrow_nudge_updates_locked_value(self)
      - def test_zero_snap_result_is_not_replaced_by_cursor_point(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Draft/drafttests/test_modification.py
classes:
  class DraftModification(test_base.DraftTestCaseDoc)
    doc: Test Draft modification tools.
    methods:
      - def test_move(self)
        doc: Create a line and move it.
      - def test_copy(self)
        doc: Create a line, then copy and move it.
      - def test_rotate(self)
        doc: Create a line, then rotate it.
      - def test_offset_open(self)
        doc: Create an open wire, then produce an offset copy.
      - def test_offset_closed_with_reversed_edge(self)
        doc: Create a closed wire with a reversed edge, then produce an offset copy.
      - def test_offset_rectangle_with_face(self)
        doc: Create a rectangle with a face, then produce an offset copy.
      - def test_trim(self)
        doc: Trim a line. NOT IMPLEMENTED.
      - def test_extend(self)
        doc: Extend a line. NOT IMPLEMENTED.
      - def test_join(self)
        doc: Join two lines into a single Draft Wire.
      - def test_split(self)
        doc: Split a Draft Wire into two Draft Wires.
      - def test_split_at_endpoint(self)
        doc: Split a Draft Wire at its endpoints should be a no-op.
      - def test_upgrade(self)
        doc: Upgrade two Lines into a closed Wire, then draftify it.
      - def test_downgrade(self)
        doc: Downgrade a closed Draft Wire into three simple Part Edges.
      - def test_wire_to_bspline(self)
        doc: Convert a polyline to BSpline and back.
      - def test_shape_2d_view(self)
        doc: Create a prism and then a 2D projection of it.
      - def test_draft_to_sketch(self)
        doc: Convert a Draft object to a Sketch and back.
      - def test_rectangular_array(self)
        doc: Create a rectangle, and a rectangular array.
      - def test_polar_array(self)
        doc: Create a rectangle, and a polar array.
      - def test_circular_array(self)
        doc: Create a rectangle, and a circular array.
      - def test_path_array(self)
        doc: Create a wire, a polygon, and a path array.
      - def test_point_array(self)
        doc: Create a polygon, various point, and a point array.
      - def test_clone(self)
        doc: Create a box, then create a clone of it. Test for a bug introduced by changes in attachment code.
      - def test_attached_clone_behavior(self)
        doc: Check if an attached clone behaves correctly. Test for https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/8771.
      - def test_draft_to_techdraw(self)
        doc: Create a solid, and then a DraftView on a TechDraw page.
      - def test_mirror(self)
        doc: Create a rectangle, then a mirrored shape.
      - def test_stretch(self)
        doc: Stretch a line. NOT IMPLEMENTED.
      - def test_scale_part_feature_arcs(self)
        doc: Create and scale a part feature (arcs).
      - def test_scale_part_feature_lines(self)
        doc: Create and scale a part feature (lines).
      - def test_scale_rectangle(self)
        doc: Create and scale a rectangle.
      - def test_scale_spline(self)
        doc: Create and scale a spline.
      - def test_scale_wire(self)
        doc: Create and scale a wire.
```
