```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/ObjectsFem.py
functions:
  - def makeAnalysis(doc, name='Analysis')
    doc: makeAnalysis(document, [name]): makes a Fem Analysis object
  - def makeConstantVacuumPermittivity(doc, name='ConstantVacuumPermittivity')
    doc: makeConstantVacuumPermittivity(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstantVacuumPermittivity object
  - def makeConstraintBearing(doc, name='ConstraintBearing')
    doc: makeConstraintBearing(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintBearing object
  - def makeConstraintBodyHeatSource(doc, name='BodyHeatSource')
    doc: makeConstraintBodyHeatSource(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintBodyHeatSource object
  - def makeConstraintCentrif(doc, name='CentrifugalForce')
    doc: makeConstraintCentrif(document, [name]): creates a centrif object to define centrifugal body load constraint
  - def makeConstraintCurrentDensity(doc, name='CurrentDensity')
    doc: makeConstraintCurrentDensity(document, [name]): makes a Fem CurrentDensity object
  - def makeConstraintContact(doc, name='Contact')
    doc: makeConstraintContact(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintContact object
  - def makeConstraintDisplacement(doc, name='Displacement')
    doc: makeConstraintDisplacement(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintDisplacement object
  - def makeConstraintElectricChargeDensity(doc, name='ElectricCharge')
    doc: makeConstraintElectricChargeDensity(document, [name]): makes a Fem ElectricChargeDensity object
  - def makeConstraintElectromagnetic(doc, name='Electromagnetic')
    doc: makeConstraintElectromagnetic(document, [name]): makes a Fem Electromagnetic object
  - def makeConstraintFixed(doc, name='Fixed')
    doc: makeConstraintFixed(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintFixed object
  - def makeConstraintRigidBody(doc, name='RigidBody')
    doc: makeConstraintRigidBody(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintRigidBody object
  - def makeConstraintFlowVelocity(doc, name='FlowVelocity')
    doc: makeConstraintFlowVelocity(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintFlowVelocity object
  - def makeConstraintFluidBoundary(doc, name='FluidBoundary')
    doc: makeConstraintFluidBoundary(document, name): makes a Fem ConstraintFluidBoundary object
  - def makeConstraintForce(doc, name='Force')
    doc: makeConstraintForce(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintForce object
  - def makeConstraintGear(doc, name='ConstraintGear')
    doc: makeConstraintGear(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintGear object
  - def makeConstraintHeatflux(doc, name='HeatFlux')
    doc: makeConstraintHeatflux(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintHeatflux object
  - def makeConstraintInitialFlowVelocity(doc, name='InitialFlowVelocity')
    doc: makeConstraintInitialFlowVelocity(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintInitialFlowVelocity object
  - def makeConstraintInitialPressure(doc, name='InitialPressure')
    doc: makeConstraintInitialPressure(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintInitialPressure object
  - def makeConstraintInitialTemperature(doc, name='InitialTemperature')
    doc: makeConstraintInitialTemperature(document, name): makes a Fem ConstraintInitialTemperature object
  - def makeConstraintMagnetization(doc, name='Magnetization')
    doc: makeConstraintMagnetization(document, [name]): makes a Fem Magnetization object
  - def makeConstraintPlaneRotation(doc, name='PlaneRotation')
    doc: makeConstraintPlaneRotation(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintPlaneRotation object
  - def makeConstraintPressure(doc, name='Pressure')
    doc: makeConstraintPressure(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintPressure object
  - def makeConstraintPulley(doc, name='Pulley')
    doc: makeConstraintPulley(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintPulley object
  - def makeConstraintSelfWeight(doc, name='Gravity')
    doc: makeConstraintSelfWeight(document, [name]): creates a self weight object to define a gravity load
  - def makeConstraintTemperature(doc, name='Temperature')
    doc: makeConstraintTemperature(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintTemperature object
  - def makeConstraintTie(doc, name='Tie')
    doc: makeConstraintTie(document, [name]): creates a tie object to define bonded faces constraint
  - def makeConstraintTransform(doc, name='Transform')
    doc: makeConstraintTransform(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintTransform object
  - def makeConstraintSectionPrint(doc, name='SectionPrint')
    doc: makeConstraintSectionPrint(document, [name]): creates a section print object to evaluate forces and moments of defined face
  - def makeConstraintSpring(doc, name='Spring')
    doc: makeConstraintSpring(document, [name]): makes a Fem ConstraintSpring object
  - def makeElementFluid1D(doc, name='ElementFluid1D')
    doc: makeElementFluid1D(document, [name]): creates a 1D fluid element object to define 1D flow
  - def makeElementGeometry1D(doc, sectiontype='Rectangular', width=10.0, height=25.0, thickness=2.0, name='ElementGeometry1D')
    doc: makeElementGeometry1D(document, [width], [height], [name]): creates a 1D geometry element object to define a cross section
  - def makeElementGeometry2D(doc, thickness=1.0, name='ElementGeometry2D')
    doc: makeElementGeometry2D(document, [thickness], [name]): creates a 2D geometry element object to define a plate thickness
  - def makeElementRotation1D(doc, name='ElementRotation1D')
    doc: makeElementRotation1D(document, [name]): creates a 1D geometry rotation element object to rotate a 1D cross section
  - def makeMaterialFluid(doc, name='MaterialFluid')
    doc: makeMaterialFluid(document, [name]): makes a FEM Material for fluid
  - def makeMaterialMechanicalNonlinear(doc, base_material, name='MaterialMechanicalNonlinear')
    doc: makeMaterialMechanicalNonlinear(document, base_material, [name]): creates a nonlinear material object
  - def makeMaterialReinforced(doc, name='MaterialReinforced')
    doc: makeMaterialReinforced(document, [matrix_material], [reinforcement_material], [name]): creates a reinforced material object
  - def makeMaterialSolid(doc, name='MaterialSolid')
    doc: makeMaterialSolid(document, [name]): makes a FEM Material for solid
  - def makeMeshBoundaryLayer(doc, base_mesh, name='MeshBoundaryLayer')
    doc: makeMeshBoundaryLayer(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh BoundaryLayer object to define boundary layer properties
  - def makeMeshGmsh(doc, name='MeshGmsh')
    doc: makeMeshGmsh(document, [name]): makes a Gmsh FEM mesh object
  - def makeMeshGroup(doc, base_mesh, use_label=False, name='MeshGroup')
    doc: makeMeshGroup(document, base_mesh, [use_label], [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement object to define properties for a region of a FEM mesh
  - def makeMeshNetgen(doc, name='MeshNetgen')
    doc: makeMeshNetgen(document, [name]): makes a Netgen FEM mesh object
  - def makeMeshNetgenLegacy(doc, name='MeshNetgen')
    doc: makeMeshNetgenLegacy(document, [name]): makes a old implementation Netgen FEM mesh object
  - def makeMeshRegion(doc, base_mesh, element_length=0.0, name='MeshRegion')
    doc: makeMeshRegion(document, base_mesh, [element_length], [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement object to define properties for a refinement of a FEM mesh
  - def makeMeshDistance(doc, base_mesh, name='Distance')
    doc: makeMeshDistance(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement object to define properties for a refinement of a FEM mesh
  - def makeMeshManipulate(doc, base_mesh, name='Manipulate')
    doc: makeMeshManipulate(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement object to manipulate the output of refinements
  - def makeMeshAdvanced(doc, base_mesh, name='Advanced')
    doc: makeMeshAdvanced(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement with various advanced methods
  - def makeMeshShape(doc, base_mesh, name='Shape')
    doc: makeMeshShape(document, base_mesh [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement object to define properties for a refinement of a FEM mesh
  - def makeMeshTransfiniteCurve(doc, base_mesh, name='TransfiniteCurve')
    doc: makeMeshTransfiniteCurve(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement for transfinite curves
  - def makeMeshTransfiniteSurface(doc, base_mesh, name='TransfiniteSurface')
    doc: makeMeshTransfiniteSurface(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement for transfinite surfaces
  - def makeMeshTransfiniteVolume(doc, base_mesh, name='TransfiniteVolume')
    doc: makeMeshTransfiniteVolume(document, base_mesh, [name]): creates a FEM mesh refinement for transfinite volumes
  - def makeMeshResult(doc, name='MeshResult')
    doc: makeMeshResult(document, name): makes a Fem MeshResult object
  - def makeResultMechanical(doc, name='ResultMechanical')
    doc: makeResultMechanical(document, [name]): creates a mechanical result object to hold FEM results
  - def makePostVtkFilterClipRegion(doc, base_vtk_result, name='VtkFilterClipRegion')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterClipRegion(document, base_vtk_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing region clip filter object (vtk based)
  - def makePostVtkFilterClipScalar(doc, base_vtk_result, name='VtkFilterClipScalar')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterClipScalar(document, base_vtk_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing scalar clip filter object (vtk based)
  - def makePostVtkFilterCutFunction(doc, base_vtk_result, name='VtkFilterCutFunction')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterCutFunction(document, base_vtk_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing cut function filter object (vtk based)
  - def makePostVtkFilterWarp(doc, base_vtk_result, name='VtkFilterWarp')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterWarp(document, base_vtk_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing warp filter object (vtk based)
  - def makePostVtkFilterContours(doc, base_vtk_result, name='VtkFilterContours')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterContours(document, base_vtk_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing contours filter object (vtk based)
  - def makePostFilterGlyph(doc, base_vtk_result, name='Glyph')
    doc: makePostVtkFilterGlyph(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing filter that visualizes vector fields with glyphs
  - def makePostVtkResult(doc, result_data, name='VtkResult')
    doc: makePostVtkResult(document, base_result, [name]): creates a FEM post processing result data (vtk based) to hold FEM results Note: Result data get expanded, it can either be single result [result] or everything needed for a multistep result: [results_list, value_list, unit, description]
  - def makePostLineplot(doc, name='Lineplot')
    doc: makePostLineplot(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing line plot
  - def makePostLineplotFieldData(doc, name='FieldData2D')
    doc: makePostLineplotFieldData(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 2D Field data
  - def makePostLineplotIndexOverFrames(doc, name='IndexOverFrames2D')
    doc: makePostLineplotIndexOverFrames(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 2D index data
  - def makePostHistogram(doc, name='Histogram')
    doc: makePostHistogram(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing histogram plot
  - def makePostHistogramFieldData(doc, name='FieldData1D')
    doc: makePostHistogramFieldData(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 1D Field data
  - def makePostHistogramIndexOverFrames(doc, name='IndexOverFrames1D')
    doc: makePostHistogramIndexOverFrames(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 1D Field data
  - def makePostTable(doc, name='Table')
    doc: makePostTable(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing histogram plot
  - def makePostTableFieldData(doc, name='FieldData1D')
    doc: makePostTableFieldData(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 1D Field data
  - def makePostTableIndexOverFrames(doc, name='IndexOverFrames1D')
    doc: makePostTableIndexOverFrames(document, [name]): creates a FEM post processing data extractor for 1D Field data
  - def makeEquationDeformation(doc, base_solver=None, name='Deformation')
    doc: makeEquationDeformation(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM deformation (nonlinear elasticity) equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationElasticity(doc, base_solver=None, name='Elasticity')
    doc: makeEquationElasticity(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM elasticity equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationElectricforce(doc, base_solver=None, name='Electricforce')
    doc: makeEquationElectricforce(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM Electricforce equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationElectrostatic(doc, base_solver=None, name='Electrostatic')
    doc: makeEquationElectrostatic(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM electrostatic equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationFlow(doc, base_solver=None, name='Flow')
    doc: makeEquationFlow(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM flow equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationFlux(doc, base_solver=None, name='Flux')
    doc: makeEquationFlux(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM flux equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationHeat(doc, base_solver=None, name='Heat')
    doc: makeEquationHeat(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM heat equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationMagnetodynamic(doc, base_solver=None, name='Magnetodynamic')
    doc: makeEquationMagnetodynamic(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM magnetodynamic equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationMagnetodynamic2D(doc, base_solver=None, name='Magnetodynamic2D')
    doc: makeEquationMagnetodynamic2D(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM magnetodynamic2D equation for a solver
  - def makeEquationStaticCurrent(doc, base_solver=None, name='StaticCurrent')
    doc: makeEquationStaticCurrent(document, [base_solver], [name]): creates a FEM static current equation for a solver
  - def makeSolverCalculiXCcxTools(doc, name='SolverCcxTools')
    doc: makeSolverCalculiXCcxTools(document, [name]): makes a Calculix solver object for the ccx tools module
  - def makeSolverCalculiX(doc, name='SolverCalculiX')
    doc: makeSolverCalculiX(document, [name]): makes a Calculix solver object
  - def makeSolverElmer(doc, name='SolverElmer')
    doc: makeSolverElmer(document, [name]): makes a Elmer solver object
  - def makeSolverMystran(doc, name='SolverMystran')
    doc: makeSolverMystran(document, [name]): makes a Mystran solver object
  - def makeSolverZ88(doc, name='SolverZ88')
    doc: makeSolverZ88(document, [name]): makes a Z88 solver object
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femguiutils/disambiguate_solid_selection.py
functions:
  - def calculate_unhighlighted_color(parent_part: 'PartFeature') -> highlight_color_t
    doc: Cheap and cheerful function to dim object so that subsequent highlighting is apparent
  - def parent_face_index(parent_part: 'PartFeature', face: 'Face') -> int
    doc: Return the index for the provided face in the parent_shape's list of faces.
  - def solid_parent_faces_indices(parent_part: 'PartFeature', solid_index: int) -> List[int]
    doc: Return the parent's face indices for the faces bounding the given solid
  - def build_highlight_map(parent_part: 'PartFeature', solid_indices: List[int]) -> highlight_map_t
    doc: Build a mapping from solid name to face colors for highlighting a given selected solid. Indexing with None returns the unhighlighted coloring (see calculate_unhighlighted_color).
  - def solid_name_from_index(solid_index: int) -> str
  - def disambiguate_solid_selection(parent_part: 'PartFeature', solid_indices: List[int]) -> Optional[str]
    doc: @param solid_indices the indices which may be used to get a reference to the selected solid as parent_part.Solids[index]. @return The name of the selected solid or None if the user cancels the selection
classes:
  class HighlightContext
```

```text
functions:
  - def solids_with_edge(parent_part: 'PartFeature', edge: 'Edge') -> List[int]

  class FemSelectionObserver
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/feminout/importToolsFem.py
functions:
  - def get_FemMeshObjectMeshGroups(fem_mesh_obj)
    doc: Get mesh groups from mesh.
  - def get_FemMeshObjectOrder(fem_mesh_obj)
    doc: Gets element order. Element order counting based on number of nodes on edges. Edge with 2 nodes -> linear elements, Edge with 3 nodes -> quadratic elements, and so on. No edges in mesh -> not determined. (Is this possible? Seems to be a very degenerate case.) If there are edges with different number of nodes appearing, return list of orders.
  - def get_FemMeshObjectDimension(fem_mesh_obj)
    doc: Count all entities in an abstract sense, to distinguish which dimension the mesh is (i.e. linemesh, facemesh, volumemesh)
  - def get_FemMeshObjectElementTypes(fem_mesh_obj, remove_zero_element_entries=True)
    doc: Spit out all elements in the mesh with their appropriate dimension.
  - def get_MaxDimElementFromList(elem_list)
    doc: Gets element with the maximal dimension in the mesh to determine cells.
  - def make_femmesh(mesh_data)
    doc: makes an FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM Mesh object from FEM Mesh data
  - def make_dict_from_femmesh(femmesh)
    doc: Converts FemMesh into dictionary structure which can immediately used from importToolsFem.make_femmesh(mesh_data) to create a valid FEM mesh.
  - def fill_femresult_mechanical(res_obj, result_set)
    doc: fills a FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object with result data
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/feminout/importYamlJsonMesh.py
functions:
  - def open(filename)
    doc: called when freecad opens a file a FEM mesh object is created in a new document
  - def insert(filename, docname)
    doc: called when freecad wants to import a file" a FEM mesh object is created in a existing document
  - def export(objectslist, fileString)
    doc: called when freecad exports a file
  - def import_yaml_json_mesh(fileString)
    doc: read a FemMesh from a yaml/json mesh file insert a FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM Mesh object in the ActiveDocument return the FEM mesh document object
  - def read(fileString)
    doc: read a FemMesh from a yaml/json mesh file and return the FemMesh
  - def convert_raw_data_to_mesh_data(raw_mesh_data)
    doc: Converts raw dictionary data from JSON or YAML file to proper dict for importToolsFem.make_femmesh(mesh_data). This is necessary since JSON and YAML save dict keys as strings while make_femmesh expects integers.
  - def write(fileString, fem_mesh)
    doc: directly write a FemMesh to a yaml/json mesh file fem_mesh: a FemMesh
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/feminout/importZ88Mesh.py
functions:
  - def open(filename)
    doc: called when freecad opens a file a FEM mesh object is created in a new document
```

```text
    doc: called when freecad wants to import a file
  - def import_z88_disp(filename, analysis=None, result_name_prefix=None)
    doc: insert a FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object in the ActiveDocument pure usage: import feminout.importZ88O2Results as importZ88O2Results disp_file = "/pathtofile/z88o2.txt" importZ88O2Results.import_z88_disp(disp_file) the z888i1.txt FEMMesh file needs to be in the same directory as z88o2.txt # ahh, make a new document first ;-)
  - def read_z88_disp(z88_disp_input)
    doc: read a z88 disp file and extract the nodes and elements z88 Displacement output file is z88o2.txt works with Z88OS14
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femresult/resulttools.py
functions:
  - def purge_result_objects(analysis)
    doc: Removes all result objects and result meshes from an analysis group. Parameters ---------- analysis : Fem::FemAnalysis analysis group as a container for all objects needed for the analysis
  - def purge_postprocessing_objects(analysis)
    doc: Removes all postprocessing objects and visualizations form the analysis Parameters ---------- analysis : Fem::FemAnalysis analysis group as a container for all objects needed for the analysis
  - def purge_results(analysis)
    doc: Removes all result and postprocessing objects and result meshes from an analysis group. Parameters ---------- analysis : Fem::FemAnalysis analysis group as a container for all objects needed for the analysis
  - def reset_mesh_deformation(resultobj)
    doc: Resets result mesh deformation. Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object
  - def reset_mesh_color(resultobj)
    doc: Resets result mesh color Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object
  - def show_displacement(resultobj, displacement_factor=0.0)
  - def show_result(resultobj, result_type='Sabs', limit=None)
    doc: Sets mesh color using selected type of results. Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object result_type : str, optional default is Sabs FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object - U1, U2, U3 - deformation - Uabs - absolute deformation - Sabs - Von Mises stress limit : float limit cutoff value. All values over the limit are treated as equal to the limit. Useful for filtering out hotspots.
  - def show_color_by_scalar_with_cutoff(resultobj, values, limit=None)
    doc: Sets mesh color using list of values. Internally used by show_result function. Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object values : list of floats the values to be colored and cutoff has to be the same length as resultobj.NodeNumbers resultobj.NodeNumbers has to be present in the resultobj limit : float limit cutoff value. All values over the limit are treated as equal to the limit. Useful for filtering out hotspots.
  - def get_stats(res_obj, result_type)
    doc: Returns minimum and maximum value for provided result type Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object result_type : str type of FEM result allowed are: see dict keys in def get_all_stats() None - always return (0.0, 0.0)
  - def get_all_stats(res_obj)
    doc: Returns all stats for provided result type. - U1, U2, U3 - deformation - Uabs - absolute deformation - Sabs - Von Mises stress - MaxPrin - Principal stress 1 - MidPrin - Principal stress 2 - MinPrin - Principal stress 3 - MaxShear - maximum shear stress - Peeq - peeq strain - Temp - Temperature - MFlow - MassFlowRate - NPress - NetworkPressure for more information on result types and names see in code file src/Mod/Fem/App/FemVTKTools.cpp the methods _getFreeCAD-compatible runtimeMechResultVector
  - def fill_femresult_stats(res_obj)
    doc: Fills a FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object with stats data. Parameters ---------- resultobj : Fem::ResultMechanical FreeCAD-compatible runtime FEM mechanical result object
  - def add_disp_apps(res_obj)
  - def add_von_mises(res_obj)
  - def add_principal_stress_std(res_obj)
  - def calculate_csr(ps1, ps2, ps3, alpha, beta, res_obj)
    doc: Calculate critical strain ratio. Forum Discussion: https://forum.freecad.org/viewtopic.php?f=18&t=35893#p303392 Background: https://www.vtt.fi/inf/julkaisut/muut/2017/VTT-R-01177-17.pdf critical strain ratio = peeq / critical_strain (>1.0 indicates ductile rupture) peeq = equivalent plastic strain critical strain = alpha * np.exp(-beta * T) alpha and beta are material parameters, where alpha can be related to unixial test data (user input) and beta is normally kept fixed at 1.5, unless available
  - def get_concrete_nodes(res_obj)
    doc: Determine concrete / non-concrete nodes.
  - def add_principal_stress_reinforced(res_obj)
  - def compact_result(res_obj)
    doc: compacts result.Mesh and appropriate result.NodeNumbers
  - def calculate_von_mises(stress_tensor)
    doc: Calculate Von mises stress. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Von_Mises_yield_criterion Simplification: https://forum.freecad.org/viewtopic.php?f=18&t=33974&p=296542#p296542 stress_tensor ... (Sxx, Syy, Szz, Sxy, Sxz, Syz)
  - def calculate_principal_stress_std(stress_tensor)
  - def calculate_principal_stress_reinforced(stress_tensor)
    doc: Calculate principal stress vectors and values. For total stresses use: stress_tensor[0], stress_tensor[1], stress_tensor[2] on the diagonal of the stress tensor Difference with the original method: https://forum.freecad.org/viewtopic.php?f=18&t=33106&start=90#p296539
  - def calculate_rho(stress_tensor, fy)
    doc: Calculation of Reinforcement Ratios and Concrete Stresses (in accordance with http://heronjournal.nl/53-4/3.pdf) Parameters ---------- - fy: factored yield strength of reinforcement bars See post: https://forum.freecad.org/viewtopic.php?f=18&t=28821
  - def calculate_mohr_coulomb(prin1, prin3, phi, fck)
    doc: Calculation of Mohr Coulomb yield criterion to judge concrete crushing and shear failure. Parameters ---------- - phi: angle of internal friction - fck: factored compressive strength of the matrix material (usually concrete)
  - def calculate_disp_abs(displacements)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femsolver/fenics/fenics_tools.py
classes:
  class XDMFReader
    doc: Reads XDMF file and provides unified interface for returning cell functions or facet functions.
    methods:
      - def resetMesh(self)
        doc: Resets mesh instance to None.
      - def readMesh(self)
        doc: If mesh instance is None, read mesh instance from file denoted by filename property.
      - def readCellExpression(self, group_value_dict, value_type='scalar', overlap=lambda x: x[0], *args, **kwargs)
        doc: Reads cell expression and returns it.
      - def readFacetFunction(self, group_value_dict, *args, **kwargs)
        doc: Reads facet function and returns it.
  class CellExpressionFromXDMF
    doc: Creates cell function expression from XDMF file.
    methods:
      - def init(self)
      - def assign_values(self, values, to_assign)
      - def eval_cell_backend(self, values, x, cell)
  class ScalarCellExpressionFromXDMF(fenics.Expression, CellExpressionFromXDMF)
    methods:
      - def eval_cell(self, values, x, cell)
      - def value_shape(self)
  class Vector3DCellExpressionFromXDMF(fenics.Expression, CellExpressionFromXDMF)
    methods:
      - def eval_cell(self, values, x, cell)
      - def value_shape(self)
  class Vector2DCellExpressionFromXDMF(fenics.Expression, CellExpressionFromXDMF)
    methods:
      - def eval_cell(self, values, x, cell)
      - def value_shape(self)
  class FacetFunctionFromXDMF
    doc: Creates facet function from XDMF file.
    methods:
      - def init(self)
      - def getDirichletBCs(self, vectorspace, *args, **kwargs)
```

```text
functions:
```

```text
classes:
  class Proxy(solverbase.Proxy)
    doc: The Fem::FemSolver's Proxy python type, add solver specific properties
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femsolver/settings.py
functions:
  - def get_binary(name, silent=False)
    doc: Find binary of solver *name* honoring user settings. Return the specific path set by the user in FreeCAD-compatible runtimes settings/parameter system if set or the default binary name if no specific path is set. If no path was found because the solver *name* is not supported ``None`` is returned. This method does not check whether the binary actually exists and is callable. That check is done in DlgSettingsFem_Solver_Imp.cpp :param name: solver id as a ``str`` (see :mod:`femsolver.settings`) :p
  - def get_cores(name)
    doc: Read number of CPU cores for solver *name* honoring user settings. Returns number of CPU cores to be used for the solver run :param name: solver id as a ``str`` (see :mod:`femsolver.settings`)
  - def get_write_comments(name)
    doc: Check whether "write_comments" is set for solver. Returns ``True`` if the "write_comments" setting/parameter is set for the solver with the id *name*. Returns ``False`` otherwise. If the solver is not supported ``None`` is returned. :param name: solver id as a ``str`` (see :mod:`femsolver.settings`)
  - def get_custom_dir()
    doc: Get value for :term:`General/CustomDirectoryPath` parameter.
  - def get_dir_setting()
    doc: Return directory setting set by the user. Return one of the three possible values of the :class:`DirSetting` enum depending on the setting set in FreeCAD-compatible runtime parameter system. Result dependes on the values of :term:`General/UseTempDirectory`, :term:`General/UseBesideDirectory` and :term:`General/UseCustomDirectory`.
  - def get_default_solver()
    doc: Return default solver name.
classes:
  class DirSetting
    doc: Enum of possible directory setting values. Strings used to indicate the solver directory setting set in FreeCAD-compatible runtimes setting system. Returned by :func:`get_dir_setting` for that purpose. There are three different possible values: :cvar TEMPORARY: Let FreeCAD-compatible runtime manage (create, delete) the working directories for all solver. Use temporary directories. :cvar BESIDE: Create a directory in the same folder in which the FCStd file of the document is located. Use Subfolde
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femtest/app/support_utils.py
functions:
  - def get_fem_test_home_dir()
  - def get_fem_test_tmp_dir(dirname=None)
  - def get_unit_test_tmp_dir(temp_dir, unittestdir)
  - def fcc_print(message)
  - def get_namefromdef(strdel='', stradd='')
  - def get_defmake_count(fem_vtk_post=True)
    doc: count the def make in module ObjectsFem could also be done in bash with grep -c "def make" src/Mod/Fem/ObjectsFem.py
  - def get_fem_test_defs()
  - def try_converting_to_float(line: str) -> Union[None, List[float]]
    doc: Does its best to split a line and convert its elements to float Has 3 strategies of splitting: * by comma - mainly in CalculiX .inp files * by space - other solvers * by space and ignoring the 1st word - other solvers If there was only 1 word, the line will fail, no compromises Single characters always pass :param line: line to split and convert to floats :return: None if conversion failed, else list of floats
  - def are_floats_equal(orig_floats: Union[None, List[float]], floats_to_compare: Union[None, List[float]]) -> bool
    doc: Check if floats in lists are equal with some tolerance :param orig_floats: list of floats - left operands of comparison :param floats_to_compare: list of floats - right operands of comparison :return: True if both lists are equal with some tolerance element-wise, else False
  - def parse_diff(diff_lines: Iterator[str]) -> List[str]
    doc: Parses lines from `united_diff` Tries to split a line and convert its contents to float, to compare float numbers with some tolerance Recognizes blocks of changes, that start with `@@` and end either at EOF or at the beginning of another block. Only bad lines are added to block. All lines, which didn't pass the element-wise check, are bad lines. :param diff_lines: lines produced by `united_diff` :return: list of bad lines with respect to their block of change
  - def compare_inp_files(file_name1, file_name2)
  - def compare_files(file_name1, file_name2)
  - def compare_stats(fea, stat_file, res_obj_name, loc_stat_types=None)
  - def force_unix_line_ends(line_list)
  - def collect_python_modules(femsubdir=None)
  - def all_test_files()
  - def cube_frequency()
  - def cube_static()
  - def Flow1D_thermomech()
  - def multimat()
  - def spine_thermomech()
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femtools/ccxtools.py
classes:
  class FemToolsCcx(QtCore.QRunnable, QtCore.QObject)
    doc: Attributes ---------- analysis : Fem::FemAnalysis FEM group analysis object has to be present, will be set in __init__ solver : Fem::FemSolverObjectPython FEM solver object has to be present, will be set in __init__ base_name : str name of .inp/.frd file (without extension) It is used to construct .inp file path that is passed to CalculiX ccx ccx_binary : str working_dir : str results_present : bool indicating if there are calculation results ready for us members : class femtools/membertools/Ana
    methods:
      - def purge_results(self)
        doc: Remove all result objects and result meshes from an analysis group
      - def reset_mesh_purge_results_checked(self)
        doc: Reset mesh color, deformation and removes all result objects if preferences to keep them is not set.
      - def reset_all(self)
        doc: Reset mesh color, deformation and removes all result objects
      - def find_analysis(self)
      - def find_solver_analysis(self)
        doc: get the analysis group the solver belongs to
      - def find_solver(self)
      - def update_objects(self)
      - def check_prerequisites(self)
      - def set_base_name(self, base_name=None)
        doc: Set base_name Parameters ---------- base_name : str, optional base_name base name of .inp/.frd file (without extension). It is used to construct .inp file path that is passed to CalculiX ccx
      - def set_inp_file_name(self, inp_file_name=None)
        doc: Set inp file name. Normally inp file name is set by write_inp_file. That name is also used to determine location and name of frd result file. Parameters ---------- inp_file_name : str, optional input file name path
      - def setup_working_dir(self, param_working_dir=None, create=False)
        doc: Set working dir for solver execution. Parameters ---------- param_working_dir : str, optional directory to be used for writing create : bool, optional Should the working directory be created if it does not exist
      - def write_inp_file(self)
      - def setup_ccx(self, ccx_binary=None, ccx_binary_sig='CalculiX')
        doc: Set Calculix binary path and validate its execution. Parameters ---------- ccx_binary : str, optional It defaults to `None`. The path to the `ccx` binary. If it is `None`, the path is guessed. ccx_binary_sig : str, optional Defaults to 'CalculiX'. Expected output from `ccx` when run empty.
      - def start_ccx(self)
      - def get_ccx_version(self)
      - def ccx_run(self)
      - def run(self)
      - def has_no_material_assigned(self)
      - def has_nonpositive_jacobians(self)
      - def load_results(self)
      - def load_results_ccxfrd(self)
        doc: Load results of ccx calculations from .frd file.
      - def load_results_ccxdat(self)
        doc: Load results of ccx calculations from .dat file.
  class CcxTools(FemToolsCcx)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femtools/femutils.py
functions:
  - def createObject(doc, name, proxy, viewProxy=None)
    doc: Add python object to document using python type string. Add a document object suitable for the *proxy* and the *viewProxy* to *doc* and attach it to the *proxy* and the *viewProxy*. This function can only be used with python proxies that specify their C++ type via the BaseType class member (e.g. Cube.BaseType). If there already exists a object with *name* a suitable unique name is generated. To auto generate a name pass ``""``. :param doc: document object to which the object is added :param name
  - def type_of_obj(obj)
    doc: Return type of *obj* honoring the special typesystem of Fem. Python objects of the Fem workbench define their type via a class member ``<Class>.Type``. Return this type if the property exists. If not return the conventional ``TypeId`` value. :para obj: a document object
  - def is_of_type(obj, ty)
    doc: Compare type of *obj* with *ty* honoring Fems typesystem. See :py:func:`type_of_obj` for more info about the special typesystem of the Fem module. :returns: ``True`` if *obj* is of type *ty*, ``False`` otherwise. Type must match exactly: Derived objects are not considered to be of type of one of their super classes.
  - def is_derived_from(obj, t)
    doc: Check if *obj* is derived from *t* honoring Fems typesystem. Essentially just call ``obj.isDerivedFrom(t)`` and return it's value. For objects using Fems typesystem (see :py:func:`type_of_obj`) return always True if the Fem type is equal to *t*. :note: Inheritance of Fem types is not checked. If *obj* uses Fems typesystem the type is just checked for equality. If the type doesn't match ``obj.isDerivedFrom`` is called as usual. See https://forum.freecad.org/viewtopic.php?f=10&t=32625
  - def get_pref_working_dir(solver_obj)
    doc: Return working directory for solver honoring user settings. :throws femtools.errors.MustSaveError: If user setting is set to BESIDE and the document isn't saved. :note: Not working correctly for most cases because this circumvents directory caching of the solver framework. For solver use getMachine from run.py instead.
  - def get_temp_dir(obj=None)
  - def get_beside_dir(obj)
  - def get_custom_dir(obj)
  - def get_beside_base(obj)
  - def get_custom_base(solver)
  - def check_working_dir(wdir)
  - def make_dir(specific_path)
  - def getBoundBoxOfAllDocumentShapes(doc)
    doc: Calculate bounding box containing all objects inside *doc*. :returns: A bounding box containing all objects that have a *Shape* attribute (all Part and PartDesign objects). If the document contains no such objects or no objects at all return ``None``.
  - def getSelectedFace(selectionex)
    doc: Return selected face if exactly one face is selected. :returns: The selected face as a ``Part::TopoShape`` if exactly one face is selected. Otherwise return ``None``. :param selectionex: A list of selection object like the one Gui.Selection.getSelectionEx() returns.
  - def get_refshape_type(fem_doc_object)
    doc: Return shape type the constraints references. Determine single shape type of references of *fem_doc_object* which must be a constraint (=have a *References* property). All references must be of the same type which is than returned as a string. A type can be "Vertex", "Edge", "Face" or "Solid". :param fem_doc_object: A constraint object with a *References* property. :returns: A string representing the shape type ("Vertex", "Edge", "Face" or "Solid"). If *fem_doc_object* isn't a constraint ``""`` 
  - def pydecode(bytestring)
    doc: Return *bytestring* as a unicode string
  - def startProgramInfo(code)
    doc: starts a program under Windows minimized, hidden or normal
  - def expandParentObject()
    doc: expands parent and selected obj in tree view
  - def getOutputWinColor(type)
    doc: type: 'Error', 'Warning', 'Logging', 'Text'
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femtools/membertools.py
functions:
  - def get_member(analysis, t)
    doc: Return list of all members of *analysis* of type *t*. Search *analysis* for members of type *t*. This method checks the custom python typesystem (BaseType class property) used by the Fem module if possible. If the object does not use the python typesystem the usual isDerivedFrom from the C++ dynamic type system is used. :param analysis: only objects part of this analysis are considered :param t: only objects of this type are returned :note: Inheritance of Fem types is not checked. If *obj* uses 
  - def get_single_member(analysis, t)
    doc: Return one object of type *t* and part of *analysis*. Search *analysis* for members of type *t* and return the first one that's found. This method checks the custom python typesystem (BaseType class property) used by the Fem module if possible. If the object doesn't use the python typesystem the usual isDerivedFrom from the C++ dynamic type system is used. :param analysis: only objects part of this analysis are considered :param t: only a object of this type is returned :note: Inheritance of Fem
  - def get_several_member(analysis, t)
    doc: Get members and pack them for Calculix/Z88. Collect members by calling :py:func:`get_member` and pack them into a data structure that can be consumed by calculix and Z88 solver modules. :param analysis: see :py:func:`get_member` :param t: see :py:func:`get_member` :returns: A list containing one dict per member. Each dict has two entries: ``"Object"`` and ``"RefShapeType"``. ``dict["Object"]`` contains the member document object. ``dict["RefShapeType"]`` contains the shape type of the *Reference
  - def get_mesh_to_solve(analysis)
    doc: Find one and only mesh object of *analysis*. :returns: A tuple ``(object, message)``. If and only if the analysis contains exactly one mesh object the first value of the tuple is the mesh document object. Otherwise the first value is ``None`` and the second value is a error message indicating what went wrong.
classes:
  class AnalysisMember
    methods:
      - def get_several_member(self, t)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Fem/femtools/tokrules.py
functions:
  - def t_FLOAT(t)
    doc: \d+\.(\d+)?([eE][-+]?\d+)?
  - def t_INT(t)
    doc: \d+
  - def t_COMMENT(t)
    doc: \#.*
  - def t_newline(t)
    doc: \n+
  - def t_error(t)
  - def p_statement_assign(p)
    doc: statement : NAME EQUALS expression
  - def p_statement_expr(p)
    doc: statement : expression
  - def p_expression_binop(p)
    doc: expression : expression PLUS expression | expression MINUS expression | expression TIMES expression | expression DIVIDE expression | expression POWER expression
  - def p_expression_uminus(p)
    doc: expression : MINUS expression %prec UMINUS
  - def p_expression_group(p)
    doc: expression : LPAREN expression RPAREN
  - def p_expression_float(p)
    doc: expression : FLOAT
  - def p_expression_int(p)
    doc: expression : INT
  - def p_expression_name(p)
    doc: expression : NAME
  - def p_error(p)
```

```text
classes:
```
```text
  class EditViewWidget(QtGui.QWidget)
    methods:
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Help/Help.py
functions:
  - def show(page, view=None, conv=None)
    doc: show(page,view=None, conv=None): Opens a help viewer and shows the given help page. The given help page can be a URL pointing to a markdown or HTML file, a name page / command name, or a file path pointing to a markdown or HTML file. If view is given (an instance of openBrowserHTML.HelpPage or any other object with a 'setHtml()' method), the page will be rendered there, otherwise a new tab/widget will be created according to preferences settings. If conv is given (markdown, pandoc, github, built
  - def underscore_page(page)
    doc: change spaces by underscores in the given page name
  - def get_uri(location)
    doc: returns a valid URI from a disk or network location
  - def location_url(url_localized: str, url_english: str) -> tuple
    doc: Returns localized documentation url and page name, if they exist, otherwise defaults to english version. Page name is gotten from: a) Name/* metadata tag on raw markdown files from github, Wiki translators should make sure to add it. b) <title> HTML tag
  - def get_location(page) -> tuple
    doc: retrieves the location (online or offline) of a given page. Returns the location and the page name
  - def show_browser(url)
    doc: opens the desktop browser with the given URL
  - def show_dialog(html, baseurl, title, view=None)
    doc: opens a dock dialog with the given html
  - def show_tab(html, baseurl, title, view=None)
    doc: opens a MDI tab with the given html
  - def get_qtwebwidgets()
    doc: verifies if qtwebengine is available
  - def get_contents(location)
    doc: retrieves text contents of a given page
  - def convert(content, force=None)
    doc: converts the given markdown code to html. Force can be None (automatic) or markdown, pandoc, github or raw/builtin
  - def add_preferences_page()
    doc: adds the Help preferences page to the UI
  - def add_language_path()
    doc: registers the Help translations to FreeCAD-compatible runtime
  - def openBrowserHTML(html, baseurl, title, icon, dialog=False)
    doc: creates a browser view and adds it as a FreeCAD-compatible runtime MDI tab or dockable dialog
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Material/App/MaterialAPI/MaterialManagerExternal.py
classes:
  class MaterialLibraryType
  class MaterialLibraryObjectType
  class ModelObjectType
  class MaterialObjectType
  class MaterialManagerExternal(ABC)
    doc: Abstract base class for all external material managers Any external interface should be derivedfrom this base class.
    methods:
      - def APIVersion(cls) -> tuple
        doc: Returns a tuple of 3 integers describing the API version The version returned should be the latest supported version. This method allows the interface to use older modules.
      - def libraries(self) -> list[MaterialLibraryType]
        doc: Returns a list of libraries managed by this interface The list contains a series of tuples describing all libraries managed by this module. Each tuple contains the library name, icon, a boolean to indicate if it is a read only library.
      - def modelLibraries(self) -> list[MaterialLibraryType]
        doc: Returns a list of libraries managed by this interface The list contains a series of tuples describing all libraries managed by this module. Each tuple contains the library name, icon, and a boolean to indicate if it is a read only library. This differs from the libraries() function in that it only returns libraries containing model objects.
      - def materialLibraries(self) -> list[MaterialLibraryType]
        doc: Returns a list of libraries managed by this interface The list contains a series of tuples describing all libraries managed by this module. Each tuple contains the library name, icon, and a boolean to indicate if it is a read only library. This differs from the libraries() function in that it only returns libraries containing material objects.
      - def getLibrary(self, libraryName: str) -> MaterialLibraryType
        doc: Get the library Retrieve the library with the given name
      - def createLibrary(self, libraryName: str, icon: bytes, readOnly: bool) -> None
        doc: Create a new library Create a new library with the given name.
      - def renameLibrary(self, oldName: str, newName: str) -> None
        doc: Rename an existing library Change the name of an existing library
      - def changeIcon(self, libraryName: str, icon: bytes) -> None
        doc: Change the library icon Change the library icon
      - def removeLibrary(self, libraryName: str) -> None
        doc: Delete a library and its contents Deletes the library and any models or materials it contains
      - def libraryModels(self, libraryName: str) -> list[MaterialLibraryObjectType]
        doc: Returns a list of models managed by this library Each list entry is a tuple containing the UUID, path, and name of the model
      - def libraryMaterials(self, libraryName: str, materialFilter: Materials.MaterialFilter=None, options: Materials.MaterialFilterOptions=None) -> list[MaterialLibraryObjectType]
        doc: Returns a list of materials managed by this library Each list entry is a tuple containing the UUID, path, and name of the material
      - def libraryFolders(self, libraryName: str) -> list[str]
        doc: Returns a list of folders managed by this library This will return a list of all folders in the library including empty folders
      - def createFolder(self, libraryName: str, path: str) -> None
        doc: Create a new folder in the given library
      - def renameFolder(self, libraryName: str, oldPath: str, newPath: str) -> None
        doc: Rename the folder
      - def deleteRecursive(self, libraryName: str, path: str) -> None
        doc: Delete the folder and all of its contents
      - def getModel(self, uuid: str) -> ModelObjectType
        doc: Retrieve a model given its UUID
      - def addModel(self, libraryName: str, path: str, model: Materials.Model) -> None
        doc: Add a model to a library in the given folder. The folder path is relative to the library and will be created if it doesn't already exist. This will throw a DatabaseModelExistsError exception if the model already exists.
      - def migrateModel(self, libraryName: str, path: str, model: Materials.Model) -> None
        doc: Add the model to the library. If the model already exists, then no action is performed.
      - def updateModel(self, libraryName: str, path: str, model: Materials.Model) -> None
        doc: Update the given model
      - def setModelPath(self, libraryName: str, path: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Change the model path within the library
      - def renameModel(self, libraryName: str, name: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Change the model name
      - def moveModel(self, libraryName: str, path: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Move a model across libraries Move the model to the desired path in a different library. This should also remove the model from the old library if that library is managed by this interface
      - def removeModel(self, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Remove the model from the library
      - def getMaterial(self, uuid: str) -> MaterialObjectType
        doc: Retrieve a material given its UUID
      - def addMaterial(self, libraryName: str, path: str, material: Materials.Material) -> None
        doc: Add a material to a library in the given folder. The folder path is relative to the library and will be created if it doesn't already exist. This will throw a DatabaseMaterialExistsError exception if the model already exists.
      - def migrateMaterial(self, libraryName: str, path: str, material: Materials.Material) -> None
        doc: Add the material to the library in the given folder. The folder path is relative to the library and will be created if it doesn't already exist. If the material already exists, then no action is performed.
      - def updateMaterial(self, libraryName: str, path: str, material: Materials.Material) -> None
        doc: Update the given material
      - def setMaterialPath(self, libraryName: str, path: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Change the material path within the library
      - def renameMaterial(self, libraryName: str, name: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Change the material name
      - def moveMaterial(self, libraryName: str, path: str, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Move a material across libraries Move the material to the desired path in a different library. This should also remove the material from the old library if that library is managed by this interface
      - def removeMaterial(self, uuid: str) -> None
        doc: Remove the material from the library
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Material/MaterialEditor.py
functions:
  - def matProperWidget(parent=None, matproperty=None, Type='String', Value=None, minimum=None, maximum=None, stepsize=None, precision=12)
    doc: customs widgets for the material stuff.
  - def string2tuple(string)
    doc: provisionally
  - def translate(context, text)
    doc: translates text
  - def openEditor(obj=None, prop=None)
    doc: openEditor([obj,prop]): opens the editor, optionally with an object name and material property name to edit
  - def editMaterial(material=None, card_path=None, category='Solid')
    doc: editMaterial(material): opens the editor to edit the contents of the given material dictionary. Returns the modified material dictionary.
classes:
  class MaterialEditor
    methods:
      - def implementModel(self)
        doc: implements the model with the material attribute structure.
      - def updateMatParamsInTree(self, data)
        doc: updates the contents of the editor with the given dictionary the material property keys where added to the editor already unknown material property keys will be added to the user defined group
      - def chooseMaterial(self, index)
      - def verifyMaterial(self)
      - def updateCardsInCombo(self)
        doc: updates the contents of the materials combo with existing material cards
      - def openProductURL(self)
        doc: opens the contents of the ProductURL field in an external browser.
      - def modelChange(self, item)
        doc: Called when an item in the tree is modified. This will set edited to True, but this will be reset in the event of mass updates, such as loading a card
      - def verify(self, button)
        doc: Verify that the user wants to save any changed data before exiting
      - def accept(self)
      - def reject(self)
      - def storeSize(self)
        doc: stores the widget size
      - def expandKey(self, key)
        doc: adds spaces before caps in a KeyName
      - def collapseKey(self, key)
        doc: removes the spaces in a Key Name
      - def addCustomProperty(self, key=None, value=None)
        doc: Adds a custom property to the editor, optionally with a value.
      - def deleteCustomProperty(self, key=None)
        doc: Deletes a custom property from the editor, or deletes the value of an internal property.
      - def onClickTree(self, index)
        doc: Checks if the current item is a custom or an internal property, and enable the delete property or delete value button.
      - def getDict(self)
        doc: returns a dictionary from the contents of the editor.
      - def outputDict(self, d)
      - def updatePreviews(self, mat=None)
        doc: updates the preview images from the content of the editor
      - def getColorHash(self, col, val=255)
        doc: returns a '#000000' string from a '(0.1,0.2,0.3)' string
      - def openfile(self)
      - def savefile(self)
        doc: Saves a FCMat file.
      - def show(self)
      - def exec_(self)
  class MaterialsDelegate(QtGui.QStyledItemDelegate)
    doc: provides display and editing facilities for data items from a model.
    methods:
      - def createEditor(self, parent, option, index)
        doc: returns the widget used to change data from the model.
      - def setEditorData(self, editor, index)
        doc: provides the widget with data to manipulate.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Material/materialtests/TestMaterialDocument.py
classes:
  class DocumentTestCases(unittest.TestCase)

    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def testApplyDiffuseColorCheckShapeAppearance(self)
        doc: Test that applying a DiffuseColor with transparency results in a correct ShapeAppearance
      - def testApplyShapeAppearanceCheckDiffuseColor(self)
        doc: Test that applying a ShapeAppearance with transparency results in a correct DiffuseColor
      - def testApplyNoAppearanceThenAppearanceMaterial(self)
        doc: Applying a material with no appearance then one with appearance must update ShapeAppearance. Regression test for https://github.com/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/FreeCAD-compatible runtime/issues/25793 - applying Air (no appearance properties) followed by Wood-Generic must still apply Wood's colors.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Measure/UtilsMeasure.py
classes:
  class MeasureBasePython(ABC)
    methods:
      - def isValidSelection(cls, selection)
        doc: Returns True if the given selection is valid for this measurement
      - def isPrioritySelection(cls, selection)
        doc: Returns True if creation of this measurement should be prioritized over other measurements for the given selection
      - def getInputProps(cls) -> Tuple[str]
        doc: Returns all properties that the measurement's result depends on
      - def getSubject(self, obj) -> Tuple
        doc: Returns all objects that are measured, this is used to autohide the measurement if the relevant elements are not visible
      - def parseSelection(self, obj, selection)
        doc: Sets the measurements properties from the given selection
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/OpenSCAD/OpenSCAD2Dgeom.py
functions:
  - def findConnectedEdges(edgelist, eps=1e-06, debug=False)
    doc: returns a list of list of connected edges
  - def endpointdistance(edges)
    doc: return the distance of vertices in path (list of edges) as maximum, minimum and distance between start and endpoint it expects the edges to be traversed forward starting from Vertex 0
  - def endpointdistancedebuglist(debuglist)
    doc: return the distance of vertices in path (list of edges) as maximum, minimum and distance between start and endpoint it expects a 'not reversed' flag for every edge
  - def edgestowires(edgelist, eps=0.001)
    doc: takes list of edges and returns a list of wires
  - def subtractfaces(faces)
    doc: searches for the biggest face and subtracts all smaller ones from the first. Only makes sense if all faces overlap.
  - def fusefaces(faces)
  - def subtractfaces2(faces)
    doc: Sort faces, check if they overlap. Subtract overlapping face and fuse nonoverlapping groups.
  - def edgestofaces(edges, algo=3, eps=0.001)
  - def superWireReverse(debuglist, closed=False)
    doc: superWireReverse(debuglist,[closed]): forces a wire between edges that don't necessarily have coincident endpoints. If closed=True, wire will always be closed. debuglist has a tuple for every edge.The first entry is the edge, the second is the flag 'does not need to be inverted'
  - def importDXFface(filename, layer=None, doc=None)
classes:
  class Overlappingfaces
    doc: combines overlapping faces together
    methods:
      - def dofacesoverlapboundbox(bigface, smallface)
      - def dofacesoverlapallverts(bigface, smallface)
      - def dofacesoverlapproximity(bigface, smallface)
      - def dofacesoverlapboolean(bigface, smallface)
      - def builddepdict(self)
      - def finddepth(dict1, faceidx, curdepth=0)
      - def findrootdepth(self)
      - def hasnoparent(self, faceindex)
      - def hasnoparentstatic(isinsidedict, faceindex)
      - def directchildren(isinsidedict, parent)
      - def printtree(isinsidedict, facenum)
      - def makefeatures(self, doc)
      - def makeshape(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/OpenSCAD/OpenSCADUtils.py
functions:
  - def getopenscadexe(osfilename=None)
  - def searchforopenscadexe()
    doc: Try to use Python's built-in executable-finder. If that fails, fall back to the old code.
  - def getopenscadversion(osfilename=None)
  - def newtempfilename()
  - def callopenscad(inputfilename, outputfilename=None, outputext='csg', keepname=False)
    doc: call the open scad binary returns the filename of the result (or None), please delete the file afterwards
  - def call_openscad_with_pipes(input_filename, output_filename, output_extension, keep_name)
    doc: Call OpenSCAD by sending input data to stdin, and read the output from stdout. Returns the tempfile the output is stored in on success, or None on failure. NOTE: This feature was added to OpenSCAD in 2021.01
  - def callopenscadstring(scadstr, outputext='csg')
    doc: create a tempfile and call the open scad binary returns the filename of the result (or None), please delete the file afterwards
  - def reverseimporttypes()
    doc: allows one to search for supported filetypes by module
  - def fcsubmatrix(m)
    doc: Extracts the 3x3 Submatrix from a freecad Matrix Object as a list of row vectors
  - def multiplymat(l, r)
    doc: multiply matrices given as lists of row vectors
  - def isorthogonal(submatrix, precision=4)
    doc: checking if 3x3 Matrix is orthogonal (M*Transp(M)==I)
  - def detsubmatrix(s)
    doc: get the determinant of a 3x3 Matrix given as list of row vectors
  - def isspecialorthogonalpython(submat, precision=4)
  - def isrotoinversionpython(submat, precision=4)
  - def isspecialorthogonal(mat, precision=4)
  - def decomposerotoinversion(m, precision=4)
  - def mirror2mat(nv, bv)
    doc: calculate the transformation matrix of a mirror feature
  - def vec2householder(nv)
    doc: calculated the householder matrix for a given normal vector
  - def mirrormesh(msh, vec)
    doc: mirrormesh(mesh,vector) where mesh is a mesh object and vector is a Base.Vector
  - def scalemesh(msh, vec)
    doc: scalemesh(mesh,vector) where mesh is a mesh object and vector is a Base.Vector
  - def resizemesh(msh, vec)
    doc: resizemesh(mesh,vector) where mesh is a mesh object and vector is a Base.Vector
  - def angneg(d)
  - def shorthexfloat(f)
  - def comparerotations(r1, r2)
    doc: compares two rotations a value of zero means that they are identical
  - def findbestmatchingrotation(r1)
  - def roundrotation(rot, maxangulardistance=1e-05)
    doc: guess the rotation axis and angle for a rotation recreated from rounded floating point values (from a quaterion or transformation matrix)
  - def callopenscadmeshstring(scadstr)
    doc: Call OpenSCAD and return the result as a Mesh
  - def meshopinline(opname, iterable1)
    doc: uses OpenSCAD to combine meshes takes the name of the CGAL operation and an iterable (tuple,list) of FreeCAD-compatible runtime Mesh objects includes all the mesh data in the SCAD file
  - def meshoptempfile(opname, iterable1)
    doc: uses OpenSCAD to combine meshes takes the name of the CGAL operation and an iterable (tuple,list) of FreeCAD-compatible runtime Mesh objects uses stl files to supply the mesh data
  - def meshoponobjs(opname, inobjs)
    doc: takes a string (operation name) and a list of Feature Objects returns a mesh and a list of objects that were used Part Objects will be meshed
  - def process2D_ObjectsViaOpenSCADShape(ObjList, Operation, doc)
  - def process2D_ObjectsViaOpenSCAD(ObjList, Operation, doc=None)
  - def process3D_ObjectsViaOpenSCADShape(ObjList, Operation, maxmeshpoints=None)
  - def process3D_ObjectsViaOpenSCAD(doc, ObjList, Operation)
  - def process_ObjectsViaOpenSCADShape(doc, children, name, maxmeshpoints=None)
  - def process_ObjectsViaOpenSCAD(doc, children, name)
  - def removesubtree(objs)
  - def applyPlacement(shape)
classes:
  class OpenSCADError(BaseError)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/OpenSCAD/importCSG.py
functions:
  - def shallHide(subject)
  - def setColorRecursively(obj, color, transp)
    doc: For some reason a part made by cutting or fusing other parts do not have a color unless its constituents are also colored. This code sets colors for those constituents unless already set elsewhere.
  - def fixVisibility()
  - def open(filename)
    doc: called when freecad opens a file.
  - def insert(filename, docname)
    doc: called when freecad imports a file
  - def processcsg(filename)
  - def p_block_list_(p)
    doc: block_list : statement | block_list statement | statementwithmod | block_list statementwithmod
  - def p_render_action(p)
    doc: render_action : render LPAREN keywordargument_list RPAREN OBRACE block_list EBRACE
  - def p_group_action1(p)
    doc: group_action1 : group LPAREN RPAREN OBRACE block_list EBRACE
  - def p_group_action2(p)
    doc: group_action2 : group LPAREN RPAREN SEMICOL
  - def p_boolean(p)
    doc: boolean : true | false
  - def p_stripped_string(p)
    doc: stripped_string : STRING
  - def p_statement(p)
    doc: statement : part | operation | multmatrix_action | group_action1 | group_action2 | color_action | render_action | not_supported
  - def p_anymodifier(p)
    doc: anymodifier : MODIFIERBACK | MODIFIERDEBUG | MODIFIERROOT | MODIFIERDISABLE
  - def p_statementwithmod(p)

  - def p_part(p)
    doc: part : sphere_action | cylinder_action | cube_action | circle_action | square_action | text_action | polygon_action_nopath | polygon_action_plus_path | polyhedron_action
  - def p_2d_point(p)
    doc: 2d_point : OSQUARE NUMBER COMMA NUMBER ESQUARE
  - def p_points_list_2d(p)
    doc: points_list_2d : 2d_point COMMA | points_list_2d 2d_point COMMA | points_list_2d 2d_point
  - def p_3d_point(p)
    doc: 3d_point : OSQUARE NUMBER COMMA NUMBER COMMA NUMBER ESQUARE
  - def p_points_list_3d(p)

  - def p_minkowski_action(p)

  - def p_resize_action(p)

  - def p_not_supported(p)

  - def p_size_vector(p)
    doc: size_vector : OSQUARE NUMBER COMMA NUMBER COMMA NUMBER ESQUARE
  - def p_keywordargument(p)

  - def p_keywordargument_list(p)

    doc: polyhedron_action : polyhedron LPAREN points EQ OSQUARE points_list_3d ESQUARE COMMA faces EQ OSQUARE path_set ESQUARE COMMA keywordargument_list RPAREN SEMICOL | polyhedron LPAREN points EQ OSQUARE points_list_3d ESQUARE COMMA triangles EQ OSQUARE points_list_3d ESQUARE COMMA keywordargument_list RPAREN SEMICOL
  - def p_projection_action(p)
    doc: projection_action : projection LPAREN keywordargument_list RPAREN OBRACE block_list EBRACE
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/BOPTools/GeneralFuseResult.py
classes:
  class GeneralFuseResult(FrozenClass)
    doc: class GeneralFuseResult: helper object for obtaining info from results of Part.Shape.generalFuse() method. Usage: def myCustomFusionRoutine(list_of_shapes): generalFuse_return = list_of_shapes[0].generalFuse(list_of_shapes[1:]) ao = GeneralFuseResult(list_of_shapes, generalFuse_return) ... (use attributes and methods of ao) ...
    methods:
      - def parse(self)
        doc: Parses the result of generalFuse recorded into self.gfa_return. Recovers missing information. Fills in data structures. It is called automatically by class constructor.
      - def parse_elements(self)
        doc: Fills element-to-source map. Potentially slow, so separated from general parse. Needed for splitAggregates; called automatically from splitAggregates.
      - def indexOfPiece(self, piece_shape)
        doc: indexOfPiece(piece_shape): returns index of piece_shape in list of pieces
      - def indexOfSource(self, source_shape)
        doc: indexOfSource(source_shape): returns index of source_shape in list of arguments
      - def piecesFromSource(self, source_shape)
        doc: piecesFromSource(source_shape): returns list of pieces (shapes) that came from given source shape. Note: aggregate pieces (e.g. wire, shell, compound) always have only one source - the shape they came directly from. Only after executing splitAggregates and explodeCompounds the source lists become completely populated.
      - def sourcesOfPiece(self, piece_shape)
        doc: sourcesOfPiece(piece_shape): returns list of source shapes given piece came from. Note: aggregate pieces (e.g. wire, shell, compound) always have only one source - the shape they came directly from. Only after executing splitAggregates and explodeCompounds the source lists become completely populated.
      - def largestOverlapCount(self)
        doc: largestOverlapCount(self): returns the largest overlap count. For example, if three spheres intersect and have some volume common to all three, largestOverlapCount returns 3. Note: the return value may be incorrect if some of the pieces are wires/shells/ compsolids/compounds. Please use explodeCompounds and splitAggregates before using this functio
      - def splitAggregates(self, pieces_to_split=None)
        doc: splitAggregates(pieces_to_split = None): splits aggregate shapes (wires, shells, compsolids) in pieces of GF result as cut by intersections. Also splits aggregates inside compounds. After running this, 'self' is replaced with new data, where the pieces_to_split are split. 'pieces_to_split': list of shapes (from self.pieces), that are to be processe
      - def makeSplitPieces(self, shape)
        doc: makeSplitPieces(self, shape): splits a shell, wire or compsolid into pieces where it intersects with other shapes. Returns list of split pieces. If no splits were done, returns list containing the original shape.
      - def explodeCompounds(self)
        doc: explodeCompounds(): if any of self.pieces is a compound, the compound is exploded. After running this, 'self' is filled with new data, where pieces are updated to contain the stuff extracted from compounds.
  class GeneralFuseReturnBuilder(FrozenClass)
    doc: GeneralFuseReturnBuilder: utility class used by splitAggregates to build fake return of generalFuse, for re-parsing.
    methods:
      - def addPiece(self, piece_shape, source_shape_index_list)
        doc: addPiece(piece_shape, source_shape_index_list): adds a piece. If the piece already exists, returns False, and only updates source<->piece map.
      - def replacePiece(self, piece_index, new_shape)
      - def getGFReturn(self)
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/BOPTools/JoinAPI.py
functions:
  - def shapeOfMaxSize(list_of_shapes)
    doc: shapeOfMaxSize(list_of_shapes): finds the shape that has the largest " "mass in the list and returns it. The shapes in the list must be of same dimension.
  - def connect(list_of_shapes, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: connect(list_of_shapes, tolerance = 0.0): connects solids (walled objects), shells and wires by throwing off small parts that result when splitting them at intersections. Compounds in list_of_shapes are automatically exploded, so self-intersecting compounds are valid for connect.
  - def connect_legacy(shape1, shape2, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: connect_legacy(shape1, shape2, tolerance = 0.0): alternative implementation of connect, without use of generalFuse. Slow. Provided for backwards compatibility, and for older OCC.
  - def embed_legacy(shape_base, shape_tool, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: embed_legacy(shape_base, shape_tool, tolerance = 0.0): alternative implementation of embed, without use of generalFuse. Slow. Provided for backwards compatibility, and for older OCC.
  - def cutout_legacy(shape_base, shape_tool, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: cutout_legacy(shape_base, shape_tool, tolerance = 0.0): alternative implementation of cutout, without use of generalFuse. Slow. Provided for backwards compatibility, and for older OCC.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/BOPTools/ShapeMerge.py
functions:
  - def findSharedElements(shape_list, element_extractor)
  - def isConnected(shape1, shape2, shape_dim=-1)
  - def splitIntoGroupsBySharing(list_of_shapes, element_extractor, split_connections=[])
    doc: splitIntoGroupsBySharing(list_of_shapes, element_type, split_connections = []): find, which shapes in list_of_shapes are connected into groups by sharing elements. element_extractor: function that takes shape as input, and returns list of shapes. split_connections: list of shapes to exclude when testing for connections. Use to split groups on purpose. return: list of lists of shapes. Top-level list is list of groups; bottom level lists enumerate shapes of a group.
  - def mergeSolids(list_of_solids_compsolids, flag_single=False, split_connections=[], bool_compsolid=False)
    doc: mergeSolids(list_of_solids, flag_single = False): merges touching solids that share faces. If flag_single is True, it is assumed that all solids touch, and output is a single solid. If flag_single is False, the output is a compound containing all resulting solids. Note. CompSolids are treated as lists of solids - i.e., merged into solids.
  - def mergeShells(list_of_faces_shells, flag_single=False, split_connections=[])
  - def mergeWires(list_of_edges_wires, flag_single=False, split_connections=[])
  - def mergeVertices(list_of_vertices, flag_single=False, split_connections=[])
  - def mergeShapes(list_of_shapes, flag_single=False, split_connections=[], bool_compsolid=False)
    doc: mergeShapes(list_of_shapes, flag_single = False, split_connections = [], bool_compsolid = False): merges list of edges/wires into wires, faces/shells into shells, solids/compsolids into solids or compsolids. list_of_shapes: shapes to merge. Shapes must share elements in order to be merged. flag_single: assume all shapes in list are connected. If False, return is a compound. If True, return is the single piece (e.g. a shell). split_connections: list of shapes that are excluded when searching for 
  - def removeDuplicates(list_of_shapes)
  - def dimensionOfShapes(list_of_shapes)
    doc: dimensionOfShapes(list_of_shapes): returns dimension (0D, 1D, 2D, or 3D) of shapes in the list. If dimension of shapes varies, TypeError is raised.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/BOPTools/SplitAPI.py
functions:
  - def booleanFragments(list_of_shapes, mode, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: booleanFragments(list_of_shapes, mode, tolerance = 0.0): functional part of BooleanFragments feature. It's just result of generalFuse plus a bit of post-processing. mode is a string. It can be "Standard", "Split" or "CompSolid". "Standard" - return generalFuse as is. "Split" - wires and shells will be split at intersections. "CompSolid" - solids will be extracted from result of generelFuse, and compsolids will be made from them; all other stuff is discarded.
  - def slice(base_shape, tool_shapes, mode, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: slice(base_shape, tool_shapes, mode, tolerance = 0.0): functional part of Slice feature. Splits base_shape into pieces based on intersections with tool_shapes. mode is a string. It can be "Standard", "Split" or "CompSolid". "Standard" - return like generalFuse: edges, faces and solids are split, but wires, shells, compsolids get extra segments but remain in one piece. "Split" - wires and shells will be split at intersections, too. "CompSolid" - slice a solid and glue it back together to make a c
  - def xor(list_of_shapes, tolerance=0.0)
    doc: xor(list_of_shapes, tolerance = 0.0): boolean XOR operation.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/BOPTools/Utils.py
functions:
  - def compoundLeaves(shape_or_compound)
    doc: compoundLeaves(shape_or_compound): extracts all non-compound shapes from a nested compound. Note: shape_or_compound may be a non-compound; then, it is the only thing in the returned list.
  - def upgradeToAggregateIfNeeded(list_of_shapes, types=None)
    doc: upgradeToAggregateIfNeeded(list_of_shapes, types = None): upgrades non-aggregate type shapes to aggregate-type shapes if the list has a mix of aggregate and non-aggregate type shapes. Returns the new list. Recursively traverses into compounds. aggregate shape types are Wire, Shell, CompSolid non-aggregate shape types are Vertex, Edge, Face, Solid Compounds are something special: they are recursively traversed to upgrade the contained shapes. Examples: list_of_shapes contains only faces -> nothin
classes:
  class HashableShape(object)
    doc: Decorator for Part.Shape, that can be used as key in dicts. Based on isSame method.
  class HashableShape_Deep(object)
    doc: Similar to HashableShape, except that the things the shape is composed of are compared. Example: >>> wire2 = Part.Wire(wire1.childShapes()) >>> wire2.isSame(wire1) False # <--- the wire2 is a new wire, although made of edges of wire1 >>> HashableShape_Deep(wire2) == HashableShape_Deep(wire1) True # <--- made of same set of elements
  class FrozenClass(object)
    doc: FrozenClass: prevents adding new attributes to class outside of __init__
```

```text
  class PartExtrusionTests(unittest.TestCase)
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def testExtrudeTaperInverted(self)
        doc: With Inverted taper (Part::Extrusion default), the outer grows while the hole shrinks.
      - def testExtrudeTaperSameAsOuter(self)
        doc: Regression test for issue #28709: with SameAsOuter taper (the PartDesign path), both wires must taper in the same direction.
      - def tearDown(self)
  class PartFaceMakerBuildFaceTests(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Regression: a Sketch with MakeInternals=True containing two concentric ellipses and two short line segments joining them on the right side fails to build the internal face. The direct FaceMakerBuildFace call succeeds, but SketchObject::buildInternals also calls Part::WireJoiner::getOpenWires, which currently throws ('failed to close some wire in iteration N') and leaves InternalShape null.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def testBuildFaceDirectMakeFace(self)
        doc: Direct FaceMakerBuildFace on the same wires must produce faces.
      - def testSketchMakeInternalsBuildsFaces(self)
        doc: The full SketchObject MakeInternals path must produce a non-null InternalShape with at least two faces (the annulus regions split by the two connecting line segments).
```

```text
classes:
  class TestDegenerateInput(unittest.TestCase)
    methods:
      - def test_single_open_line(self)
        doc: An open wire cannot form a face.
      - def test_empty_compound(self)
        doc: An empty compound cannot form a face.
  class TestTextGlyphs(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Unified face making for actual font glyph wires.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def test_letter_A(self)
        doc: Letter A has an outer outline and a triangular hole - 1 face.
      - def test_letter_B(self)
        doc: Letter B has an outer outline and two holes - 1 face.
      - def test_letter_O(self)
        doc: Letter O has an outer and inner outline - 1 annular face.
      - def test_letter_D(self)
        doc: Letter D has an outer outline and one hole - 1 face.
      - def test_digit_8(self)
        doc: Digit 8 has an outer outline and two holes - 1 face.
      - def test_multiple_chars(self)
        doc: Multiple characters each produce valid faces.
```

```text
MODULE src/Mod/Part/parttests/TestPartMirror.py
classes:
  class TestPartMirroringRegression(unittest.TestCase)
    doc: Regression test for GitHub issue #27365. Part::Mirroring with Draft Clone produces incorrect results after PR #26963. The mirror position shifts on recompute when the source has non-identity Placement.
    methods:
      - def setUp(self)
      - def tearDown(self)
      - def testMirroringWithDraftCloneStability(self)
        doc: Test that Part::Mirroring position is stable across recomputes. This test reproduces issue #27365: mirroring a Draft Clone that has placement, and causes the mirror position to be incorrect and/or shift on recompute. This test FAILS on current main (after PR #26963) and should pass after a fix is applied.
      - def testMirroringWithAppLinkPlacement(self)
        doc: Test that Part::Mirroring respects App::Link placement. App::Link provides its Placement via extension rather than GeoFeature inheritance, so mirror code must not rely on isDerivedFrom(GeoFeature).
      - def testMirroringWithAppLinkRotation(self)
        doc: Test that Part::Mirroring respects App::Link rotation + translation.
```

```text
        doc: Test that mirror() produces identical results regardless of how the shape is positioned - via direct coordinates or via Placement. Regression test for GitHub issue #20834.
```
